## Lay of the Land: Board Banner Feature Design Reference

**1. Welcome Article Seed Flow**

The welcome article seed is triggered in `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/cmd/sshbbs/main.go:58`, calling:
```go
seed.Articles(context.Background(), st, auth.ReservedUsernameAdmin)
```

The "first boot vs already seeded" decision is made by **article existence per board** in `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/seed/articles.go:86–107`. It calls `st.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, board.ID, 1)` and only seeds if the result is empty (`len(existing) == 0`). Once a board has at least one article, all seed files targeting it are skipped. This is idempotent—an admin who edits the welcome article won't see their changes overwritten on restart.

**2. `go:embed` Usage**

The welcome markdown is embedded in `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/seed/articles.go:35`:
```go
//go:embed articles/*.md
var articlesFS embed.FS
```

The directory structure is `internal/seed/articles/welcome.md`. Currently only one file (`welcome.md`), but the glob `articles/*.md` allows adding more `.md` files to the same directory—they'll be auto-picked at build. Each `.md` file uses frontmatter with `board:` and `title:` fields to declare the target board.

**3. `boards` Table Schema**

Current schema from `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/migrations/0001_init.sql:23–31`:
```sql
CREATE TABLE boards (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    name            TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE COLLATE NOCASE,
    title           TEXT NOT NULL,
    description     TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    bm              TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    attr            INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);
```

Highest existing migration: **0005** (`0005_articles_updated_at.sql`). Your banner migration would be **0006**.

**4. Boards Seed**

Default boards are seeded in *two* places (belt-and-braces):
- **Migration 0002** (`/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/migrations/0002_seed_boards.sql`): SQL `INSERT OR IGNORE` at schema-apply time.
- **Runtime** (`/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/boards.go:70–80`, `BoardRepo.SeedDefaults()`): Called from `cmd/sshbbs/main.go:50`, runs every startup with `INSERT OR IGNORE` (no-op if rows exist).

Boards seeded: `Welcome`, `Test`, `ChitChat` with titles in Traditional Chinese. This is separate from the article seed flow.

**5. BoardRepo Methods**

Defined in `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/boards.go:20`. Current public methods:
- `List(ctx)` — all boards
- `GetByID(ctx, id)` — fetch by ID
- `GetByName(ctx, name)` — fetch by name
- `SeedDefaults(ctx)` — idempotent insert

The `Board` struct (lines 10–18) has no banner field yet. 25	func main() {
26		// Subcommand dispatch: anything that isn't a flag is treated as a
27		// subcommand. Today only `import` exists, but the shape leaves room
28		// for future ops tools (e.g. `export`, `dump`).
29		if len(os.Args) >= 2 && !strings.HasPrefix(os.Args[1], "-") {
30			switch os.Args[1] {
31			case "import":
32				os.Exit(runImport(os.Args[2:]))
33			default:
34				log.Fatalf("unknown subcommand: %s", os.Args[1])
35			}
36		}
37	
38		addr := flag.String("addr", ":2222", "SSH listen address")
39		dbPath := flag.String("db", "data/bbs.db", "SQLite database path")
40		hostkey := flag.String("hostkey", ".ssh/host_ed25519", "SSH host key path")
41		adminPassword := flag.String("admin-password", "",
42			"override admin password on first seed; empty uses the baked default 'admin' with must_change_password=1")
43		flag.Parse()
44	
45		st, err := store.Open(*dbPath)
46		if err != nil {
47			log.Fatalf("store.Open: %v", err)
48		}
49		defer st.Close()
50		if err := st.Boards().SeedDefaults(context.Background()); err != nil {
51			log.Fatalf("seed boards: %v", err)
52		}
53		if err := auth.SeedSystemAccounts(context.Background(), st, auth.SeedOpts{
54			AdminPassword: *adminPassword,
55		}); err != nil {
56			log.Fatalf("seed system accounts: %v", err)
57		}
58		if err := seed.Articles(context.Background(), st, auth.ReservedUsernameAdmin); err != nil {
59			log.Fatalf("seed articles: %v", err)
60		}
61		log.Printf("storage ready at %s", *dbPath)
62	
63		broker := chat.NewBroker()
64	
65		srv, err := server.New(server.Config{Addr: *addr, HostKey: *hostkey}, st, broker)
66		if err != nil {
67			log.Fatalf("server.New: %v", err)
68		}
69	
70		ctx, stop := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
71		defer stop()
72	
73		errCh := make(chan error, 1)
74		go func() {
75			log.Printf("sshbbs %s listening on %s", version.Version, *addr)
76			errCh <- srv.ListenAndServe()
77		}()
78	
79		select {
80		case err := <-errCh:
81			if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, ssh.ErrServerClosed) {
82				log.Fatalf("listen: %v", err)
83			}
84		case <-ctx.Done():
85			log.Println("shutdown signal received")
86			shutdown(srv, broker)
87		}
88	}
89	
90	// shutdown closes the listener (refuses new connections), then asks every
91	// live bubbletea program to quit, then closes the SSH server hard. The DB
92	// closes on the deferred st.Close() in main.
93	func shutdown(srv *ssh.Server, broker *chat.Broker) {
94		const drainTimeout = 3 * time.Second
95	
96		// 1. Stop accepting new connections.
97		listenerCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), drainTimeout)
98		defer cancel()
99		if err := srv.Shutdown(listenerCtx); err != nil {
100			log.Printf("server.Shutdown: %v", err)
101		}
102	
103		// 2. Tell every active session to quit.
104		sessions := broker.SessionsSnapshot()
105		if len(sessions) == 0 {
106			return
107		}
108		log.Printf("draining %d active session(s)", len(sessions))
109		for _, s := range sessions {
110			s.Program.Send(tea.Quit())
111		}
112	
113		// 3. Give them a moment to flush their final view, then force-close.
114		timer := time.NewTimer(drainTimeout)
115		defer timer.Stop()
116		for {
117			if len(broker.SessionsSnapshot()) == 0 {
118				log.Println("all sessions drained")
119				return
120			}
121			select {
122			case <-timer.C:
123				log.Printf("drain timeout; %d session(s) still attached", len(broker.SessionsSnapshot()))
124				return
125			case <-time.After(100 * time.Millisecond):
126			}
127		}
128	}
129

```text
1	// Package seed loads default content (currently: welcome / intro articles)
2	// from embedded markdown files into the database on first startup.
3	//
4	// The contract is intentionally weak: a board with any existing article is
5	// skipped entirely. That way an admin who edits the seeded welcome article
6	// does not see their changes overwritten on the next restart.
7	//
8	// To add more seed articles, drop another *.md file under internal/seed/articles/
9	// with frontmatter naming the target board:
10	//
11	//	---
12	//	title: ...
13	//	board: Welcome
14	//	---
15	//
16	//	body...
17	//
18	// `go:embed` will pick it up at the next build.
19	package seed
20	
21	import (
22		"context"
23		"embed"
24		"errors"
25		"fmt"
26		"io/fs"
27		"log"
28		"sort"
29		"strings"
30	
31		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/markdown"
32		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
33	)
34	
35	//go:embed articles/*.md
36	var articlesFS embed.FS
37	
38	// Articles parses every embedded *.md file and inserts it into the board
39	// named by its frontmatter, attributing all of them to adminUserID. A
40	// board that already has at least one article is left alone, regardless
41	// of how many seed files target it.
42	//
43	// Failure to parse or insert any single file logs and continues — a bad
44	// seed file should not block startup. Missing boards are also skipped
45	// with a log line.
46	func Articles(ctx context.Context, st *store.Store, adminUserID string) error {
47		admin, err := st.Users().GetByUserID(ctx, adminUserID)
48		if err != nil {
49			return fmt.Errorf("seed.Articles: lookup admin %q: %w", adminUserID, err)
50		}
51	
52		entries, err := fs.ReadDir(articlesFS, "articles")
53		if err != nil {
54			return fmt.Errorf("seed.Articles: read embedded fs: %w", err)
55		}
56		// Stable iteration order so logs are deterministic.
57		sort.Slice(entries, func(i, j int) bool { return entries[i].Name() < entries[j].Name() })
58	
59		// Cache "has-articles?" per board so several seed files targeting the
60		// same board only hit the DB once.
61		boardEmpty := map[int64]bool{}
62	
63		for _, e := range entries {
64			if e.IsDir() || !strings.HasSuffix(e.Name(), ".md") {
65				continue
66			}
67			body, err := articlesFS.ReadFile("articles/" + e.Name())
68			if err != nil {
69				log.Printf("seed.Articles: read %s: %v", e.Name(), err)
70				continue
71			}
72			parsed, err := markdown.Parse(string(body))
73			if err != nil {
74				log.Printf("seed.Articles: parse %s: %v", e.Name(), err)
75				continue
76			}
77			if parsed.BoardName == "" {
78				log.Printf("seed.Articles: %s has no `board:` frontmatter, skipping", e.Name())
79				continue
80			}
81			if parsed.Title == "" {
82				log.Printf("seed.Articles: %s has no `title:` frontmatter, skipping", e.Name())
83				continue
84			}
85	
86			board, err := st.Boards().GetByName(ctx, parsed.BoardName)
87			if err != nil {
88				if errors.Is(err, store.ErrBoardNotFound) {
89					log.Printf("seed.Articles: %s targets board %q which doesn't exist; skipping",
90						e.Name(), parsed.BoardName)
91					continue
92				}
93				return fmt.Errorf("seed.Articles: lookup board %q: %w", parsed.BoardName, err)
94			}
95	
96			empty, cached := boardEmpty[board.ID]
97			if !cached {
98				existing, err := st.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, board.ID, 1)
99				if err != nil {
100					return fmt.Errorf("seed.Articles: list %q: %w", parsed.BoardName, err)
101				}
102				empty = len(existing) == 0
103				boardEmpty[board.ID] = empty
104			}
105			if !empty {
106				continue
107			}
108	
109			if _, err := st.Articles().Create(ctx, board.ID, admin.ID, admin.UserID, parsed.Title, parsed.Body); err != nil {
110				log.Printf("seed.Articles: insert %s: %v", e.Name(), err)
111				continue
112			}
113			// Mark this board as no-longer-empty so subsequent seed files for
114			// the same board are skipped.
115			boardEmpty[board.ID] = false
116			log.Printf("seed.Articles: seeded %s into board %q", e.Name(), parsed.BoardName)
117		}
118		return nil
119	}
120
```

1	-- Initial schema for the SSH BBS.
2	-- All tables use auto-increment INTEGER PRIMARY KEY plus a unique business
3	-- key where appropriate. We deliberately do NOT mimic pttbbs's recno-based
4	-- addressing (a holdover from mmap'd binary .DIR files) — a relational DB
5	-- removes the recno-tombstone fragility documented in pttbbs.
6	
7	PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;
8	
9	CREATE TABLE users (
10	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
11	    user_id         TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE COLLATE NOCASE, -- userec_t.userid
12	    password_hash   TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- bcrypt; replaces userec_t.passwd (DES)
13	    nickname        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- userec_t.nickname
14	    realname        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- userec_t.realname
15	    email           TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- userec_t.email
16	    num_logins      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- userec_t.numlogindays (loose)
17	    num_posts       INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- userec_t.numposts
18	    last_login_at   DATETIME,                            -- userec_t.lastlogin
19	    last_host       TEXT,                                -- userec_t.lasthost
20	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
21	);
22	
23	CREATE TABLE boards (
24	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
25	    name            TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE COLLATE NOCASE, -- boardheader_t.brdname

1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"time"
8	)
9	
10	type Board struct {
11		ID          int64
12		Name        string
13		Title       string
14		Description string
15		BM          string
16		Attr        int64
17		CreatedAt   time.Time
18	}
19	
20	type BoardRepo struct{ s *Store }
21	
22	var ErrBoardNotFound = errors.New("board not found")
23	
24	const boardColumns = `id, name, title, description, bm, attr, created_at`
25	
26	func scanBoard(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Board, error) {
27		b := &Board{}
28		err := row.Scan(&b.ID, &b.Name, &b.Title, &b.Description, &b.BM, &b.Attr, &b.CreatedAt)
29		return b, err
30	}
31	
32	func (r *BoardRepo) List(ctx context.Context) ([]*Board, error) {
33		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
34			`SELECT `+boardColumns+` FROM boards ORDER BY name COLLATE NOCASE`)
35		if err != nil {
36			return nil, err
37		}
38		defer rows.Close()
39		var out []*Board
40		for rows.Next() {
41			b, err := scanBoard(rows)
42			if err != nil {
43				return nil, err
44			}
45			out = append(out, b)
46		}
47		return out, rows.Err()
48	}
49	
50	func (r *BoardRepo) GetByID(ctx context.Context, id int64) (*Board, error) {
51		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+boardColumns+` FROM boards WHERE id = ?`, id)
52		b, err := scanBoard(row)
53		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
54			return nil, ErrBoardNotFound
55		}
56		return b, err
57	}
58	
59	func (r *BoardRepo) GetByName(ctx context.Context, name string) (*Board, error) {
60		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+boardColumns+` FROM boards WHERE name = ? COLLATE NOCASE`, name)
61		b, err := scanBoard(row)
62		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
63			return nil, ErrBoardNotFound
64		}
65		return b, err
66	}
67	
68	// SeedDefaults is a belt-and-braces complement to migration 0002. It runs
69	// every startup; INSERT OR IGNORE makes it a no-op once the rows exist.
70	func (r *BoardRepo) SeedDefaults(ctx context.Context) error {
71		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
72		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
73		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx, `
74			INSERT OR IGNORE INTO boards (name, title, description) VALUES
75				('Welcome',  '歡迎來到 SSH-BBS', 'System welcome and announcements'),
76				('Test',     '測試板',           'Try things here. Posts may be cleared periodically.'),
77				('ChitChat', '閒聊',             'General chat. Be kind.')
78		`)
79		return err
80	}

```text
1	---
2	title: 歡迎來到 SSH-BBS
3	board: Welcome
4	author: admin
5	---
6	
7	哈囉，歡迎來到這個簡化版的 PTT 風格 BBS。整個站台就是一個 SSH server：
8	你看到的所有東西都跑在 wish + bubbletea 上面，後端用 SQLite 存資料。
9	
10	# 這裡能做什麼
11	
12	- **看板**：預設有 `Welcome`（這裡）、`Test`、`ChitChat`。
13	- **發文 / 讀文**：UTF-8 全程支援，CJK 寬度自動處理。
14	- **推文**：在文章畫面按 `+` 推、`-` 噓、`=` 留 →。其他線上看同篇的人會即時看到。
15	- **水球**：私訊一行話。`Ctrl+U` 開水球收件匣，離線收到的也會在你重連時補播。
16	- **站內信 (mail)**：水球留不下，重要的話寫信。`Ctrl+U` 進收件匣後切到郵件分頁。
17	
18	# 鍵盤總覽
19	
20	```
21	↑/↓ 或 j/k     上下移動
22	←/→ 或 h/l     上下層導覽（表單例外，h/l 留給文字編輯）
23	Enter / Space  進入 / 翻頁
24	Esc / Backspace 返回
25	g / G          捲到頂 / 底
26	[ / ]          上一篇 / 下一篇
27	p              在看板裡發新文
28	+ / - / =      推 / 噓 / →
29	y              匯出此篇為 markdown
30	E              編輯此篇（作者本人或 mod 以上）
31	D              刪除此篇 / 推文（作者本人或 mod 以上）
32	Ctrl+U         水球 / 站內信
33	Ctrl+C         斷線離開
34	```
35	
36	# 權限
37	
38	- **guest**：唯讀，只看不寫。SSH 用 `guest` 帳號連進來不需密碼。
39	- **user**：一般註冊帳號。`ssh new@host -p 2222` 註冊，密碼欄位會被忽略，用 TUI 內表單填。
40	- **mod**：可以刪 / 編輯任何人的文與推文。
41	- **admin**：站長，可以調整他人權限。
42	
43	# 文章是 markdown
44	
45	每篇文章都能用 markdown 表達。在文章畫面按 `y` 會跳到匯出檢視，
46	你可以選 (1) 純內文 (2) 含留言 (3) 寫到伺服器端檔案 (`data/exports/<你的帳號>/`)，
47	或按 `c` 推到終端機剪貼簿（OSC 52，需終端機支援）。
48	
49	寫文也可以走 markdown 路：在發文畫面按 `Ctrl+I`，貼上含 frontmatter 的 markdown，
50	標題會自動從 `title:` 帶進來。
51	
52	# 這篇文是怎麼長出來的
53	
54	這篇 welcome 不是寫死在程式碼或 DB migration 裡。它是 `internal/seed/articles/welcome.md`，
55	用 `go:embed` 包進 binary，server 第一次發現 `Welcome` 看板是空的就把它寫進 DB。
56	之後 admin 改過一次，下次重啟也不會被覆蓋（種子的冪等規則：看板有任何文就跳過）。
57	
58	要新增種子文章，丟一個新的 `*.md` 進那個資料夾，frontmatter 寫 `board: 看板名` 就好。
59	
60	# 想了解更多

62	- 專案 README：repo 根目錄 `README.md`
63	- pttbbs 對照筆記：`docs/ptt_trace_code/*`
64	- 測試策略：`docs/testing.md`
65	- 待辦清單：`TODO.md`（含 P1～P3 + 工作量估計）

-- Track edits to articles. NULL = never edited; the original CreatedAt is the
-- only authoritative timestamp. The article-edit screen and ArticleRepo.Update
-- set this to CURRENT_TIMESTAMP on every successful update.
--
-- Ordering: ListByBoard intentionally still sorts by created_at, so an edited
-- article does NOT bubble up to the top — bookmark/next-prev semantics stay
-- intact.

ALTER TABLE articles ADD COLUMN updated_at DATETIME;
```

## Board Banner Feature — Architecture Tour

### 1. **Board View Model & Insertion Point** (`screen_board_view.go`)

**Model struct** (lines 13–21):

    deps     Deps

}
```

**View() function** (lines 88–161) builds output in this sequence:
1. **Line 90–95**: Blank line, then **header block** using `StyleHeader`:
   - `" 看板 {board.Name} · {board.Title} "`
   - This is the precedent for "top bar" styling—match this with `.Render()` for consistency
2. **Line 96**: Blank line (breathing room)

   #  Date  Sc.  Author  Title

6. **Line 130–152**: Article rows (with cursor highlight at line 147–151)

**Insertion point for banner**: Between lines 96 and 98 — right after the board header and before any content. You'd insert the banner block there, consuming some of the height budget (calculate remaining lines for articles).

### 2. **Width Helpers** (`runewidth.go`)

**Exported functions** (signatures at lines 13–54):

| Function | Signature | ANSI-Aware? |
|----------|-----------|------------|
| `PadRight` | `PadRight(s string, w int) string` | ❌ NO |
| `PadLeft` | `PadLeft(s string, w int) string` | ❌ NO |
| `Truncate` | `Truncate(s string, w int) string` | ❌ NO |
| `Width` | `Width(s string) int` | ❌ NO |

**Critical finding**: All four helpers delegate to `github.com/mattn/go-runewidth` (line 6 import). `runewidth.StringWidth()` and `runewidth.Truncate()` **do NOT handle ANSI escape sequences** — they count CSI bytes as display width.

**Consequence for banners**: If you render an ANSI-colored banner (e.g., `\x1b[31mRED TEXT\x1b[0m`), these helpers will overcount width. **You must strip ANSI before measuring or padding.** Recommend adding a helper like `stripANSI(s string) string` using a regex `\x1b\[[^m]*m` before calling `Width()` / `PadRight()` / `Truncate()`.

---

### 3. **Window-Resize Handling**

**Root.Update()** (`root.go`, lines 61–102):
- Receives `tea.WindowSizeMsg` (line 63)
- Sets `m.width` and `m.height` (line 64–65)
- **Forwards to active sub-model** (line 67): `m.sub.Update(msg)`

**Board view receives resize** (`screen_board_view.go`, line 37–39):
```go
case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
    m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
    return m, nil
```

**Fields to use for banner truncation**:
- `m.width` — total terminal width
- `m.height` — total terminal height
- **Article list calculation** (line 120): `titleW := max(20, m.width-2-idxW-1-dateW-1-scoreW-1-authorW-2)` — shows how width budget is carved up

### 4. **Glamour Markdown Rendering** (`screen_article_view.go`)

**Import** (line 10):
```go
"github.com/charmbracelet/glamour"
```

**Function `renderBody()`** (lines 51–81):
```go
func (m *articleViewModel) renderBody() {
    width := m.width - 4
    if width < 40 {
        width = glamourFallbackWidth // = 80
    }
    r, err := glamour.NewTermRenderer(
        glamour.WithStandardStyle("dark"),
        glamour.WithWordWrap(width),
    )
    // ...
    out, err := r.Render(m.article.Body)  // ← glamour runs here
    m.rendered = strings.Trim(out, "\n")
    m.renderedWidth = width
}
```

Called on:
- Init (line 99)
- `tea.WindowSizeMsg` if width changes (lines 149–156)
- `ArticleUpdatedMsg` (line 178)

**For banners**: Glamour would apply markdown styling (bold, italics, code blocks, lists, etc.) with syntax highlighting. If you want a markdown banner, run it through `glamour.NewTermRenderer(glamour.WithStandardStyle("dark"), glamour.WithWordWrap(bannerWidth)).Render(bannerText)`. Otherwise, raw ANSI passthrough is simpler.

---

**NavigateMsg** (lines 26–33) carries context:

    To           Screen

}
```

**For banners**, you have options:
- **Option A** (simple): Embed banner in `boardViewModel.View()` directly — no new screen needed.
- **Option B** (splash): Add `ScreenBoardSplash` to the enum, create `screen_board_splash.go`, and have it navigate to `ScreenBoardView` on Enter. (See precedent below.)

---

### 6. **Splash/Welcome Precedent**

**No existing splash in the codebase** — every screen goes straight to content. **Root initialization** (`root.go`, lines 44–54) prioritizes:
1. Register (if `IsRegister = true`)
2. Password change (if `MustChangePassword = true`)
3. Main menu (default)

**All three are direct screens**, not splash overlays. The **main menu** (`screen_main_menu.go`, lines 82–125) is the closest precedent for a "welcome" feel:
- Shows user nickname and login stats (lines 84–101)
- Renders a centered header (line 104): `StyleHeader.Render(" 主選單 Main Menu ")`
- Lists 4–5 menu items with hints
- Single `q` or Esc to quit

**If you want a splash** (e.g., "Welcome! Press Enter to view the board banner"), you'd create:
1. Add `ScreenBoardSplash` to `messages.go`
2. Add case in `root.go` navigate() to instantiate it
3. Create `screen_board_splash.go` with a simple model that displays the banner text + "Press Enter to continue" and navigates back to `ScreenBoardView` on Enter

1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"time"
8	)
9	
10	type Board struct {
11		ID          int64
12		Name        string
13		Title       string
14		Description string
15		BM          string
16		Attr        int64
17		CreatedAt   time.Time
18	}
19	
20	type BoardRepo struct{ s *Store }
21	
22	var ErrBoardNotFound = errors.New("board not found")
23	
24	const boardColumns = `id, name, title, description, bm, attr, created_at`
25	
26	func scanBoard(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Board, error) {
27		b := &Board{}
28		err := row.Scan(&b.ID, &b.Name, &b.Title, &b.Description, &b.BM, &b.Attr, &b.CreatedAt)
29		return b, err
30	}
31	
32	func (r *BoardRepo) List(ctx context.Context) ([]*Board, error) {
33		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
34			`SELECT `+boardColumns+` FROM boards ORDER BY name COLLATE NOCASE`)
35		if err != nil {
36			return nil, err
37		}
38		defer rows.Close()
39		var out []*Board
40		for rows.Next() {
41			b, err := scanBoard(rows)
42			if err != nil {
43				return nil, err
44			}
45			out = append(out, b)
46		}
47		return out, rows.Err()
48	}
49	
50	func (r *BoardRepo) GetByID(ctx context.Context, id int64) (*Board, error) {
51		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+boardColumns+` FROM boards WHERE id = ?`, id)
52		b, err := scanBoard(row)
53		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
54			return nil, ErrBoardNotFound
55		}
56		return b, err
57	}
58	
59	func (r *BoardRepo) GetByName(ctx context.Context, name string) (*Board, error) {
60		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+boardColumns+` FROM boards WHERE name = ? COLLATE NOCASE`, name)
61		b, err := scanBoard(row)
62		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
63			return nil, ErrBoardNotFound
64		}
65		return b, err
66	}
67	
68	// SeedDefaults is a belt-and-braces complement to migration 0002. It runs
69	// every startup; INSERT OR IGNORE makes it a no-op once the rows exist.
70	func (r *BoardRepo) SeedDefaults(ctx context.Context) error {
71		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
72		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
73		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx, `
74			INSERT OR IGNORE INTO boards (name, title, description) VALUES
75				('Welcome',  '歡迎來到 SSH-BBS', 'System welcome and announcements'),
76				('Test',     '測試板',           'Try things here. Posts may be cleared periodically.'),
77				('ChitChat', '閒聊',             'General chat. Be kind.')
78		`)
79		return err
80	}
81

23	func newBoardViewModel(deps Deps, boardID int64) boardViewModel {
24		ctx := context.Background()
25		board, err := deps.Store.Boards().GetByID(ctx, boardID)
26		if err != nil {
27			return boardViewModel{deps: deps, loadErr: err}
28		}
29		articles, err := deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, boardID, 100)
30		return boardViewModel{deps: deps, board: board, articles: articles, loadErr: err}
31	}

33	func (m boardViewModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
34	
35	func (m boardViewModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
36		switch msg := msg.(type) {
37		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
38			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
39			return m, nil
40		case ArticleAddedMsg:
41			if m.board != nil && msg.BoardID == m.board.ID {
42				ctx := context.Background()
43				if arts, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, m.board.ID, 100); err == nil {
44					m.articles = arts
45				}
46			}
47			return m, nil
48		case tea.KeyMsg:
49			switch msg.String() {
50			case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
51				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardList} }
52			case "Q":
53				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
54			case "up", "k", "[":
55				if m.cursor > 0 {
56					m.cursor--
57				}
58			case "down", "j", "]":
59				if m.cursor < len(m.articles)-1 {
60					m.cursor++
61				}
62			case "home", "g":
63				m.cursor = 0
64			case "end", "G":
65				if len(m.articles) > 0 {
66					m.cursor = len(m.articles) - 1
67				}
68			case "enter", " ", "right", "l":
69				if len(m.articles) == 0 {
70					return m, nil
71				}
72				a := m.articles[m.cursor]
73				return m, func() tea.Msg {
74					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ArticleID: a.ID, BoardID: a.BoardID}
75				}
76			case "p":
77				if m.board == nil {
78					return m, nil
79				}
80				return m, func() tea.Msg {
81					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenPostCompose, BoardID: m.board.ID}
82				}
83			}
84		}
85		return m, nil
86	}
87	
88	func (m boardViewModel) View() string {
89		var b strings.Builder
90		b.WriteString("\n")
91		if m.board != nil {
92			b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" 看板 %s · %s ", m.board.Name, m.board.Title)))
93		} else {
94			b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 看板 "))
95		}
96		b.WriteString("\n\n")
97	
98		if m.loadErr != nil {
99			b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n")
100			return b.String()
101		}
102		if len(m.articles) == 0 {
103			hint := "(no articles yet — press p to write one)"
104			help := "p post · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
105			if m.isGuest() {
106				hint = "(no articles yet)"
107				help = "Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
108			}
109			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(hint) + "\n")
110			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render(help))
111			return b.String()
112		}
113	
114		const (
115			idxW    = 4
116			dateW   = 11
117			scoreW  = 4
118			authorW = 14
119		)
120		titleW := max(20, m.width-2-idxW-1-dateW-1-scoreW-1-authorW-2)
121		header := fmt.Sprintf(" %s  %s  %s  %s  %s",
122			PadRight("#", idxW),
123			PadRight("Date", dateW),
124			PadRight("Sc.", scoreW),
125			PadRight("Author", authorW),
126			PadRight("Title", titleW),
127		)
128		b.WriteString(StyleDim.Render(header) + "\n")
129	
130		for i, a := range m.articles {
131			score := ""
132			switch {
133			case a.RecommendScore >= 100:
134				score = "爆"
135			case a.RecommendScore > 0:
136				score = fmt.Sprintf("%d", a.RecommendScore)
137			case a.RecommendScore < 0:
138				score = "X" + fmt.Sprintf("%d", -a.RecommendScore)
139			}
140			row := fmt.Sprintf(" %s  %s  %s  %s  %s",
141				PadRight(fmt.Sprintf("%d", i+1), idxW),
142				PadRight(a.CreatedAt.Format("01/02 15:04"), dateW),
143				PadRight(score, scoreW),
144				PadRight(a.AuthorUserID, authorW),
145				Truncate(a.Title, titleW),
146			)
147			if i == m.cursor {
148				b.WriteString(StyleHighlight.Render("▸"+row[1:]) + "\n")
149			} else {
150				b.WriteString(" " + row + "\n")
151			}
152		}
153	
154		help := "↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l open · p post · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
155		if m.isGuest() {
156			help = "↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l open · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
157		}
158		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render(help))
159		b.WriteString("\n")
160		return b.String()
161	}
162	
163	func (m boardViewModel) isGuest() bool {
164		return m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role == store.RoleGuest
165	}

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"strings"
5	
6		"github.com/mattn/go-runewidth"
7	)
8	
9	// PadRight pads s with spaces on the right so its display width equals w.
10	// If s is wider than w, it's truncated. Critical for table-like rendering
11	// with mixed ASCII + CJK strings — lipgloss's automatic padding miscounts
12	// double-width glyphs in some terminals.
13	func PadRight(s string, w int) string {
14		if w <= 0 {
15			return ""
16		}
17		cw := runewidth.StringWidth(s)
18		if cw == w {
19			return s
20		}
21		if cw > w {
22			return runewidth.Truncate(s, w, "")
23		}
24		return s + strings.Repeat(" ", w-cw)
25	}
26	
27	// PadLeft is PadRight's mirror, padding before the string.
28	func PadLeft(s string, w int) string {
29		if w <= 0 {
30			return ""
31		}
32		cw := runewidth.StringWidth(s)
33		if cw == w {
34			return s
35		}
36		if cw > w {
37			return runewidth.Truncate(s, w, "")
38		}
39		return strings.Repeat(" ", w-cw) + s
40	}
41	
42	// Truncate cuts s to display-width w, appending "…" if truncation occurred.
43	func Truncate(s string, w int) string {
44		if runewidth.StringWidth(s) <= w {
45			return s
46		}
47		if w <= 1 {
48			return runewidth.Truncate(s, w, "")
49		}
50		return runewidth.Truncate(s, w, "…")
51	}
52	
53	// Width returns the display width of s.
54	func Width(s string) int { return runewidth.StringWidth(s) }
55

1	package tui
2	
3	// Screen identifies which sub-model the Root should be showing.
4	type Screen int
5	
6	const (
7		ScreenMainMenu Screen = iota
8		ScreenBoardList
9		ScreenBoardView
10		ScreenArticleView
11		ScreenPostCompose
12		ScreenWBInbox
13		ScreenWBCompose
14		ScreenOnline
15		ScreenMailInbox
16		ScreenMailThread
17		ScreenMailCompose
18		ScreenPasswordChange
19		ScreenAdminUsers
20		ScreenArticleEdit
21		ScreenArticleExport
22	)
23	
24	// NavigateMsg is emitted by sub-screens to ask the Root to swap screens.
25	// Optional fields carry context the destination screen needs.
26	type NavigateMsg struct {
27		To           Screen
28		BoardID      int64
29		ArticleID    int64
30		Recipient    string // for ScreenWBCompose / ScreenMailCompose: prefilled recipient userid
31		MailID       int64  // for ScreenMailCompose: parent mail id when replying
32		MailThreadID int64  // for ScreenMailThread: which thread to open
33	}
34	
35	// ErrorMsg surfaces an error as a transient toast.
36	type ErrorMsg struct{ Err error }
37	
38	// WBIncomingMsg is delivered by the chat broker (or replayed from DB on Init)
39	// to notify the recipient that a new water balloon arrived.
40	type WBIncomingMsg struct {
41		ID         int64
42		FromUserID string
43		Body       string
44	}
45	
46	// PushAddedMsg is broadcast by the chat broker when a push is added to an
47	// article. The article-view model checks ArticleID to decide whether to
48	// re-render.
49	type PushAddedMsg struct {
50		ArticleID  int64
51		UserUserID string
52		Kind       string
53		Body       string
54	}
55	
56	// MailIncomingMsg notifies the recipient that a new mail has arrived.
57	// Unlike WBIncomingMsg it does NOT auto-toast — mail is asynchronous; the
58	// open mail inbox can choose to refresh, and the main menu reads the unread
59	// counter from DB.
60	type MailIncomingMsg struct {
61		ID         int64
62		ThreadID   int64
63		FromUserID string
64		Subject    string
65	}
66	
67	// ArticleAddedMsg is broadcast by the chat broker when a new article is
68	// posted. Board-view models filter by BoardID and re-fetch the article
69	// list from DB so timestamps and ordering reflect canonical state.
70	type ArticleAddedMsg struct {
71		BoardID      int64
72		ArticleID    int64
73		AuthorUserID string
74		Title        string
75	}
76	
77	// ArticleUpdatedMsg is broadcast by the chat broker when an article's
78	// title or body is edited. Article-view models filter by ArticleID and
79	// re-fetch from DB so they don't render stale text.
80	type ArticleUpdatedMsg struct {
81		ArticleID int64
82	}

1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"strings"
7	
8		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput"
9		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
10		"github.com/charmbracelet/glamour"
11	
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
13	)
14	
15	// glamourFallbackWidth is the wrap width used before we get a
16	// WindowSizeMsg. 80 is the historical terminal default; the renderer
17	// re-runs with the real width as soon as the first resize lands.
18	const glamourFallbackWidth = 80
19	
20	type articleViewModel struct {
21		deps    Deps
22		article *store.Article
23		pushes  []*store.Push
24		width   int
25		height  int
26		scroll  int
27		loadErr error
28	
29		// rendered is the article body after running through glamour. We cache
30		// because (a) glamour's first call loads chroma's syntax highlighter
31		// (~tens of ms) and (b) View() is called on every keystroke. Re-render
32		// only when the article changes or the window width changes.
33		rendered      string
34		renderedWidth int
35	
36		// push input state
37		pushing  bool
38		pushKind store.PushKind
39		pushIn   textinput.Model
40		err      string
41	
42		// delete state. pendingDelete: showing the y/N confirm overlay.
43		// pushCursor: -1 = no push selected (D targets article); 0..len-1 = D targets that push.
44		pendingDelete bool
45		pushCursor    int
46	}
47	
48	// renderBody runs the article body through glamour with word-wrap set
49	// to the current viewport. Falls back to the raw body on any glamour
50	// error so a corrupt-markdown article is still readable.
51	func (m *articleViewModel) renderBody() {
52		if m.article == nil {
53			m.rendered = ""
54			m.renderedWidth = 0
55			return
56		}
57		width := m.width - 4
58		if width < 40 {
59			width = glamourFallbackWidth
60		}
61		r, err := glamour.NewTermRenderer(
62			glamour.WithStandardStyle("dark"),
63			glamour.WithWordWrap(width),
64		)
65		if err != nil {
66			m.rendered = m.article.Body
67			m.renderedWidth = width
68			return
69		}
70		out, err := r.Render(m.article.Body)
71		if err != nil {
72			m.rendered = m.article.Body
73			m.renderedWidth = width
74			return
75		}
76		// Trim leading/trailing blank lines glamour likes to add for
77		// breathing room — they push the content off-screen on small
78		// viewports. Internal blank lines stay.
79		m.rendered = strings.Trim(out, "\n")
80		m.renderedWidth = width
81	}
82	
83	func newArticleViewModel(deps Deps, articleID int64) articleViewModel {
84		ctx := context.Background()
85		a, err := deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, articleID)
86		if err != nil {
87			return articleViewModel{deps: deps, loadErr: err}
88		}
89		pushes, err := deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, articleID)
90	
91		ti := textinput.New()
92		ti.Placeholder = "comment…"
93		ti.CharLimit = 80
94		ti.Width = 60
95	
96		m := articleViewModel{deps: deps, article: a, pushes: pushes, loadErr: err, pushIn: ti, pushCursor: -1}
97		// Render with the fallback width so the body is readable even before
98		// the first WindowSizeMsg arrives — Update re-renders on resize.
99		m.renderBody()
100		return m
101	}
102	
103	// canDeleteArticle returns true if the current user may delete the loaded
104	// article (author, or mod-or-above). Used both as a permission gate at
105	// the key handler and to decide whether to render the help-line hint.
106	func (m articleViewModel) canDeleteArticle() bool {
107		if m.deps.User == nil || m.article == nil {
108			return false
109		}
110		if m.deps.User.ID == m.article.AuthorID {
111			return true
112		}
113		return m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
114	}
115	
116	// canDeletePush mirrors canDeleteArticle for the push at index idx.
117	func (m articleViewModel) canDeletePush(idx int) bool {
118		if m.deps.User == nil || idx < 0 || idx >= len(m.pushes) {
119			return false
120		}
121		if m.deps.User.ID == m.pushes[idx].UserID {
122			return true
123		}
124		return m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
125	}
126	
127	// canPush returns true if the current user is allowed to add new pushes.
128	// Guests can read but not write.
129	func (m articleViewModel) canPush() bool {
130		return m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role != store.RoleGuest
131	}
132	
133	// canEditArticle mirrors canDeleteArticle: the author may edit their own
134	// post, mods and admins may edit anyone's.
135	func (m articleViewModel) canEditArticle() bool {
136		if m.deps.User == nil || m.article == nil {
137			return false
138		}
139		if m.deps.User.ID == m.article.AuthorID {
140			return true
141		}
142		return m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
143	}
144	
145	func (m articleViewModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
146	
147	func (m articleViewModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
148		switch msg := msg.(type) {
149		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
150			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
151			// Only re-render when the width target actually changes — height
152			// changes don't affect glamour wrap.
153			newWidth := max(msg.Width-4, 40)
154			if newWidth != m.renderedWidth {
155				m.renderBody()
156			}
157			return m, nil
158	
159		case PushAddedMsg:
160			if m.article != nil && msg.ArticleID == m.article.ID {
161				ctx := context.Background()
162				// Re-fetch from DB so timestamps and recommend_score reflect
163				// the canonical state, not the just-synthesized broker payload.
164				if pushes, err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
165					m.pushes = pushes
166				}
167				if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
168					m.article = a
169				}
170			}
171	
172		case ArticleUpdatedMsg:
173			if m.article != nil && msg.ArticleID == m.article.ID {
174				// Edit happened in another session — refetch the canonical
175				// title/body so we render the new text.
176				if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(context.Background(), m.article.ID); err == nil {
177					m.article = a
178					m.renderBody()
179					// Reset scroll if the new body is shorter than where we
180					// were parked.
181					if m.scroll > m.bodyLineCount() {
182						m.scroll = 0
183					}
184				}
185			}
186			return m, nil
187	
188		case tea.KeyMsg:
189			if m.pushing {
190				return m.updatePushInput(msg)
191			}
192			if m.pendingDelete {
193				return m.updateDeleteConfirm(msg)
194			}
195			switch msg.String() {
196			case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
197				boardID := int64(0)
198				if m.article != nil {
199					boardID = m.article.BoardID
200				}
201				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: boardID} }
202			case "Q":
203				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
204			case "up", "k":
205				if m.scroll > 0 {
206					m.scroll--
207				}
208			case "down", "j":
209				m.scroll++
210			case "pgup", "b":
211				m.scroll = max(0, m.scroll-10)
212			case "pgdown", " ":
213				m.scroll += 10
214			case "g":
215				m.scroll = 0
216			case "G":
217				m.scroll = max(0, m.bodyLineCount()-m.viewportLines())
218			case "[":
219				if m.article == nil {
220					return m, nil
221				}
222				prev, _, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().NeighboursOf(context.Background(), m.article.BoardID, m.article.ID)
223				if err != nil || prev == 0 {
224					return m, nil
225				}
226				return m, func() tea.Msg {
227					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ArticleID: prev, BoardID: m.article.BoardID}
228				}
229			case "]":
230				if m.article == nil {
231					return m, nil
232				}
233				_, next, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().NeighboursOf(context.Background(), m.article.BoardID, m.article.ID)
234				if err != nil || next == 0 {
235					return m, nil
236				}
237				return m, func() tea.Msg {
238					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ArticleID: next, BoardID: m.article.BoardID}
239				}
240			case "+":
241				if !m.canPush() {
242					return m, nil
243				}
244				return m.openPush(store.PushKindPush), nil
245			case "-":
246				if !m.canPush() {
247					return m, nil
248				}
249				return m.openPush(store.PushKindBoo), nil
250			case "=":
251				if !m.canPush() {
252					return m, nil
253				}
254				return m.openPush(store.PushKindArrow), nil
255			case "p":
256				// Advance the push cursor. -1 → 0 → ... → len-1 → -1 (cycles
257				// back to "no selection, D targets the article").
258				if len(m.pushes) == 0 {
259					return m, nil
260				}
261				m.pushCursor++
262				if m.pushCursor >= len(m.pushes) {
263					m.pushCursor = -1
264				}
265				m.err = ""
266				return m, nil
267			case "P":
268				if len(m.pushes) == 0 {
269					return m, nil
270				}
271				if m.pushCursor < 0 {
272					m.pushCursor = len(m.pushes) - 1
273				} else {
274					m.pushCursor--
275				}
276				m.err = ""
277				return m, nil
278			case "D":
279				if m.pushCursor >= 0 {
280					if !m.canDeletePush(m.pushCursor) {
281						m.err = "權限不足 (only the push author or a mod can delete it)"
282						return m, nil
283					}
284				} else {
285					if !m.canDeleteArticle() {
286						m.err = "權限不足 (only the author or a mod can delete)"
287						return m, nil
288					}
289				}
290				m.pendingDelete = true
291				m.err = ""
292				return m, nil
293			case "E":
294				if m.article == nil {
295					return m, nil
296				}
297				if !m.canEditArticle() {
298					m.err = "權限不足 (only the author or a mod can edit)"
299					return m, nil
300				}
301				id := m.article.ID
302				boardID := m.article.BoardID
303				return m, func() tea.Msg {
304					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleEdit, ArticleID: id, BoardID: boardID}
305				}
306			case "y":
307				if m.article == nil {
308					return m, nil
309				}
310				id := m.article.ID
311				boardID := m.article.BoardID
312				return m, func() tea.Msg {
313					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleExport, ArticleID: id, BoardID: boardID}
314				}
315			}
316		}
317		return m, nil
318	}
319	
320	// updateDeleteConfirm handles y/n while the confirm overlay is up.
321	// y: dispatch the delete; if targeting a push, refresh the push list
322	// and the article (for the score). If targeting the article, navigate
323	// back to the board view.
324	// n / esc: cancel.
325	func (m articleViewModel) updateDeleteConfirm(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
326		switch msg.String() {
327		case "y", "Y":
328			if m.article == nil || m.deps.User == nil {
329				m.pendingDelete = false
330				return m, nil
331			}
332			ctx := context.Background()
333			if m.pushCursor >= 0 && m.pushCursor < len(m.pushes) {
334				pushID := m.pushes[m.pushCursor].ID
335				if err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().Delete(ctx, pushID, m.deps.User.ID, m.deps.User.Role); err != nil {
336					m.err = err.Error()
337					m.pendingDelete = false
338					return m, nil
339				}
340				// Refresh the push list and re-fetch the article so its
341				// recommend_score reflects the reverted delta.
342				if pushes, err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
343					m.pushes = pushes
344				}
345				if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
346					m.article = a
347				}
348				// Clamp the cursor so the next D / p continues from a valid index.
349				if m.pushCursor >= len(m.pushes) {
350					m.pushCursor = -1
351				}
352				m.pendingDelete = false
353				return m, nil
354			}
355			if err := m.deps.Store.Articles().Delete(ctx, m.article.ID, m.deps.User.ID, m.deps.User.Role); err != nil {
356				m.err = err.Error()
357				m.pendingDelete = false
358				return m, nil
359			}
360			boardID := m.article.BoardID
361			return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: boardID} }
362		case "n", "N", "esc":
363			m.pendingDelete = false
364			return m, nil
365		}
366		return m, nil
367	}
368	
369	// bodyLineCount returns the number of lines View will render for the
370	// loaded article. Uses the glamour-rendered body so g/G/PgDn agree
371	// with what's actually on screen; falls back to raw if rendering hasn't
372	// run yet (shouldn't happen post-construction but defensive).
373	func (m articleViewModel) bodyLineCount() int {
374		if m.rendered != "" {
375			return len(strings.Split(m.rendered, "\n"))
376		}
377		if m.article == nil {
378			return 0
379		}
380		return len(strings.Split(m.article.Body, "\n"))
381	}
382	
383	// viewportLines mirrors the maxLines computation in View so g/G/PgDn
384	// agree on how many lines fit on screen.
385	func (m articleViewModel) viewportLines() int {
386		return max(m.height-16, 5)
387	}
388	
389	func (m articleViewModel) openPush(k store.PushKind) tea.Model {
390		m.pushing = true
391		m.pushKind = k
392		m.pushIn.SetValue("")
393		m.pushIn.Focus()
394		m.err = ""
395		return m
396	}
397	
398	func (m articleViewModel) updatePushInput(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
399		switch msg.String() {
400		case "esc":
401			m.pushing = false
402			m.pushIn.Blur()
403			return m, nil
404		case "enter":
405			body := strings.TrimSpace(m.pushIn.Value())
406			if body == "" && m.pushKind != store.PushKindArrow {
407				m.err = "comment required for 推/噓"
408				return m, nil
409			}
410			ctx := context.Background()
411			u := m.deps.User
412			p, err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().Create(ctx, m.article.ID, u.ID, u.UserID, m.pushKind, body)
413			if err != nil {
414				m.err = err.Error()
415				return m, nil
416			}
417			// Append locally so the originator sees it immediately.
418			m.pushes = append(m.pushes, p)
419			if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
420				m.article = a
421			}
422			m.pushing = false
423			m.pushIn.Blur()
424			// Broadcast to other live sessions; they filter by ArticleID.
425			if m.deps.Broker != nil {
426				m.deps.Broker.SendToAll(u.ID, PushAddedMsg{
427					ArticleID:  m.article.ID,
428					UserUserID: u.UserID,
429					Kind:       string(m.pushKind),
430					Body:       body,
431				})
432			}
433			return m, nil
434		}
435		var cmd tea.Cmd
436		m.pushIn, cmd = m.pushIn.Update(msg)
437		return m, cmd
438	}
439	
440	func (m articleViewModel) View() string {
441		if m.loadErr != nil {
442			return "\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n"
443		}
444		if m.article == nil {
445			return "\n  " + StyleDim.Render("(no article)") + "\n"
446		}
447		a := m.article
448	
449		var b strings.Builder
450		b.WriteString("\n")
451		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" 文章 #%d · %s ", a.ID, a.AuthorUserID)))
452		b.WriteString("\n\n")
453	
454		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Title: ") + a.Title + "\n")
455		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Date:  ") + a.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02 15:04:05") + "\n")
456		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Score: ") + fmt.Sprintf("%d", a.RecommendScore) + "\n")
457		b.WriteString("\n")
458	
459		body := m.rendered
460		if body == "" {
461			body = a.Body
462		}
463		bodyLines := strings.Split(body, "\n")
464		start := min(m.scroll, len(bodyLines))
465		maxLines := max(m.height-16, 5)
466		end := min(start+maxLines, len(bodyLines))
467		for _, line := range bodyLines[start:end] {
468			b.WriteString(line + "\n")
469		}
470		if start > 0 || end < len(bodyLines) {
471			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("[lines %d-%d / %d]", start+1, end, len(bodyLines))))
472			b.WriteString("\n")
473		}
474	
475		if len(m.pushes) > 0 {
476			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("── 推文 (%d) ──", len(m.pushes))) + "\n")
477			for i, p := range m.pushes {
478				ts := p.CreatedAt.Format("01/02 15:04")
479				gutter := "  "
480				if i == m.pushCursor {
481					gutter = "▸ "
482				}
483				line := fmt.Sprintf("%s%s %s %s  %s",
484					gutter,
485					renderPushKind(p.Kind),
486					PadRight(p.UserUserID, 14),
487					p.Body,
488					StyleDim.Render(ts),
489				)
490				if i == m.pushCursor {
491					line = StyleHighlight.Render(line)
492				}
493				b.WriteString(line + "\n")
494			}
495		}
496	
497		if m.pushing {
498			b.WriteString("\n  ")
499			b.WriteString(renderPushKind(m.pushKind) + " ")
500			b.WriteString(m.pushIn.View())
501			b.WriteString("\n")
502			if m.err != "" {
503				b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err) + "\n")
504			}
505			b.WriteString("  " + StyleHelp.Render("Enter send · Esc cancel"))
506		} else if m.pendingDelete {
507			prompt := "確定刪除這篇文章? (y/N)"
508			if m.pushCursor >= 0 && m.pushCursor < len(m.pushes) {
509				prompt = fmt.Sprintf("確定刪除推文 #%d? (y/N)", m.pushCursor+1)
510			}
511			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+prompt))
512		} else {
513			help := "j/k scroll · y 匯出"
514			if m.canPush() {
515				help += " · + 推 · - 噓 · = →"
516			}
517			if len(m.pushes) > 0 {
518				help += " · p/P 選推文"
519			}
520			if m.canEditArticle() {
521				help += " · E 編輯"
522			}
523			if m.pushCursor >= 0 && m.canDeletePush(m.pushCursor) {
524				help += " · D 刪除推文"
525			} else if m.canDeleteArticle() {
526				help += " · D 刪除文章"
527			}
528			help += " · Esc/← back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
529			if m.err != "" {
530				b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err))
531			}
532			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render(help))
533		}
534		b.WriteString("\n")
535		return b.String()
536	}
537	
538	func renderPushKind(k store.PushKind) string {
539		switch k {
540		case store.PushKindPush:
541			return StylePushKind.Render("推")
542		case store.PushKindBoo:
543			return StyleBooKind.Render("噓")
544		case store.PushKindArrow:
545			return StyleArrowKind.Render("→")
546		}
547		return "?"
548	}
549
```

Excellent! Now I have all the pieces. Let me write a comprehensive summary report.

---

## Summary Report: Permission System, Article-Edit Pattern & Form Navigation

### 1. **Permission System Layout**

**Type & Definition:**
- **Role type**: String enum (`type Role string`), defined in `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/role.go:1-38`
- Four roles: `RoleGuest` ("guest"), `RoleUser` ("user"), `RoleMod` ("mod"), `RoleAdmin` ("admin")
- Stored as TEXT column with CHECK constraint; ordered by rank (0→1→2→3)

**Canonical "is mod or admin" check:**
```
Role.AtLeast(min Role) bool
/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/role.go:25-32
```
Example usage in delete/edit gates: `requesterRole.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)` (see `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/articles.go:131, 157`)

**Board-mod binding:**
- **NOT per-board**. `BM` field in Board struct (`/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/boards.go:15`) is a comma-separated string (pttbbs heritage, line 28 in migration), but the current codebase **does not use it** for permission checks
- Mod/admin are **global roles** in the `users.role` column
- The `BM` field is a column name but currently unused in TUI permission gates

---

### 2. **Article-Edit Screen Structure**

**File**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/tui/screen_article_edit.go`

**(a) Model struct fields** (lines 23–34):
```go
type articleEditModel struct {
    deps      Deps                      // Store, User, Broker
    articleID int64
    boardID   int64
    title     textinput.Model           // bubbles textarea input
    body      textarea.Model            // bubbles textarea for multi-line
    focus     int                       // editFocusTitle (0) or editFocusBody (1)
    width, height, err string           // layout + error messaging
    loadErr   error                     // early permission denial
}
```

**(b) Init/Update/View skeleton:**
- **Init** (line 73): Returns `textinput.Blink` command
- **Update** (lines 75–118):
  - WindowSizeMsg: resize body textarea width/height
  - KeyMsg: `esc` → cancel, `tab` → toggle focus, `ctrl+s` → submit, `enter` (in title) → advance to body
  - Delegates key input to active field's Update (title or body)
  - Early return if `loadErr` set (permission denied at construction)
- **View** (lines 174–199): Renders title input, body textarea, help text, errors

**(c) Submission & Navigation:**
- **Submit** (lines 145–172): Calls `m.deps.Store.Articles().Update(ctx, articleID, requesterID, requesterRole, title, body)`
- **Cancel** (lines 132–143): Returns `NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ...}` or fallback to board list if load failed
- Both return NavigateMsg from a closure (tea.Cmd)

**(d) Text input:**
- Uses **`textarea.Model`** from bubbles (line 8 import, line 28 field)
- **Multi-line support**: full textarea with SetWidth/SetHeight (lines 42–46)
- h/l are **NOT bound** — comment at line 22 confirms this is intentional so textarea receives raw keypresses

---

### 3. **Form Screens That Skip h/l Navigation**

**Per CLAUDE.md** (lines 131–135):
> Form screens skip h/l navigation so the keys remain available for text editing. Currently: `screen_register`, `screen_post_compose`, `screen_wb` (compose half).

**How they implement it:**
- **No separate flag or opt-out mechanism** — they simply **don't bind h/l in their Update switches**
- Example: `screen_post_compose.go` (lines 54–77) handles `esc`, `tab`, `ctrl+s`, `ctrl+i` but has **no `case "h"` or `case "l"`**. Instead, all KeyMsg falls through to the focused field's Update (lines 80–91)
- Same pattern: `screen_register.go` (lines 67–86) binds `up`, `down`, `tab`, `shift+tab`, `enter`, `esc` but not `h`/`l`

**Convention:**
For your banner-edit screen, simply omit h/l from the main Update switch; let the textarea receive the key directly.

---

### 4. **Permission Gates in TUI**

**Pattern:**
- Define a predicate method on the screen model (e.g., `canEditArticle()`, `canDeleteArticle()`)
- Check it **before** rendering the help text AND before dispatching the action
- If check fails, set an error message instead of navigating

**Example from screen_article_view.go:**

```go
// Helper at lines 135–143:
func (m articleViewModel) canEditArticle() bool {
    if m.deps.User == nil || m.article == nil {
        return false
    }
    if m.deps.User.ID == m.article.AuthorID {
        return true  // author can always edit
    }
    return m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)  // or mod+
}

// Gate in Update at lines 293–305:
case "E":
    if !m.canEditArticle() {
        m.err = "權限不足 (only the author or a mod can edit)"
        return m, nil  // stay on screen, show error
    }
    // ... navigate to edit screen
```

**In View, only show the key hint if allowed** (line 520–522):
```go
if m.canEditArticle() {
    help += " · E 編輯"
}
```

14	// Deps bundles everything every screen may need. Pass-by-value into

22	}
23	
24	// Root is the top-level tea.Model. It owns the active sub-model and

26	// and the toast footer.

29		sub    tea.Model
30		width  int
31		height int
32	
33		toast      string
34		toastUntil time.Time
35	}

38	
39	// NewRoot builds the Root with the right initial sub-screen.
40	//
41	// Priority: IsRegister wins over MustChangePassword wins over the regular
42	// authenticated main menu — these flags are mutually exclusive in practice
43	// (set by the SSH password callback / makeProgramHandler).
44	func NewRoot(deps Deps) Root {
45		r := Root{deps: deps}
46		switch {
47		case deps.IsRegister:
48			r.sub = newRegisterModel(deps)
49		case deps.MustChangePassword:
50			r.sub = newPasswordChangeModel(deps)
51		default:
52			r.sub = newMainMenuModel(deps)
53		}
54		return r
55	}
56	
57	func (m Root) Init() tea.Cmd {
58		return m.sub.Init()
59	}
60	
61	func (m Root) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
62		switch msg := msg.(type) {
63		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
64			m.width = msg.Width
65			m.height = msg.Height
66			var cmd tea.Cmd
67			m.sub, cmd = m.sub.Update(msg)
68			return m, cmd
69	
70		case tea.KeyMsg:
71			// Global ctrl+c always quits.
72			if msg.String() == "ctrl+c" {
73				return m, tea.Quit
74			}
75			// Global Ctrl+U opens water balloon inbox (logged-in only).
76			if msg.String() == "ctrl+u" && !m.deps.IsRegister {
77				return m.navigate(NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBInbox})
78			}
79	
80		case NavigateMsg:
81			return m.navigate(msg)
82	
83		case ErrorMsg:
84			if msg.Err != nil {
85				m.setToast("⚠ " + msg.Err.Error())
86			}
87			return m, nil
88	
89		case WBIncomingMsg:
90			m.setToast("💧 " + msg.FromUserID + ": " + msg.Body)
91			// Mark this WB as read so it doesn't replay on reconnect.
92			if m.deps.Store != nil && msg.ID != 0 {
93				go func(id int64) {
94					_ = m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(context.Background(), id)
95				}(msg.ID)
96			}
97			// fall through so the active screen can also react
98		}
99	
100		var cmd tea.Cmd
101		m.sub, cmd = m.sub.Update(msg)
102		return m, cmd
103	}
104	
105	func (m Root) View() string {
106		v := m.sub.View()
107		if m.toast != "" && time.Now().Before(m.toastUntil) {
108			v += "\n" + StyleToast.Render(m.toast)
109		}
110		return v
111	}
112	
113	func (m *Root) setToast(s string) {
114		m.toast = s
115		m.toastUntil = time.Now().Add(toastDuration)
116	}
117	
118	// guestWriteBlocked returns an ErrorMsg cmd if a guest is trying to enter
119	// a write-capable screen, otherwise nil. Layer 1 of the read-only path.
120	func (m Root) guestWriteBlocked(to Screen) tea.Cmd {
121		if m.deps.User == nil || m.deps.User.Role != store.RoleGuest {
122			return nil
123		}
124		switch to {
125		case ScreenPostCompose, ScreenWBCompose, ScreenMailCompose, ScreenAdminUsers, ScreenArticleEdit:
126			return func() tea.Msg { return ErrorMsg{Err: errors.New("guest 為唯讀帳號 (read-only)")} }
127		}
128		return nil
129	}
130	
131	// navigateNonAdminBlocked returns an ErrorMsg cmd if a non-admin tries
132	// to reach the admin user-management screen.
133	func (m Root) navigateNonAdminBlocked(to Screen) tea.Cmd {
134		if to != ScreenAdminUsers {
135			return nil
136		}
137		if m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role == store.RoleAdmin {
138			return nil
139		}
140		return func() tea.Msg { return ErrorMsg{Err: errors.New("管理員專用 (admin only)")} }
141	}
142	
143	func (m Root) navigate(n NavigateMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
144		if cmd := m.guestWriteBlocked(n.To); cmd != nil {
145			return m, cmd
146		}
147		if cmd := m.navigateNonAdminBlocked(n.To); cmd != nil {
148			return m, cmd
149		}
150		var sub tea.Model
151		switch n.To {
152		case ScreenMainMenu:
153			sub = newMainMenuModel(m.deps)
154		case ScreenBoardList:
155			sub = newBoardListModel(m.deps)
156		case ScreenBoardView:
157			sub = newBoardViewModel(m.deps, n.BoardID)
158		case ScreenArticleView:
159			sub = newArticleViewModel(m.deps, n.ArticleID)
160		case ScreenPostCompose:
161			sub = newPostComposeModel(m.deps, n.BoardID)
162		case ScreenWBInbox:
163			sub = newWBInboxModel(m.deps)
164		case ScreenWBCompose:
165			sub = newWBComposeModel(m.deps, n.Recipient)
166		case ScreenOnline:
167			sub = newOnlineModel(m.deps)
168		case ScreenMailInbox:
169			sub = newMailInboxModel(m.deps)
170		case ScreenMailThread:
171			sub = newMailThreadModel(m.deps, n.MailThreadID)
172		case ScreenMailCompose:
173			sub = newMailComposeModel(m.deps, n.Recipient, n.MailID)
174		case ScreenPasswordChange:
175			sub = newPasswordChangeModel(m.deps)
176		case ScreenAdminUsers:
177			sub = newAdminUsersModel(m.deps)
178		case ScreenArticleEdit:
179			sub = newArticleEditModel(m.deps, n.ArticleID)
180		case ScreenArticleExport:
181			sub = newArticleExportModel(m.deps, n.ArticleID)
182		default:
183			// Unknown screen — leave sub untouched. Later steps add cases.
184			return m, nil
185		}
186		m.sub = sub
187		cmd := sub.Init()
188		if m.width > 0 {
189			// Forward current size to the new sub so it can lay out immediately.
190			var cmd2 tea.Cmd
191			m.sub, cmd2 = m.sub.Update(tea.WindowSizeMsg{Width: m.width, Height: m.height})
192			if cmd2 != nil {
193				cmd = tea.Batch(cmd, cmd2)
194			}
195		}
196		return m, cmd
197	}

22	}

24	func newMainMenuModel(deps Deps) mainMenuModel {
25		items := []menuItem{
26			{label: "看板列表 Boards", hint: "browse and read articles", to: ScreenBoardList},
27			{label: "水球 Water Balloons", hint: "private messages with online users", to: ScreenWBInbox},
28			{label: "線上使用者 Online", hint: "see who's logged in", to: ScreenOnline},
29			{label: "信箱 Mail", hint: "persistent threaded mail", to: ScreenMailInbox},
30		}
31		// Admin gets an extra entry; positioned before the Quit row so the
32		// numeric shortcut for Quit always lands on the LAST item regardless
33		// of role (the "5 quits" shortcut becomes "6 quits" for admin).
34		if deps.User != nil && deps.User.Role == store.RoleAdmin {
35			items = append(items, menuItem{
36				label: "管理 Admin",
37				hint:  "manage user roles",
38				to:    ScreenAdminUsers,
39			})
40		}
41		items = append(items, menuItem{label: "離線 Quit", hint: "disconnect", to: -1})
42		return mainMenuModel{deps: deps, items: items}
43	}
44	
45	func (m mainMenuModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
46	
47	func (m mainMenuModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
48		if k, ok := msg.(tea.KeyMsg); ok {
49			switch k.String() {
50			case "q", "esc":
51				return m, tea.Quit
52			case "up", "k":
53				if m.cursor > 0 {
54					m.cursor--
55				}
56				return m, nil
57			case "down", "j":
58				if m.cursor < len(m.items)-1 {
59					m.cursor++
60				}
61				return m, nil
62			case "enter", " ", "right", "l":
63				it := m.items[m.cursor]
64				if it.to == -1 {
65					return m, tea.Quit
66				}
67				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: it.to} }
68			case "1", "2", "3", "4", "5", "6":
69				idx := int(k.String()[0] - '1')
70				if idx < len(m.items) {
71					it := m.items[idx]
72					if it.to == -1 {
73						return m, tea.Quit
74					}
75					return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: it.to} }
76				}
77			}
78		}
79		return m, nil
80	}
81	
82	func (m mainMenuModel) View() string {
83		u := m.deps.User
84		nick := u.Nickname
85		if nick == "" {
86			nick = u.UserID
87		}
88	
89		var b strings.Builder
90		b.WriteString("\n")
91		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" SSH-BBS · %s (%s) ", nick, u.UserID)))
92		b.WriteString("\n\n")
93	
94		if u.LastLoginAt.Valid {
95			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("上次登入 %s · 累計登入 %d 次 · 發文 %d 篇",
96				u.LastLoginAt.Time.Format("2006-01-02 15:04"),
97				u.NumLogins,
98				u.NumPosts,
99			)))
100			b.WriteString("\n\n")
101		}
102	
103		b.WriteString("  ")
104		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 主選單 Main Menu "))
105		b.WriteString("\n\n")
106	
107		for i, it := range m.items {
108			marker := "  "
109			row := fmt.Sprintf("  %d. %s", i+1, it.label)
110			if i == m.cursor {
111				marker = "▸ "
112				row = StyleHighlight.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" %d. %-32s ", i+1, it.label))
113			}
114			b.WriteString(marker + row)
115			if it.hint != "" {
116				b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(it.hint))
117			}
118			b.WriteString("\n")
119		}
120	
121		b.WriteString("\n  ")
122		b.WriteString(StyleHelp.Render(fmt.Sprintf("↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l choose · 1-%d jump · q quit", len(m.items))))
123		b.WriteString("\n")
124		return b.String()
125	}
126

Great! Now I have the full picture. Let me compile my findings:

## Lay of the Land: Board Banner Feature Design Reference

**1. Welcome Article Seed Flow**

The welcome article seed is triggered in `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/cmd/sshbbs/main.go:58`, calling:
```go
seed.Articles(context.Background(), st, auth.ReservedUsernameAdmin)
```

The "first boot vs already seeded" decision is made by **article existence per board** in `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/seed/articles.go:86–107`. It calls `st.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, board.ID, 1)` and only seeds if the result is empty (`len(existing) == 0`). Once a board has at least one article, all seed files targeting it are skipped. This is idempotent—an admin who edits the welcome article won't see their changes overwritten on restart.

**2. `go:embed` Usage**

The welcome markdown is embedded in `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/seed/articles.go:35`:
```go
//go:embed articles/*.md
var articlesFS embed.FS
```

The directory structure is `internal/seed/articles/welcome.md`. Currently only one file (`welcome.md`), but the glob `articles/*.md` allows adding more `.md` files to the same directory—they'll be auto-picked at build. Each `.md` file uses frontmatter with `board:` and `title:` fields to declare the target board.

**3. `boards` Table Schema**

Current schema from `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/migrations/0001_init.sql:23–31`:
```sql
CREATE TABLE boards (
    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    name            TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE COLLATE NOCASE,
    title           TEXT NOT NULL,
    description     TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    bm              TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
    attr            INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);
```

Highest existing migration: **0005** (`0005_articles_updated_at.sql`). Your banner migration would be **0006**.

**4. Boards Seed**

Default boards are seeded in *two* places (belt-and-braces):
- **Migration 0002** (`/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/migrations/0002_seed_boards.sql`): SQL `INSERT OR IGNORE` at schema-apply time.
- **Runtime** (`/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/boards.go:70–80`, `BoardRepo.SeedDefaults()`): Called from `cmd/sshbbs/main.go:50`, runs every startup with `INSERT OR IGNORE` (no-op if rows exist).

Boards seeded: `Welcome`, `Test`, `ChitChat` with titles in Traditional Chinese. This is separate from the article seed flow.

**5. BoardRepo Methods**

Defined in `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/boards.go:20`. Current public methods:
- `List(ctx)` — all boards
- `GetByID(ctx, id)` — fetch by ID
- `GetByName(ctx, name)` — fetch by name
- `SeedDefaults(ctx)` — idempotent insert

The `Board` struct (lines 10–18) has no banner field yet. You'll need to add a `Banner` field to the struct, update `boardColumns` constant, `scanBoard()` function, and create an `UpdateBanner(ctx, boardID, banner)` method. The pattern: use `writeMu.Lock()` (see `SeedDefaults` line 71) for writes, `UPDATE boards SET banner = ? WHERE id = ?`.

8	// Force lipgloss's package-level renderer to a profile that supports

10	// which is wrong for a server: stdout might be redirected to a log file
11	// (e.g. `./sshbbs >server.log 2>&1 &`), making termenv pick `Ascii` and
12	// silently strip every Background() escape we render. The actual SSH
13	// session renders into the client's terminal (capable of TrueColor in
14	// every modern client), so committing to TrueColor here is safe and
15	// matches what wish's per-session renderer would pick anyway.

 `./internal/store/role.go`

```text
1	package store
2	
3	// Role is the user permission tier. Stored as a TEXT column with a CHECK
4	// constraint; ordered by AtLeast for permission gates. Lives in the store
5	// package (rather than auth) because the type is the column type, and
6	// auth already imports store — the reverse would create a cycle.
7	type Role string
8	
9	const (
10		RoleGuest Role = "guest" // read-only spectator; no password required
11		RoleUser  Role = "user"  // registered, default after register
12		RoleMod   Role = "mod"   // can delete anyone's article/push
13		RoleAdmin Role = "admin" // can promote/demote others
14	)
15	
16	var roleRank = map[Role]int{
17		RoleGuest: 0,
18		RoleUser:  1,
19		RoleMod:   2,
20		RoleAdmin: 3,
21	}
22	
23	// AtLeast returns true if r is at or above min in the rank table.
24	// Unknown roles always return false (fail-closed).
25	func (r Role) AtLeast(min Role) bool {
26		rr, rok := roleRank[r]
27		mr, mok := roleRank[min]
28		if !rok || !mok {
29			return false
30		}
31		return rr >= mr
32	}
33	
34	// Valid reports whether r is one of the four known role values.
35	func (r Role) Valid() bool {
36		_, ok := roleRank[r]
37		return ok
38	}
39
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	package auth
2	
3	import (
4		"net"
5		"strings"
6	
7		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
8	)
9	
10	// MustChangePasswordRemoteBlocked reports whether an SSH login attempt
11	// for an admin account whose password is still the bootstrap default
12	// should be refused because the connection came from outside the loopback
13	// range. The intent: if the binary is published with the baked default
14	// `admin`/`admin` and the operator never rotated it, a remote attacker
15	// can't crack it just by reading the source. Local administration via
16	// `ssh admin@localhost` (the documented bootstrap path) still works.
17	//
18	// The host parameter is the SSH session's RemoteAddr().String() — typically
19	// "127.0.0.1:1234" or "[::1]:5678". Unparseable hosts are treated as
20	// non-loopback (fail-closed).
21	func MustChangePasswordRemoteBlocked(u *store.User, host string) bool {
22		if u == nil {
23			return false
24		}
25		if u.Role != store.RoleAdmin {
26			return false
27		}
28		if !u.MustChangePassword {
29			return false
30		}
31		return !isLoopbackHost(host)
32	}
33	
34	func isLoopbackHost(host string) bool {
35		if host == "" {
36			return false
37		}
38		// Strip the port if present. SplitHostPort fails on bare hosts like
39		// "127.0.0.1" — fall back to using the raw string.
40		h, _, err := net.SplitHostPort(host)
41		if err != nil {
42			h = host
43		}
44		// Trim IPv6 brackets if SplitHostPort didn't (it normally does).
45		h = strings.TrimPrefix(h, "[")
46		h = strings.TrimSuffix(h, "]")
47		ip := net.ParseIP(h)
48		if ip == nil {
49			// Hostname like "localhost". Conservatively accept the literal
50			// "localhost" string; anything else is non-loopback.
51			return strings.EqualFold(h, "localhost")
52		}
53		return ip.IsLoopback()
54	}
55
```

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"strings"
7	
8		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textarea"
9		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput"
10		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
11	
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
13	)
14	
15	const (
16		editFocusTitle = iota
17		editFocusBody
18	)
19	
20	// articleEditModel mirrors postComposeModel but pre-fills from an existing
21	// article and routes to ArticleRepo.Update on submit. This is a form
22	// screen — h/l are intentionally NOT bound (they belong to the textarea).
23	type articleEditModel struct {
24		deps      Deps
25		articleID int64
26		boardID   int64
27		title     textinput.Model
28		body      textarea.Model
29		focus     int
30		width     int
31		height    int
32		err       string
33		loadErr   error
34	}
35	
36	func newArticleEditModel(deps Deps, articleID int64) articleEditModel {
37		ti := textinput.New()
38		ti.Placeholder = "標題 title"
39		ti.CharLimit = 64
40		ti.Width = 60
41	
42		ta := textarea.New()
43		ta.Placeholder = "內容 body…"
44		ta.CharLimit = 8000
45		ta.SetWidth(72)
46		ta.SetHeight(12)
47	
48		m := articleEditModel{deps: deps, articleID: articleID, title: ti, body: ta, focus: editFocusTitle}
49	
50		if deps.User == nil {
51			m.loadErr = errors.New("not logged in")
52			return m
53		}
54		a, err := deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(context.Background(), articleID)
55		if err != nil {
56			m.loadErr = err
57			return m
58		}
59		// Permission check at construction time — deny early so the screen
60		// doesn't even mount for a user who can't edit. Mirrors
61		// canDeleteArticle: author OR mod+.
62		if a.AuthorID != deps.User.ID && !deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod) {
63			m.loadErr = store.ErrPermissionDenied
64			return m
65		}
66		m.boardID = a.BoardID
67		m.title.SetValue(a.Title)
68		m.body.SetValue(a.Body)
69		m.title.Focus()
70		return m
71	}
72	
73	func (m articleEditModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return textinput.Blink }
74	
75	func (m articleEditModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
76		switch msg := msg.(type) {
77		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
78			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
79			bodyW := max(40, msg.Width-8)
80			bodyH := max(8, msg.Height-12)
81			m.body.SetWidth(bodyW)
82			m.body.SetHeight(bodyH)
83			m.title.Width = max(20, msg.Width-12)
84			return m, nil
85	
86		case tea.KeyMsg:
87			if m.loadErr != nil {
88				// Any key bounces back to article view (or board view if we
89				// don't even know the article).
90				return m, m.cancelCmd()
91			}
92			switch msg.String() {
93			case "esc":
94				return m, m.cancelCmd()
95			case "tab":
96				m.toggleFocus()
97				return m, nil
98			case "ctrl+s":
99				return m.submit()
100			}
101		}
102	
103		if m.loadErr != nil {
104			return m, nil
105		}
106	
107		var cmd tea.Cmd
108		if m.focus == editFocusTitle {
109			if k, ok := msg.(tea.KeyMsg); ok && k.String() == "enter" {
110				m.toggleFocus()
111				return m, nil
112			}
113			m.title, cmd = m.title.Update(msg)
114		} else {
115			m.body, cmd = m.body.Update(msg)
116		}
117		return m, cmd
118	}
119	
120	func (m *articleEditModel) toggleFocus() {
121		if m.focus == editFocusTitle {
122			m.focus = editFocusBody
123			m.title.Blur()
124			m.body.Focus()
125		} else {
126			m.focus = editFocusTitle
127			m.body.Blur()
128			m.title.Focus()
129		}
130	}
131	
132	func (m articleEditModel) cancelCmd() tea.Cmd {
133		id := m.articleID
134		boardID := m.boardID
135		return func() tea.Msg {
136			// If we never loaded a board id (loadErr early), fall back to the
137			// board list — better than landing on an invalid article view.
138			if boardID == 0 {
139				return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardList}
140			}
141			return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ArticleID: id, BoardID: boardID}
142		}
143	}
144	
145	func (m articleEditModel) submit() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
146		title := strings.TrimSpace(m.title.Value())
147		body := m.body.Value()
148		if title == "" {
149			m.err = "title is required"
150			return m, nil
151		}
152		if strings.TrimSpace(body) == "" {
153			m.err = "body is required"
154			return m, nil
155		}
156		ctx := context.Background()
157		u := m.deps.User
158		if err := m.deps.Store.Articles().Update(ctx, m.articleID, u.ID, u.Role, title, body); err != nil {
159			m.err = err.Error()
160			return m, nil
161		}
162		if m.deps.Broker != nil {
163			// Exclude the editor's own session — they already mutated their
164			// local article state by reloading on the way back to article view.
165			m.deps.Broker.SendToAll(u.ID, ArticleUpdatedMsg{ArticleID: m.articleID})
166		}
167		id := m.articleID
168		boardID := m.boardID
169		return m, func() tea.Msg {
170			return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ArticleID: id, BoardID: boardID}
171		}
172	}
173	
174	func (m articleEditModel) View() string {
175		var b strings.Builder
176		b.WriteString("\n")
177		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 編輯文章 Edit Post "))
178		b.WriteString("\n\n")
179	
180		if m.loadErr != nil {
181			b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n")
182			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("press any key to go back"))
183			b.WriteString("\n")
184			return b.String()
185		}
186	
187		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Title:") + "\n")
188		b.WriteString("  " + m.title.View() + "\n\n")
189	
190		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Body:") + "\n")
191		b.WriteString("  " + indent(m.body.View(), "  ") + "\n")
192	
193		if m.err != "" {
194			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err) + "\n")
195		}
196		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("Tab switch field · Enter (in title) → body · Ctrl+S save · Esc cancel"))
197		b.WriteString("\n")
198		return b.String()
199	}
200

1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"

7		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textarea"
