10	
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/markdown"
12	)
13	
14	const (
15		composeFocusTitle = iota
16		composeFocusBody
17	)
18	
19	type postComposeModel struct {
20		deps    Deps
21		boardID int64
22		title   textinput.Model
23		body    textarea.Model
24		focus   int
25		width   int
26		height  int
27		err     string
28	}
29	
30	func newPostComposeModel(deps Deps, boardID int64) postComposeModel {
31		ti := textinput.New()
32		ti.Placeholder = "標題 title"
33		ti.CharLimit = 64
34		ti.Width = 60
35		ti.Focus()
36	
37		ta := textarea.New()
38		ta.Placeholder = "內容 body…"
39		ta.CharLimit = 8000
40		ta.SetWidth(72)
41		ta.SetHeight(12)
42	
43		return postComposeModel{
44			deps:    deps,
45			boardID: boardID,
46			title:   ti,
47			body:    ta,
48			focus:   composeFocusTitle,
49		}
50	}
51	
52	func (m postComposeModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return textinput.Blink }
53	
54	func (m postComposeModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
55		switch msg := msg.(type) {
56		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
57			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
58			bodyW := max(40, msg.Width-8)
59			bodyH := max(8, msg.Height-12)
60			m.body.SetWidth(bodyW)
61			m.body.SetHeight(bodyH)
62			m.title.Width = max(20, msg.Width-12)
63			return m, nil
64	
65		case tea.KeyMsg:
66			switch msg.String() {
67			case "esc":
68				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: m.boardID} }
69			case "tab":
70				m.toggleFocus()
71				return m, nil
72			case "ctrl+s":
73				return m.submit()
74			case "ctrl+i":
75				m.importFromBody()
76				return m, nil
77			}
78		}
79	
80		var cmd tea.Cmd
81		if m.focus == composeFocusTitle {
82			// In title field, Enter advances to body.
83			if k, ok := msg.(tea.KeyMsg); ok && k.String() == "enter" {
84				m.toggleFocus()
85				return m, nil
86			}
87			m.title, cmd = m.title.Update(msg)
88		} else {
89			m.body, cmd = m.body.Update(msg)
90		}
91		return m, cmd
92	}
93	
94	func (m *postComposeModel) toggleFocus() {
95		if m.focus == composeFocusTitle {
96			m.focus = composeFocusBody
97			m.title.Blur()
98			m.body.Focus()
99		} else {
100			m.focus = composeFocusTitle
101			m.body.Blur()
102			m.title.Focus()
103		}
104	}
105	
106	// importFromBody parses the current body as markdown and prefills title +
107	// body from any frontmatter found. If the body has no frontmatter it's
108	// left alone (with a helpful error). Pushes in the parsed input are
109	// silently ignored — see plan: import via Ctrl+I always credits the
110	// current user as author and never restores foreign-authored pushes.
111	func (m *postComposeModel) importFromBody() {
112		raw := m.body.Value()
113		if strings.TrimSpace(raw) == "" {
114			m.err = "貼上 markdown（含 --- frontmatter ---）後再按 Ctrl+I"
115			return
116		}
117		parsed, err := markdown.Parse(raw)
118		if err != nil {
119			m.err = "parse: " + err.Error()
120			return
121		}
122		if parsed.Title == "" && parsed.Body == strings.TrimRight(raw, "\n") {
123			// Parser didn't find frontmatter — body is verbatim. Tell the
124			// user so they don't think Ctrl+I silently no-op'd.
125			m.err = "找不到 frontmatter（請貼上開頭含 --- ... --- 的 markdown）"
126			return
127		}
128		if parsed.Title != "" {
129			m.title.SetValue(parsed.Title)
130		}
131		m.body.SetValue(parsed.Body)
132		m.err = ""
133	}
134	
135	func (m postComposeModel) submit() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
136		title := strings.TrimSpace(m.title.Value())
137		body := m.body.Value()
138		if title == "" {
139			m.err = "title is required"
140			return m, nil
141		}
142		if strings.TrimSpace(body) == "" {
143			m.err = "body is required"
144			return m, nil
145		}
146		ctx := context.Background()
147		u := m.deps.User
148		art, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().Create(ctx, m.boardID, u.ID, u.UserID, title, body)
149		if err != nil {
150			m.err = err.Error()
151			return m, nil
152		}
153		if err := m.deps.Store.Users().IncrementPosts(ctx, u.ID); err != nil {
154			m.err = err.Error()
155			return m, nil
156		}
157		// Broadcast to other live sessions; recipients filter by BoardID and
158		// re-fetch the canonical list (mirrors the PushAddedMsg pattern).
159		if m.deps.Broker != nil {
160			m.deps.Broker.SendToAll(u.ID, ArticleAddedMsg{
161				BoardID:      m.boardID,
162				ArticleID:    art.ID,
163				AuthorUserID: u.UserID,
164				Title:        art.Title,
165			})
166		}
167		return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: m.boardID} }
168	}
169	
170	func (m postComposeModel) View() string {
171		var b strings.Builder
172		b.WriteString("\n")
173		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 發表新文章 New Post "))
174		b.WriteString("\n\n")
175	
176		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Title:") + "\n")
177		b.WriteString("  " + m.title.View() + "\n\n")
178	
179		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Body:") + "\n")
180		b.WriteString("  " + indent(m.body.View(), "  ") + "\n")
181	
182		if m.err != "" {
183			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err) + "\n")
184		}
185		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("Tab switch field · Enter (in title) → body · Ctrl+S send · Ctrl+I import markdown · Esc cancel"))
186		b.WriteString("\n")
187		return b.String()
188	}
189	
190	func indent(s, prefix string) string {
191		lines := strings.Split(s, "\n")
192		for i, ln := range lines {
193			if i == 0 {
194				continue
195			}
196			lines[i] = prefix + ln
197		}
198		return strings.Join(lines, "\n")
199	}

1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"strings"
7	
8		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput"
9		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
10	
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/auth"
12	)
13	
14	const (
15		regFieldUserID = iota
16		regFieldPassword
17		regFieldNickname
18		regFieldEmail
19		regFieldCount
20	)
21	
22	type registerModel struct {
23		deps    Deps
24		inputs  []textinput.Model
25		focus   int
26		err     string
27		success bool
28		done    string // success: registered userid
29	}
30	
31	func newRegisterModel(deps Deps) registerModel {
32		inputs := make([]textinput.Model, regFieldCount)
33	
34		inputs[regFieldUserID] = textinput.New()
35		inputs[regFieldUserID].Placeholder = "alice"
36		inputs[regFieldUserID].CharLimit = 12
37		inputs[regFieldUserID].Width = 32
38		inputs[regFieldUserID].Focus()
39	
40		inputs[regFieldPassword] = textinput.New()
41		inputs[regFieldPassword].Placeholder = "≥ 6 characters"
42		inputs[regFieldPassword].EchoMode = textinput.EchoPassword
43		inputs[regFieldPassword].EchoCharacter = '•'
44		inputs[regFieldPassword].CharLimit = 128
45		inputs[regFieldPassword].Width = 32
46	
47		inputs[regFieldNickname] = textinput.New()
48		inputs[regFieldNickname].Placeholder = "愛麗絲"
49		inputs[regFieldNickname].CharLimit = 64
50		inputs[regFieldNickname].Width = 40
51	
52		inputs[regFieldEmail] = textinput.New()
53		inputs[regFieldEmail].Placeholder = "alice@example.com"
54		inputs[regFieldEmail].CharLimit = 128
55		inputs[regFieldEmail].Width = 40
56	
57		return registerModel{deps: deps, inputs: inputs}
58	}
59	
60	func (m registerModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return textinput.Blink }
61	
62	func (m registerModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
63		switch msg := msg.(type) {
64		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
65			return m, nil
66	
67		case tea.KeyMsg:
68			if m.success {
69				return m, tea.Quit
70			}
71			switch msg.String() {
72			case "esc":
73				return m, tea.Quit
74			case "tab", "down":
75				m.advance(+1)
76				return m, nil
77			case "shift+tab", "up":
78				m.advance(-1)
79				return m, nil
80			case "enter":
81				if m.focus < len(m.inputs)-1 {
82					m.advance(+1)
83					return m, nil
84				}
85				return m.submit()
86			}
87		}
88	
89		var cmd tea.Cmd
90		m.inputs[m.focus], cmd = m.inputs[m.focus].Update(msg)
91		return m, cmd
92	}
93	
94	func (m *registerModel) advance(d int) {
95		n := len(m.inputs)
96		m.focus = (m.focus + d + n) % n
97		for i := range m.inputs {
98			if i == m.focus {
99				m.inputs[i].Focus()
100			} else {
101				m.inputs[i].Blur()
102			}
103		}
104	}
105	
106	func (m registerModel) submit() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
107		user, err := auth.Register(context.Background(), m.deps.Store,
108			m.inputs[regFieldUserID].Value(),
109			m.inputs[regFieldPassword].Value(),
110			m.inputs[regFieldNickname].Value(),
111			m.inputs[regFieldEmail].Value(),
112		)
113		if err != nil {
114			m.err = err.Error()
115			return m, nil
116		}
117		m.success = true
118		m.done = user.UserID
119		return m, nil
120	}
121	
122	func (m registerModel) View() string {
123		if m.success {
124			return StyleSuccess.Render("\n  ✓ 註冊成功！") +
125				"\n\n  請重新連線：\n\n    " +
126				StyleHighlight.Render(fmt.Sprintf("ssh %s@<host> -p <port>", m.done)) +
127				"\n\n  按任意鍵結束。\n"
128		}
129	
130		var b strings.Builder
131		b.WriteString("\n")
132		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render("  === 註冊新帳號 ==="))
133		b.WriteString("\n\n")
134	
135		labels := []string{
136			"帳號 user id (3-12, 字母開頭, 英數+底線)",
137			"密碼 password (≥ 6)",
138			"暱稱 nickname",
139			"Email (optional)",
140		}
141		for i, label := range labels {
142			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(label))
143			b.WriteString("\n  ")
144			b.WriteString(m.inputs[i].View())
145			b.WriteString("\n\n")
146		}
147	
148		if m.err != "" {
149			b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err) + "\n\n")
150		}
151		b.WriteString("  " + StyleHelp.Render("Tab/↓ next · Shift+Tab/↑ prev · Enter submit · Esc/Ctrl+C cancel") + "\n")
152		return b.String()
153	}
154

1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"

7	
8		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"

11	)
12	
13	type boardViewModel struct {
14		deps     Deps
15		board    *store.Board
16		articles []*store.Article
17		cursor   int
18		width    int
19		height   int
20		loadErr  error
21	}
22	
23	func newBoardViewModel(deps Deps, boardID int64) boardViewModel {
24		ctx := context.Background()
25		board, err := deps.Store.Boards().GetByID(ctx, boardID)
26		if err != nil {
27			return boardViewModel{deps: deps, loadErr: err}
28		}
29		articles, err := deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, boardID, 100)
30		return boardViewModel{deps: deps, board: board, articles: articles, loadErr: err}
31	}
32	
33	func (m boardViewModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
34	
35	func (m boardViewModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
36		switch msg := msg.(type) {
37		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
38			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
39			return m, nil
40		case ArticleAddedMsg:
41			if m.board != nil && msg.BoardID == m.board.ID {
42				ctx := context.Background()
43				if arts, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, m.board.ID, 100); err == nil {
44					m.articles = arts
45				}
46			}
47			return m, nil
48		case tea.KeyMsg:
49			switch msg.String() {
50			case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
51				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardList} }
52			case "Q":
53				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
54			case "up", "k", "[":
55				if m.cursor > 0 {
56					m.cursor--
57				}
58			case "down", "j", "]":
59				if m.cursor < len(m.articles)-1 {
60					m.cursor++
61				}
62			case "home", "g":
63				m.cursor = 0
64			case "end", "G":
65				if len(m.articles) > 0 {
66					m.cursor = len(m.articles) - 1
67				}
68			case "enter", " ", "right", "l":
69				if len(m.articles) == 0 {
70					return m, nil
71				}
72				a := m.articles[m.cursor]
73				return m, func() tea.Msg {
74					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ArticleID: a.ID, BoardID: a.BoardID}
75				}
76			case "p":
77				if m.board == nil {
78					return m, nil
79				}
80				return m, func() tea.Msg {
81					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenPostCompose, BoardID: m.board.ID}
82				}
83			}
84		}
85		return m, nil
86	}
87	
88	func (m boardViewModel) View() string {
89		var b strings.Builder
90		b.WriteString("\n")
91		if m.board != nil {
92			b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" 看板 %s · %s ", m.board.Name, m.board.Title)))
93		} else {
94			b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 看板 "))
95		}
96		b.WriteString("\n\n")
97	
98		if m.loadErr != nil {
99			b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n")
100			return b.String()
101		}
102		if len(m.articles) == 0 {
103			hint := "(no articles yet — press p to write one)"
104			help := "p post · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
105			if m.isGuest() {
106				hint = "(no articles yet)"
107				help = "Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
108			}
109			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(hint) + "\n")
110			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render(help))
111			return b.String()
112		}
113	
114		const (
115			idxW    = 4
116			dateW   = 11
117			scoreW  = 4
118			authorW = 14
119		)
120		titleW := max(20, m.width-2-idxW-1-dateW-1-scoreW-1-authorW-2)
121		header := fmt.Sprintf(" %s  %s  %s  %s  %s",
122			PadRight("#", idxW),
123			PadRight("Date", dateW),
124			PadRight("Sc.", scoreW),
125			PadRight("Author", authorW),
126			PadRight("Title", titleW),
127		)
128		b.WriteString(StyleDim.Render(header) + "\n")
129	
130		for i, a := range m.articles {
131			score := ""
132			switch {
133			case a.RecommendScore >= 100:
134				score = "爆"
135			case a.RecommendScore > 0:
136				score = fmt.Sprintf("%d", a.RecommendScore)
137			case a.RecommendScore < 0:
138				score = "X" + fmt.Sprintf("%d", -a.RecommendScore)
139			}
140			row := fmt.Sprintf(" %s  %s  %s  %s  %s",
141				PadRight(fmt.Sprintf("%d", i+1), idxW),
142				PadRight(a.CreatedAt.Format("01/02 15:04"), dateW),
143				PadRight(score, scoreW),
144				PadRight(a.AuthorUserID, authorW),
145				Truncate(a.Title, titleW),
146			)
147			if i == m.cursor {
148				b.WriteString(StyleHighlight.Render("▸"+row[1:]) + "\n")
149			} else {
150				b.WriteString(" " + row + "\n")
151			}
152		}
153	
154		help := "↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l open · p post · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
155		if m.isGuest() {
156			help = "↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l open · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
157		}
158		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render(help))
159		b.WriteString("\n")
160		return b.String()
161	}
162	
163	func (m boardViewModel) isGuest() bool {
164		return m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role == store.RoleGuest
165	}
166

31		recommend_score, filemode, created_at, updated_at`

33	func scanArticle(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Article, error) {
34		a := &Article{}
35		err := row.Scan(
36			&a.ID, &a.BoardID, &a.AuthorID, &a.AuthorUserID, &a.Title, &a.Body,
37			&a.RecommendScore, &a.Filemode, &a.CreatedAt, &a.UpdatedAt,
38		)
39		return a, err
40	}

42	func (r *ArticleRepo) ListByBoard(ctx context.Context, boardID int64, limit int) ([]*Article, error) {
43		if limit <= 0 {
44			limit = 50
45		}
46		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
47			`SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE board_id = ? ORDER BY created_at DESC, id DESC LIMIT ?`,
48			boardID, limit,
49		)
50		if err != nil {
51			return nil, err
52		}
53		defer rows.Close()
54		var out []*Article
55		for rows.Next() {
56			a, err := scanArticle(rows)
57			if err != nil {
58				return nil, err
59			}
60			out = append(out, a)
61		}
62		return out, rows.Err()
63	}

65	func (r *ArticleRepo) GetByID(ctx context.Context, id int64) (*Article, error) {
66		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, id)
67		a, err := scanArticle(row)
68		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
69			return nil, ErrArticleNotFound
70		}
71		return a, err
72	}

74	// NeighboursOf returns the article IDs immediately older (prev) and newer
75	// (next) than articleID within the same board, or 0 when there is no
76	// neighbour on that side. "Older" / "newer" are by id (monotonic insert
77	// order); this matches the user-visible "[ / ]" semantics in article view.
78	func (r *ArticleRepo) NeighboursOf(ctx context.Context, boardID, articleID int64) (prev, next int64, err error) {
79		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
80			`SELECT id FROM articles WHERE board_id = ? AND id < ? ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1`,
81			boardID, articleID,
82		)
83		if scanErr := row.Scan(&prev); scanErr != nil && !errors.Is(scanErr, sql.ErrNoRows) {
84			return 0, 0, scanErr
85		}
86		row = r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
87			`SELECT id FROM articles WHERE board_id = ? AND id > ? ORDER BY id ASC LIMIT 1`,
88			boardID, articleID,
89		)
90		if scanErr := row.Scan(&next); scanErr != nil && !errors.Is(scanErr, sql.ErrNoRows) {
91			return 0, 0, scanErr
92		}
93		return prev, next, nil
94	}
95	
96	func (r *ArticleRepo) Create(ctx context.Context, boardID, authorID int64, authorUserID, title, body string) (*Article, error) {
97		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
98		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
99		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
100			`INSERT INTO articles (board_id, author_id, author_userid, title, body) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
101			boardID, authorID, authorUserID, title, body,
102		)
103		if err != nil {
104			return nil, err
105		}
106		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
107		if err != nil {
108			return nil, err
109		}
110		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, id)
111		return scanArticle(row)
112	}
113	
114	// Delete hard-deletes the article. Permission: requester must be the author
115	// OR have a role at-or-above mod. Returns ErrArticleNotFound when the row
116	// is gone, ErrPermissionDenied when the requester is unauthorized.
117	// Cascades to pushes via the FK on pushes.article_id.
118	func (r *ArticleRepo) Delete(ctx context.Context, articleID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role) error {
119		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
120		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
121		var authorID int64
122		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
123			`SELECT author_id FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID,

128		if err != nil {

130		}
131		if authorID != requesterID && !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
132			return ErrPermissionDenied
133		}
134		_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx, `DELETE FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID)
135		return err
136	}

143	// edit flow.
144	func (r *ArticleRepo) Update(ctx context.Context, articleID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role, newTitle, newBody string) error {

147		var authorID int64

1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"strings"
7	
8		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput"
9		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
10		"github.com/charmbracelet/glamour"
11	
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
13	)
14	
15	// glamourFallbackWidth is the wrap width used before we get a
16	// WindowSizeMsg. 80 is the historical terminal default; the renderer
17	// re-runs with the real width as soon as the first resize lands.
18	const glamourFallbackWidth = 80
19	
20	type articleViewModel struct {
21		deps    Deps
22		article *store.Article
23		pushes  []*store.Push
24		width   int
25		height  int
26		scroll  int
27		loadErr error
28	
29		// rendered is the article body after running through glamour. We cache
30		// because (a) glamour's first call loads chroma's syntax highlighter
31		// (~tens of ms) and (b) View() is called on every keystroke. Re-render
32		// only when the article changes or the window width changes.
33		rendered      string
34		renderedWidth int
35	
36		// push input state
37		pushing  bool
38		pushKind store.PushKind
39		pushIn   textinput.Model
40		err      string
41	
42		// delete state. pendingDelete: showing the y/N confirm overlay.
43		// pushCursor: -1 = no push selected (D targets article); 0..len-1 = D targets that push.
44		pendingDelete bool
45		pushCursor    int
46	}
47	
48	// renderBody runs the article body through glamour with word-wrap set
49	// to the current viewport. Falls back to the raw body on any glamour
50	// error so a corrupt-markdown article is still readable.
51	func (m *articleViewModel) renderBody() {
52		if m.article == nil {
53			m.rendered = ""
54			m.renderedWidth = 0
55			return
56		}
57		width := m.width - 4
58		if width < 40 {
59			width = glamourFallbackWidth
60		}
61		r, err := glamour.NewTermRenderer(
62			glamour.WithStandardStyle("dark"),
63			glamour.WithWordWrap(width),
64		)
65		if err != nil {
66			m.rendered = m.article.Body
67			m.renderedWidth = width
68			return
69		}
70		out, err := r.Render(m.article.Body)
71		if err != nil {
72			m.rendered = m.article.Body
73			m.renderedWidth = width
74			return
75		}
76		// Trim leading/trailing blank lines glamour likes to add for
77		// breathing room — they push the content off-screen on small
78		// viewports. Internal blank lines stay.
79		m.rendered = strings.Trim(out, "\n")
80		m.renderedWidth = width
81	}
82	
83	func newArticleViewModel(deps Deps, articleID int64) articleViewModel {
84		ctx := context.Background()
85		a, err := deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, articleID)
86		if err != nil {
87			return articleViewModel{deps: deps, loadErr: err}
88		}
89		pushes, err := deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, articleID)
90	
91		ti := textinput.New()
92		ti.Placeholder = "comment…"
93		ti.CharLimit = 80
94		ti.Width = 60
95	
96		m := articleViewModel{deps: deps, article: a, pushes: pushes, loadErr: err, pushIn: ti, pushCursor: -1}
97		// Render with the fallback width so the body is readable even before
98		// the first WindowSizeMsg arrives — Update re-renders on resize.
99		m.renderBody()
100		return m
101	}
102	
103	// canDeleteArticle returns true if the current user may delete the loaded
104	// article (author, or mod-or-above). Used both as a permission gate at
105	// the key handler and to decide whether to render the help-line hint.
106	func (m articleViewModel) canDeleteArticle() bool {
107		if m.deps.User == nil || m.article == nil {
108			return false
109		}
110		if m.deps.User.ID == m.article.AuthorID {
111			return true
112		}
113		return m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
114	}
115	
116	// canDeletePush mirrors canDeleteArticle for the push at index idx.
117	func (m articleViewModel) canDeletePush(idx int) bool {
118		if m.deps.User == nil || idx < 0 || idx >= len(m.pushes) {
119			return false
120		}
121		if m.deps.User.ID == m.pushes[idx].UserID {
122			return true
123		}
124		return m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
125	}
126	
127	// canPush returns true if the current user is allowed to add new pushes.
128	// Guests can read but not write.
129	func (m articleViewModel) canPush() bool {
130		return m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role != store.RoleGuest
131	}
132	
133	// canEditArticle mirrors canDeleteArticle: the author may edit their own
134	// post, mods and admins may edit anyone's.
135	func (m articleViewModel) canEditArticle() bool {
136		if m.deps.User == nil || m.article == nil {
137			return false
138		}
139		if m.deps.User.ID == m.article.AuthorID {
140			return true
141		}
142		return m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
143	}
144	
145	func (m articleViewModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
146	
147	func (m articleViewModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
148		switch msg := msg.(type) {
149		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
150			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
151			// Only re-render when the width target actually changes — height
152			// changes don't affect glamour wrap.
153			newWidth := max(msg.Width-4, 40)
154			if newWidth != m.renderedWidth {
155				m.renderBody()
156			}
157			return m, nil
158	
159		case PushAddedMsg:
160			if m.article != nil && msg.ArticleID == m.article.ID {
161				ctx := context.Background()
162				// Re-fetch from DB so timestamps and recommend_score reflect
163				// the canonical state, not the just-synthesized broker payload.
164				if pushes, err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
165					m.pushes = pushes
166				}
167				if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
168					m.article = a
169				}
170			}
171	
172		case ArticleUpdatedMsg:
173			if m.article != nil && msg.ArticleID == m.article.ID {
174				// Edit happened in another session — refetch the canonical
175				// title/body so we render the new text.
176				if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(context.Background(), m.article.ID); err == nil {
177					m.article = a
178					m.renderBody()
179					// Reset scroll if the new body is shorter than where we
180					// were parked.
181					if m.scroll > m.bodyLineCount() {
182						m.scroll = 0
183					}
184				}
185			}
186			return m, nil
187	
188		case tea.KeyMsg:
189			if m.pushing {
190				return m.updatePushInput(msg)
191			}
192			if m.pendingDelete {
193				return m.updateDeleteConfirm(msg)
194			}
195			switch msg.String() {
196			case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
197				boardID := int64(0)
198				if m.article != nil {
199					boardID = m.article.BoardID
200				}
201				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: boardID} }
202			case "Q":
203				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
204			case "up", "k":
205				if m.scroll > 0 {
206					m.scroll--
207				}
208			case "down", "j":
209				m.scroll++
210			case "pgup", "b":
211				m.scroll = max(0, m.scroll-10)
212			case "pgdown", " ":
213				m.scroll += 10
214			case "g":
215				m.scroll = 0
216			case "G":
217				m.scroll = max(0, m.bodyLineCount()-m.viewportLines())
218			case "[":
219				if m.article == nil {
220					return m, nil
221				}
222				prev, _, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().NeighboursOf(context.Background(), m.article.BoardID, m.article.ID)
223				if err != nil || prev == 0 {
224					return m, nil
225				}
226				return m, func() tea.Msg {
227					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ArticleID: prev, BoardID: m.article.BoardID}
228				}
229			case "]":
230				if m.article == nil {
231					return m, nil
232				}
233				_, next, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().NeighboursOf(context.Background(), m.article.BoardID, m.article.ID)
234				if err != nil || next == 0 {
235					return m, nil
236				}
237				return m, func() tea.Msg {
238					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ArticleID: next, BoardID: m.article.BoardID}
239				}
240			case "+":
241				if !m.canPush() {
242					return m, nil
243				}
244				return m.openPush(store.PushKindPush), nil
245			case "-":
246				if !m.canPush() {
247					return m, nil
248				}
249				return m.openPush(store.PushKindBoo), nil
250			case "=":
251				if !m.canPush() {
252					return m, nil
253				}
254				return m.openPush(store.PushKindArrow), nil
255			case "p":
256				// Advance the push cursor. -1 → 0 → ... → len-1 → -1 (cycles
257				// back to "no selection, D targets the article").
258				if len(m.pushes) == 0 {
259					return m, nil
260				}
261				m.pushCursor++
262				if m.pushCursor >= len(m.pushes) {
263					m.pushCursor = -1
264				}
265				m.err = ""
266				return m, nil
267			case "P":
268				if len(m.pushes) == 0 {
269					return m, nil
270				}
271				if m.pushCursor < 0 {
272					m.pushCursor = len(m.pushes) - 1
273				} else {
274					m.pushCursor--
275				}
276				m.err = ""
277				return m, nil
278			case "D":
279				if m.pushCursor >= 0 {
280					if !m.canDeletePush(m.pushCursor) {
281						m.err = "權限不足 (only the push author or a mod can delete it)"
282						return m, nil
283					}
284				} else {
285					if !m.canDeleteArticle() {
286						m.err = "權限不足 (only the author or a mod can delete)"
287						return m, nil
288					}
289				}
290				m.pendingDelete = true
291				m.err = ""
292				return m, nil
293			case "E":
294				if m.article == nil {
295					return m, nil
296				}
297				if !m.canEditArticle() {
298					m.err = "權限不足 (only the author or a mod can edit)"
299					return m, nil
300				}
301				id := m.article.ID
302				boardID := m.article.BoardID
303				return m, func() tea.Msg {
304					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleEdit, ArticleID: id, BoardID: boardID}
305				}
306			case "y":
307				if m.article == nil {
308					return m, nil
309				}
310				id := m.article.ID
311				boardID := m.article.BoardID
312				return m, func() tea.Msg {
313					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleExport, ArticleID: id, BoardID: boardID}
314				}
315			}
316		}
317		return m, nil
318	}
319	
320	// updateDeleteConfirm handles y/n while the confirm overlay is up.
321	// y: dispatch the delete; if targeting a push, refresh the push list
322	// and the article (for the score). If targeting the article, navigate
323	// back to the board view.
324	// n / esc: cancel.
325	func (m articleViewModel) updateDeleteConfirm(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
326		switch msg.String() {
327		case "y", "Y":
328			if m.article == nil || m.deps.User == nil {
329				m.pendingDelete = false
330				return m, nil
331			}
332			ctx := context.Background()
333			if m.pushCursor >= 0 && m.pushCursor < len(m.pushes) {
334				pushID := m.pushes[m.pushCursor].ID
335				if err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().Delete(ctx, pushID, m.deps.User.ID, m.deps.User.Role); err != nil {
336					m.err = err.Error()
337					m.pendingDelete = false
338					return m, nil
339				}
340				// Refresh the push list and re-fetch the article so its
341				// recommend_score reflects the reverted delta.
342				if pushes, err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
343					m.pushes = pushes
344				}
345				if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
346					m.article = a
347				}
348				// Clamp the cursor so the next D / p continues from a valid index.
349				if m.pushCursor >= len(m.pushes) {
350					m.pushCursor = -1
351				}
352				m.pendingDelete = false
353				return m, nil
354			}
355			if err := m.deps.Store.Articles().Delete(ctx, m.article.ID, m.deps.User.ID, m.deps.User.Role); err != nil {
356				m.err = err.Error()
357				m.pendingDelete = false
358				return m, nil
359			}
360			boardID := m.article.BoardID
361			return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: boardID} }
362		case "n", "N", "esc":
363			m.pendingDelete = false
364			return m, nil
365		}
366		return m, nil
367	}
368	
369	// bodyLineCount returns the number of lines View will render for the
370	// loaded article. Uses the glamour-rendered body so g/G/PgDn agree
371	// with what's actually on screen; falls back to raw if rendering hasn't
372	// run yet (shouldn't happen post-construction but defensive).
373	func (m articleViewModel) bodyLineCount() int {
374		if m.rendered != "" {
375			return len(strings.Split(m.rendered, "\n"))
376		}
377		if m.article == nil {
378			return 0
379		}
380		return len(strings.Split(m.article.Body, "\n"))
381	}
382	
383	// viewportLines mirrors the maxLines computation in View so g/G/PgDn
384	// agree on how many lines fit on screen.
385	func (m articleViewModel) viewportLines() int {
386		return max(m.height-16, 5)
387	}
388	
389	func (m articleViewModel) openPush(k store.PushKind) tea.Model {
390		m.pushing = true
391		m.pushKind = k
392		m.pushIn.SetValue("")
393		m.pushIn.Focus()
394		m.err = ""
395		return m
396	}
397	
398	func (m articleViewModel) updatePushInput(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
399		switch msg.String() {
400		case "esc":
401			m.pushing = false
402			m.pushIn.Blur()
403			return m, nil
404		case "enter":
405			body := strings.TrimSpace(m.pushIn.Value())
406			if body == "" && m.pushKind != store.PushKindArrow {
407				m.err = "comment required for 推/噓"
408				return m, nil
409			}
410			ctx := context.Background()
411			u := m.deps.User
412			p, err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().Create(ctx, m.article.ID, u.ID, u.UserID, m.pushKind, body)
413			if err != nil {
414				m.err = err.Error()
415				return m, nil
416			}
417			// Append locally so the originator sees it immediately.
418			m.pushes = append(m.pushes, p)
419			if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
420				m.article = a
421			}
422			m.pushing = false
423			m.pushIn.Blur()
424			// Broadcast to other live sessions; they filter by ArticleID.
425			if m.deps.Broker != nil {
426				m.deps.Broker.SendToAll(u.ID, PushAddedMsg{
427					ArticleID:  m.article.ID,
428					UserUserID: u.UserID,
429					Kind:       string(m.pushKind),
430					Body:       body,
431				})
432			}
433			return m, nil
434		}
435		var cmd tea.Cmd
436		m.pushIn, cmd = m.pushIn.Update(msg)
437		return m, cmd
438	}
439	
440	func (m articleViewModel) View() string {
441		if m.loadErr != nil {
442			return "\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n"
443		}
444		if m.article == nil {
445			return "\n  " + StyleDim.Render("(no article)") + "\n"
446		}
447		a := m.article
448	
449		var b strings.Builder
450		b.WriteString("\n")
451		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" 文章 #%d · %s ", a.ID, a.AuthorUserID)))
452		b.WriteString("\n\n")
453	
454		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Title: ") + a.Title + "\n")
455		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Date:  ") + a.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02 15:04:05") + "\n")
456		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Score: ") + fmt.Sprintf("%d", a.RecommendScore) + "\n")
457		b.WriteString("\n")
458	
459		body := m.rendered
460		if body == "" {
461			body = a.Body
462		}
463		bodyLines := strings.Split(body, "\n")
464		start := min(m.scroll, len(bodyLines))
465		maxLines := max(m.height-16, 5)
466		end := min(start+maxLines, len(bodyLines))
467		for _, line := range bodyLines[start:end] {
468			b.WriteString(line + "\n")
469		}
470		if start > 0 || end < len(bodyLines) {
471			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("[lines %d-%d / %d]", start+1, end, len(bodyLines))))
472			b.WriteString("\n")
473		}
474	
475		if len(m.pushes) > 0 {
476			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("── 推文 (%d) ──", len(m.pushes))) + "\n")
477			for i, p := range m.pushes {
478				ts := p.CreatedAt.Format("01/02 15:04")
479				gutter := "  "
480				if i == m.pushCursor {
481					gutter = "▸ "
482				}
483				line := fmt.Sprintf("%s%s %s %s  %s",
484					gutter,
485					renderPushKind(p.Kind),
486					PadRight(p.UserUserID, 14),
487					p.Body,
488					StyleDim.Render(ts),
489				)
490				if i == m.pushCursor {
491					line = StyleHighlight.Render(line)
492				}
493				b.WriteString(line + "\n")
494			}
495		}
496	
497		if m.pushing {
498			b.WriteString("\n  ")
499			b.WriteString(renderPushKind(m.pushKind) + " ")
500			b.WriteString(m.pushIn.View())
501			b.WriteString("\n")
502			if m.err != "" {
503				b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err) + "\n")
504			}
505			b.WriteString("  " + StyleHelp.Render("Enter send · Esc cancel"))
506		} else if m.pendingDelete {
507			prompt := "確定刪除這篇文章? (y/N)"
508			if m.pushCursor >= 0 && m.pushCursor < len(m.pushes) {
509				prompt = fmt.Sprintf("確定刪除推文 #%d? (y/N)", m.pushCursor+1)
510			}
511			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+prompt))
512		} else {
513			help := "j/k scroll · y 匯出"
514			if m.canPush() {
515				help += " · + 推 · - 噓 · = →"
516			}
517			if len(m.pushes) > 0 {
518				help += " · p/P 選推文"
519			}
520			if m.canEditArticle() {
521				help += " · E 編輯"
522			}
523			if m.pushCursor >= 0 && m.canDeletePush(m.pushCursor) {
524				help += " · D 刪除推文"
525			} else if m.canDeleteArticle() {
526				help += " · D 刪除文章"
527			}
528			help += " · Esc/← back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
529			if m.err != "" {
530				b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err))
531			}
532			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render(help))
533		}
534		b.WriteString("\n")
535		return b.String()
536	}
537	
538	func renderPushKind(k store.PushKind) string {
539		switch k {
540		case store.PushKindPush:
541			return StylePushKind.Render("推")
542		case store.PushKindBoo:
543			return StyleBooKind.Render("噓")
544		case store.PushKindArrow:
545			return StyleArrowKind.Render("→")
546		}
547		return "?"
548	}
549
```

## Board Banner Feature — Architecture Tour

### 1. **Board View Model & Insertion Point** (`screen_board_view.go`)

**Model struct** (lines 13–21):
```go

    deps     Deps
    board    *store.Board
    articles []*store.Article

    width    int   // ← current viewport width
    height   int   // ← current viewport height

}
```

**View() function** (lines 88–161) builds output in this sequence:
1. **Line 90–95**: Blank line, then **header block** using `StyleHeader`:
   - `" 看板 {board.Name} · {board.Title} "`
   - This is the precedent for "top bar" styling—match this with `.Render()` for consistency
2. **Line 96**: Blank line (breathing room)
3. **Line 98–101**: Error display (if loadErr exists)
4. **Line 102–112**: Empty-articles hint (if no articles)
5. **Line 114–128**: **Column header** for the article list (dimmed style):
   ```
   #  Date  Sc.  Author  Title
   ```
6. **Line 130–152**: Article rows (with cursor highlight at line 147–151)
7. **Line 154–159**: Help footer + blank line

**Insertion point for banner**: Between lines 96 and 98 — right after the board header and before any content. You'd insert the banner block there, consuming some of the height budget (calculate remaining lines for articles).

---

### 2. **Width Helpers** (`runewidth.go`)

**Exported functions** (signatures at lines 13–54):

| Function | Signature | ANSI-Aware? |
|----------|-----------|------------|
| `PadRight` | `PadRight(s string, w int) string` | ❌ NO |
| `PadLeft` | `PadLeft(s string, w int) string` | ❌ NO |
| `Truncate` | `Truncate(s string, w int) string` | ❌ NO |
| `Width` | `Width(s string) int` | ❌ NO |

**Critical finding**: All four helpers delegate to `github.com/mattn/go-runewidth` (line 6 import). `runewidth.StringWidth()` and `runewidth.Truncate()` **do NOT handle ANSI escape sequences** — they count CSI bytes as display width.

**Consequence for banners**: If you render an ANSI-colored banner (e.g., `\x1b[31mRED TEXT\x1b[0m`), these helpers will overcount width. **You must strip ANSI before measuring or padding.** Recommend adding a helper like `stripANSI(s string) string` using a regex `\x1b\[[^m]*m` before calling `Width()` / `PadRight()` / `Truncate()`.

---

### 3. **Window-Resize Handling**

**Root.Update()** (`root.go`, lines 61–102):
- Receives `tea.WindowSizeMsg` (line 63)
- Sets `m.width` and `m.height` (line 64–65)
- **Forwards to active sub-model** (line 67): `m.sub.Update(msg)`

**Board view receives resize** (`screen_board_view.go`, line 37–39):
```go
case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
    m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
    return m, nil
```

**Fields to use for banner truncation**:
- `m.width` — total terminal width
- `m.height` — total terminal height
- **Article list calculation** (line 120): `titleW := max(20, m.width-2-idxW-1-dateW-1-scoreW-1-authorW-2)` — shows how width budget is carved up

---

**Import** (line 10):
```go
"github.com/charmbracelet/glamour"
```

**Function `renderBody()`** (lines 51–81):
```go
func (m *articleViewModel) renderBody() {
    width := m.width - 4
    if width < 40 {
        width = glamourFallbackWidth // = 80
    }
    r, err := glamour.NewTermRenderer(
        glamour.WithStandardStyle("dark"),
        glamour.WithWordWrap(width),
    )
    // ...
    out, err := r.Render(m.article.Body)  // ← glamour runs here
    m.rendered = strings.Trim(out, "\n")
    m.renderedWidth = width
}
```

Called on:
- Init (line 99)
- `tea.WindowSizeMsg` if width changes (lines 149–156)
- `ArticleUpdatedMsg` (line 178)

**For banners**: Glamour would apply markdown styling (bold, italics, code blocks, lists, etc.) with syntax highlighting. If you want a markdown banner, run it through `glamour.NewTermRenderer(glamour.WithStandardStyle("dark"), glamour.WithWordWrap(bannerWidth)).Render(bannerText)`. Otherwise, raw ANSI passthrough is simpler.

---

**For banners**, you have options:
- **Option A** (simple): Embed banner in `boardViewModel.View()` directly — no new screen needed.
- **Option B** (splash): Add `ScreenBoardSplash` to the enum, create `screen_board_splash.go`, and have it navigate to `ScreenBoardView` on Enter. (See precedent below.)

---

### 6. **Splash/Welcome Precedent**

**No existing splash in the codebase** — every screen goes straight to content. **Root initialization** (`root.go`, lines 44–54) prioritizes:
1. Register (if `IsRegister = true`)
2. Password change (if `MustChangePassword = true`)
3. Main menu (default)

**All three are direct screens**, not splash overlays. The **main menu** (`screen_main_menu.go`, lines 82–125) is the closest precedent for a "welcome" feel:

**If you want a splash** (e.g., "Welcome! Press Enter to view the board banner"), you'd create:
1. Add `ScreenBoardSplash` to `messages.go`
2. Add case in `root.go` navigate() to instantiate it

```text
1	# CLAUDE.md
2	
3	This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
4	
5	## What this is
6	
7	A simplified PTT-style BBS (批踢踢 clone) served over SSH. Built with
8	`charmbracelet/wish` (SSH framework) + `bubbletea` (TUI), persisted in
9	SQLite via the pure-Go `modernc.org/sqlite` driver. **Vim-style navigation
10	is a deliberate product decision** — `h`/`j`/`k`/`l` work alongside arrow
11	keys and Esc/Enter on every list/menu screen. Form screens (register,
12	post compose, water-balloon compose) intentionally do *not* bind h/l so
13	they remain usable as text-edit keys.
14	
15	For the why-we-do-it-this-way notes mapping back to pttbbs internals, see
16	`docs/ptt_trace_code/`. For the testing strategy, see `docs/testing.md`.
17	
18	## Common commands
19	
20	```bash
21	make hostkey            # generate .ssh/host_ed25519 (run once before first run)
22	make run                # boots :2222, creates data/bbs.db with seed boards
23	make build              # produces ./sshbbs
24	make test               # plain go test
25	make test-race          # what we treat as the CI standard
26	make cover              # per-package coverage
27	make db-reset           # rm data/bbs.db*
28	
29	# focused testing
30	go test -run TestPushes_ConcurrentScoreAtomicity -race -v ./internal/store/...
31	go test -race ./internal/chat/...
32	
33	# manual two-user smoke
34	ssh new@localhost -p 2222     # password ignored — fills the in-TUI register form
35	ssh alice@localhost -p 2222   # use the password set during register
36	```
37	
38	If `make hostkey` is re-run, clients see `Host key verification failed`.
39	Fix: `ssh-keygen -R '[localhost]:2222'`.
40	
41	## Architecture — the non-obvious parts
42	
43	### SSH-user-as-login, with `new` as the register sentinel
44	`auth.ReservedUsernameNew = "new"`. The password-auth callback in
45	`internal/server/auth_middleware.go` accepts any password for SSH user
46	`new` and stashes a "register mode" flag in `ssh.Context`; the TUI
47	handler reads it and routes straight to the register screen. For any
48	other SSH username, `auth.VerifyLogin` runs bcrypt against the DB and
49	stashes the `user_id` (int64) in the context for `makeProgramHandler`
50	to load.
51	
52	This means `auth.Register` rejects the literal username `new` — that
53	constant is referenced in two places (auth + middleware) and they must
54	not drift. `auth.go` defines it once.
55	
56	### Wish uses `MiddlewareWithProgramHandler`, not `Middleware`
57	We pick the *ProgramHandler* variant deliberately
58	(`internal/server/server.go`) because we need the `*tea.Program` pointer
59	to register the session with `chat.Broker` and to `program.Send(tea.Quit())`
60	during graceful shutdown. The simpler `Middleware(handler)` form constructs
61	the program internally and hides the pointer — you can't broadcast
62	into it. Don't switch back.
63	
64	### The chat broker takes a `Sender` interface, not `*tea.Program`
65	`chat.Sender` is `interface { Send(tea.Msg) }`. `*tea.Program` satisfies
66	it naturally. The interface exists purely so unit tests can substitute
67	a recording fake (`fakeSender` in `broker_test.go`) without spinning up
68	a real bubbletea program. If you find yourself wanting to mock the
69	broker, you probably want to add a method here, not refactor.
70	
71	### Live broadcasts are "send to everyone, filter on receive"
72	For 推文 (push) updates, `screen_article_view.updatePushInput` calls
73	`broker.SendToAll(senderUID, PushAddedMsg{ArticleID, ...})`. Every
74	connected session receives the message; each article-view model
75	ignores it unless `msg.ArticleID == m.article.ID`. This is simpler than
76	tracking who's viewing what and good enough for our scale. When the
77	filter matches, we **re-fetch from DB** rather than trust the broker
78	payload — that way timestamps and `recommend_score` are always canonical.
79	
80	Water balloons (`broker.Send(toUID, ...)`) target a single user; the
81	multi-session "雙開" case fans out to every live `Session` of that user.
82	
83	### `Store.writeMu` is process-level, not row-level
84	`internal/store/store.go` exposes a single `sync.Mutex` that's grabbed
85	inside every multi-statement repo method (e.g. `PushRepo.Create` runs
86	`INSERT INTO pushes` and `UPDATE articles SET recommend_score` in one
87	transaction under the mutex). This keeps the cached score atomic from
88	the application's perspective and avoids `SQLITE_BUSY` flapping with
89	the pure-Go driver. Don't use `sql.Tx` ad-hoc inside repo methods
90	without also taking `writeMu`. The `pushes` test
91	(`TestPushes_ConcurrentScoreAtomicity`, 50 concurrent inserts) is the
92	canary — if it ever fails under `-race`, the mutex discipline broke.
93	
94	### Migrations live under the store package, not the repo root
95	`internal/store/migrations/*.sql` is embedded with `go:embed
96	migrations/*.sql` from `internal/store/migrate.go`. `go:embed` can't
97	ascend with `..`, so the SQL files live next to the Go code that loads
98	them. Filename format `NNNN_name.sql`; `migrate.apply()` parses the
99	prefix as the version and tracks applied versions in the
100	`schema_migrations` table.
101	
102	### Data deliberately diverges from pttbbs in three places
103	1. **No recno-based article addressing** — `articles.id` (auto-increment)
104	   is opaque and never re-used. Bookmarks stay valid forever.
105	2. **bcrypt cost-12, not `crypt(3)` DES** — `auth.bcryptCost`.
106	3. **No binary shared memory / `userinfo_t` arrays** — presence is a
107	   `sync.RWMutex`-guarded `map[int64][]*Session` in `chat.Broker`.
108	
109	The full mapping (userec_t / boardheader_t / fileheader_t / `.DIR` /
110	推文 / 水球 / userinfo_t) is in `docs/ptt_trace_code/01..06_*.md`.
111	
112	## Testing layers (see `docs/testing.md` for the full doc)
113	
114	- **Layer 1**: plain `go test` for `auth` / `store` / `chat`. Most coverage lives here.
115	- **Layer 2**: direct `tea.Model.Update()` tests in `internal/tui/`. Use
116	  `package tui` (internal), not `tui_test`, to reach unexported screen
117	  constructors. Keybinding-parity tests are table-driven over
118	  `[]string{"esc","backspace","left","h"}` etc. — when adding new keys,
119	  extend the table.
120	- **No `teatest` dependency.** It's experimental (`x/exp`); we test
121	  `Update()` directly with `tea.KeyMsg{...}` synthetic input.
122	- **Always run `-race`.** Several tests (`TestPushes_ConcurrentScoreAtomicity`,
123	  `TestBroker_Concurrency`) exist specifically as race-detector bait.
124	- **Any test needing a DB** uses `storetest.New(t)` which gives a
125	  fresh `t.TempDir()`-backed SQLite. Never share stores across tests.
126	
127	## Conventions worth preserving
128	
129	- **UTF-8 throughout, no Big5.** Use `internal/tui/runewidth.go` helpers
130	  (`PadRight`, `PadLeft`, `Truncate`, `Width`) for any list rendering
131	  that mixes ASCII and CJK — `lipgloss`'s automatic width is unreliable
132	  for double-width glyphs.
133	- **Form screens skip h/l navigation** so the keys remain available for
134	  text editing. Currently: `screen_register`, `screen_post_compose`,
135	  `screen_wb` (compose half).
136	- **NavigateMsg is the only legitimate way to swap screens.** Sub-models
137	  emit it from `Update`; `Root.navigate` is the single switch statement
138	  that constructs the new sub. When you add a new screen, add the case
139	  there and a `Screen*` constant in `messages.go`.
140	- **Window-resize forwarding**: `Root.Update` always forwards
141	  `tea.WindowSizeMsg` to the active sub before its own logic. When
142	  switching to a new sub, `Root.navigate` resends the last known size
143	  so the freshly-mounted screen lays out correctly on first render.
144	
145	<!-- project-knowledge-harness:agent-guidance -->
146	<!-- Snippet for the project's agent contract file (AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md /
147	     similar). The bundled scripts/init.sh appends this between sentinel
148	     markers; safe to re-run. -->
149	
150	### Long-term backlog → `TODO.md` + `backlog/`
151	
152	When the user surfaces an idea explicitly **not** being implemented this
153	session (signals: "maybe later", "nice to have", "if I'm interested",
154	"工程量太大需要再評估", "先記下來"), add an entry to [`TODO.md`](TODO.md) using
155	the priority + effort tag schema. Do **not** create new `ROADMAP.md` /
156	`IDEAS.md` / `BACKLOG.md` files — `TODO.md` is the single index.
157	
158	The bundled `scripts/todo-kanban.sh` validates the format. Run it
159	(`scripts/todo-kanban.sh --validate-only TODO.md`) after editing so syntax
160	drift is caught immediately.
161	
162	#### Three ways to add a TODO entry (preferred order)
163	
164	1. **Structured CLI — `scripts/add-todo.sh`** (default):
165	
166	   ```
167	   scripts/add-todo.sh --priority P3 --effort M \
168	     --title "Title" --description "Description"
169	   ```
170	
171	   Inserts a canonically-formatted line into the right `## P*` lane and
172	   re-runs the validator. Add `--backlog` to also scaffold
173	   `backlog/<slug>.md` from the bundled template.
174	
175	2. **Quick capture — `backlog/inbox.md`** (when priority/effort unclear):
176	
177	   ```
178	   echo "- maybe add docs versioning with mike" >> backlog/inbox.md
179	   ```
180	
181	   When the user asks "sweep the inbox", run
182	   `scripts/sweep-inbox.sh`. It prompts for the missing fields per loose
183	   line and calls `add-todo.sh`. Use `--batch` for non-interactive runs
184	   that only formalize lines with parseable `key=value` pairs.
185	
186	3. **Direct edit of `TODO.md`** — fine if the format is fresh; run
187	   `scripts/todo-kanban.sh --validate-only` afterwards.
188	
189	Add a `backlog/<slug>.md` companion doc when the item meets any of:
190	
191	- carries a `P?` tag (record what was tried so it doesn't need re-investigation)
192	- captures a paused troubleshooting session that you intend to fix later
193	  (preserve the error trace + root cause analysis before context evaporates)
194	- weighs multiple options (record trade-offs, not only the winner)
195	- is `[L]` or `[XL]` (architectural; needs design before code)
196	
197	`[S]` items rarely need a backlog doc — a file path in the `TODO.md` line is
198	usually enough. See [`backlog/README.md`](backlog/README.md) for the full
199	template and "when to add a doc" rules.
200	
201	When implementing a `TODO.md` item, in the same commit:
202	
203	1. Run `scripts/promote-todo.sh --title "<substring>" --summary "<what shipped>"`
204	   to move the entry into `## Done` with the dated syntax and re-validate.
205	2. Mark the corresponding `backlog/<slug>.md` (if any) `Status: shipped`
206	   and keep it as a historical record (don't delete — future-you may
207	   revisit adjacent decisions).
208	
209	`backlog/` is excluded from N/A (no packaging — these files stay in the repo) (see N/A); it
210	is repo metadata for maintainers, not user-facing config to deploy.
211	
212	### Past pitfalls → `pitfalls/`
213	
214	When you spend more than ~15 minutes debugging something that wasn't
215	googleable and the fix is non-obvious, write a `pitfalls/<slug>.md`
216	capturing:
217	
218	1. **Verbatim symptom** — copy-paste error messages exactly, do not
219	   paraphrase (preserves grep-ability for future-you / future agent)
220	2. **Root cause** — why this happens (with source / docs / upstream issue link)
221	3. **Workaround** — copy-pasteable commands or config diff
222	4. **Prevention** — how to avoid stepping on this again
223	
224	Title the doc by the **symptom**, not the root cause (you'll search by what
225	you're seeing, not by what you eventually learned). See
226	[`pitfalls/README.md`](pitfalls/README.md) for the full template and
227	when-to-add rules.
228	
229	**Pitfall vs Hard invariant**: a pitfall *graduates* to a Hard invariant in
230	this file when it (a) recurs across machines/agents/sessions despite being
231	documented, (b) silently corrupts state, or (c) the workaround is non-obvious
232	enough that "remember to do X" isn't safe. When graduating, leave the
233	`pitfalls/<slug>.md` as historical record and link to it from the new
234	invariant.
235	
236	`pitfalls/` is excluded from N/A (no packaging — these files stay in the repo) (see N/A) and
237	**not** auto-redacted; review for secrets before committing.
238	<!-- project-knowledge-harness:agent-guidance --> (end)
239
```

1	-- Initial schema for the SSH BBS.
2	-- All tables use auto-increment INTEGER PRIMARY KEY plus a unique business
3	-- key where appropriate. We deliberately do NOT mimic pttbbs's recno-based
4	-- addressing (a holdover from mmap'd binary .DIR files) — a relational DB
5	-- removes the recno-tombstone fragility documented in pttbbs.
6	
7	PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;
8	
9	CREATE TABLE users (
10	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
11	    user_id         TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE COLLATE NOCASE, -- userec_t.userid
12	    password_hash   TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- bcrypt; replaces userec_t.passwd (DES)
13	    nickname        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- userec_t.nickname
14	    realname        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- userec_t.realname
15	    email           TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- userec_t.email
16	    num_logins      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- userec_t.numlogindays (loose)
17	    num_posts       INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- userec_t.numposts
18	    last_login_at   DATETIME,                            -- userec_t.lastlogin
19	    last_host       TEXT,                                -- userec_t.lasthost
20	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
21	);
22	
23	CREATE TABLE boards (
24	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
25	    name            TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE COLLATE NOCASE, -- boardheader_t.brdname
26	    title           TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- boardheader_t.title
27	    description     TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
28	    bm              TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- boardheader_t.BM (comma-sep)
29	    attr            INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- boardheader_t.brdattr (P1+)
30	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
31	);
32	
33	CREATE TABLE articles (
34	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
35	    board_id        INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES boards(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
36	    author_id       INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
37	    author_userid   TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- denormalized (fileheader_t.owner)
38	    title           TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- fileheader_t.title
39	    body            TEXT NOT NULL,
40	    recommend_score INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- cached sum of pushes (+1) - boos (-1)
41	    filemode        INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- fileheader_t.filemode (P1+)
42	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
43	);
44	CREATE INDEX idx_articles_board_created ON articles(board_id, created_at DESC);
45	
46	CREATE TABLE pushes (
47	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
48	    article_id      INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES articles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
49	    user_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
50	    user_userid     TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- denormalized
51	    kind            TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (kind IN ('push','boo','arrow')), -- 推/噓/→
52	    body            TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
53	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
54	);
55	CREATE INDEX idx_pushes_article ON pushes(article_id, id);
56	
57	CREATE TABLE water_balloons (
58	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
59	    from_user_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
60	    from_userid     TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- denormalized
61	    to_user_id      INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
62	    body            TEXT NOT NULL,
63	    delivered_live  INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
64	    read_at         DATETIME,
65	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
66	);
67	CREATE INDEX idx_wb_to_unread ON water_balloons(to_user_id, read_at);
68
```

---

## Summary Report: Permission System, Article-Edit Pattern & Form Navigation

### 1. **Permission System Layout**

**Type & Definition:**
- **Role type**: String enum (`type Role string`), defined in `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/role.go:1-38`
- Four roles: `RoleGuest` ("guest"), `RoleUser` ("user"), `RoleMod` ("mod"), `RoleAdmin` ("admin")
- Stored as TEXT column with CHECK constraint; ordered by rank (0→1→2→3)

**Canonical "is mod or admin" check:**
```
Role.AtLeast(min Role) bool
/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/role.go:25-32
```
Example usage in delete/edit gates: `requesterRole.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)` (see `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/articles.go:131, 157`)

**Board-mod binding:**
- **NOT per-board**. `BM` field in Board struct (`/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/boards.go:15`) is a comma-separated string (pttbbs heritage, line 28 in migration), but the current codebase **does not use it** for permission checks
- Mod/admin are **global roles** in the `users.role` column
- The `BM` field is a column name but currently unused in TUI permission gates

---

### 2. **Article-Edit Screen Structure**

**File**: `/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/tui/screen_article_edit.go`

**(a) Model struct fields** (lines 23–34):
```go
type articleEditModel struct {
    deps      Deps                      // Store, User, Broker
    articleID int64
    boardID   int64
    title     textinput.Model           // bubbles textarea input
    body      textarea.Model            // bubbles textarea for multi-line
    focus     int                       // editFocusTitle (0) or editFocusBody (1)
    width, height, err string           // layout + error messaging
    loadErr   error                     // early permission denial
}
```

**(b) Init/Update/View skeleton:**
- **Init** (line 73): Returns `textinput.Blink` command
- **Update** (lines 75–118):
  - WindowSizeMsg: resize body textarea width/height
  - KeyMsg: `esc` → cancel, `tab` → toggle focus, `ctrl+s` → submit, `enter` (in title) → advance to body
  - Delegates key input to active field's Update (title or body)
  - Early return if `loadErr` set (permission denied at construction)
- **View** (lines 174–199): Renders title input, body textarea, help text, errors

**(c) Submission & Navigation:**
- **Submit** (lines 145–172): Calls `m.deps.Store.Articles().Update(ctx, articleID, requesterID, requesterRole, title, body)`
- **Cancel** (lines 132–143): Returns `NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ...}` or fallback to board list if load failed
- Both return NavigateMsg from a closure (tea.Cmd)

**(d) Text input:**
- Uses **`textarea.Model`** from bubbles (line 8 import, line 28 field)
- **Multi-line support**: full textarea with SetWidth/SetHeight (lines 42–46)
- h/l are **NOT bound** — comment at line 22 confirms this is intentional so textarea receives raw keypresses

---

### 3. **Form Screens That Skip h/l Navigation**

**Per CLAUDE.md** (lines 131–135):
> Form screens skip h/l navigation so the keys remain available for text editing. Currently: `screen_register`, `screen_post_compose`, `screen_wb` (compose half).

**How they implement it:**
- **No separate flag or opt-out mechanism** — they simply **don't bind h/l in their Update switches**
- Example: `screen_post_compose.go` (lines 54–77) handles `esc`, `tab`, `ctrl+s`, `ctrl+i` but has **no `case "h"` or `case "l"`**. Instead, all KeyMsg falls through to the focused field's Update (lines 80–91)
- Same pattern: `screen_register.go` (lines 67–86) binds `up`, `down`, `tab`, `shift+tab`, `enter`, `esc` but not `h`/`l`

**Convention:**
For your banner-edit screen, simply omit h/l from the main Update switch; let the textarea receive the key directly.

---

### 4. **Permission Gates in TUI**

**Pattern:**
- Define a predicate method on the screen model (e.g., `canEditArticle()`, `canDeleteArticle()`)
- Check it **before** rendering the help text AND before dispatching the action
- If check fails, set an error message instead of navigating

**Example from screen_article_view.go:**

```go
// Helper at lines 135–143:
func (m articleViewModel) canEditArticle() bool {
    if m.deps.User == nil || m.article == nil {
        return false
    }
    if m.deps.User.ID == m.article.AuthorID {
        return true  // author can always edit
    }
    return m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)  // or mod+
}

// Gate in Update at lines 293–305:
case "E":
    if !m.canEditArticle() {
        m.err = "權限不足 (only the author or a mod can edit)"
        return m, nil  // stay on screen, show error
    }
    // ... navigate to edit screen
```

**In View, only show the key hint if allowed** (line 520–522):
```go
if m.canEditArticle() {
    help += " · E 編輯"
}
```

For board-banner edit, add a `canEditBoardBanner()` check in screen_board_view (mirroring `isGuest()` at line 163), then gate the `'b'` key and help text similarly.

---

### 5. **Article-Edit Persistence**

**Call location**: `screen_article_edit.go` line 158
```go
err := m.deps.Store.Articles().Update(ctx, m.articleID, u.ID, u.Role, title, body)
```

**Method signature** (`/Volumes/Data/Program/tries/2026-04-29-ssh-bbs/internal/store/articles.go:144–165`):
```go
func (r *ArticleRepo) Update(ctx context.Context, articleID, requesterID int64, 
    requesterRole Role, newTitle, newBody string) error
```
- Locks `writeMu`, fetches `author_id`, checks permission (author OR mod+)
- Returns `ErrPermissionDenied` or `ErrArticleNotFound` if checks fail
- Updates `title`, `body`, `updated_at` in a single SQL statement

**For your `BoardRepo.UpdateBanner`:**
Mirror this shape exactly:
```go
func (r *BoardRepo) UpdateBanner(ctx context.Context, boardID, requesterID int64, 
    requesterRole Role, newBanner string) error
```
- Lock, fetch the board (check exists)
- Permission check: admin only, or use BM field for per-board mods (if you decide to enable it)
- Single UPDATE statement setting `banner` column (you'll need to add this column)
- Return permission/not-found errors

---

### Key Takeaways for `screen_board_banner_edit`

1. **Mirror `articleEditModel`** structure: title field (if you want one) + `textarea.Model` for ANSI art
2. **Reuse permission pattern**: `canEditBoardBanner()` predicate, checked at construction time (line 59–65 in article_edit) to deny early
3. **Skip h/l binding** — just omit them from Update's switch, like `screen_post_compose` does
4. **Gate the 'B' key on board-view** using the same pattern as 'E' in article-view (lines 293–305)
5. **Call `BoardRepo.UpdateBanner(ctx, boardID, u.ID, u.Role, bannerText)`** on submit, broadcast `BoardUpdatedMsg` if changes succeed

1	// Package seed loads default content (currently: welcome / intro articles)
2	// from embedded markdown files into the database on first startup.
3	//
4	// The contract is intentionally weak: a board with any existing article is
5	// skipped entirely. That way an admin who edits the seeded welcome article
6	// does not see their changes overwritten on the next restart.
7	//
8	// To add more seed articles, drop another *.md file under internal/seed/articles/
9	// with frontmatter naming the target board:
10	//
11	//	---
12	//	title: ...
13	//	board: Welcome
14	//	---
15	//
16	//	body...
17	//
18	// `go:embed` will pick it up at the next build.
19	package seed
20	
21	import (
22		"context"
23		"embed"
24		"errors"
25		"fmt"
26		"io/fs"
27		"log"
28		"sort"
29		"strings"
30	
31		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/markdown"
32		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
33	)
34	
35	//go:embed articles/*.md
36	var articlesFS embed.FS
37	
38	// Articles parses every embedded *.md file and inserts it into the board
39	// named by its frontmatter, attributing all of them to adminUserID. A
40	// board that already has at least one article is left alone, regardless
41	// of how many seed files target it.
42	//
43	// Failure to parse or insert any single file logs and continues — a bad
44	// seed file should not block startup. Missing boards are also skipped
45	// with a log line.
46	func Articles(ctx context.Context, st *store.Store, adminUserID string) error {
47		admin, err := st.Users().GetByUserID(ctx, adminUserID)
48		if err != nil {
49			return fmt.Errorf("seed.Articles: lookup admin %q: %w", adminUserID, err)
50		}
51	
52		entries, err := fs.ReadDir(articlesFS, "articles")
53		if err != nil {
54			return fmt.Errorf("seed.Articles: read embedded fs: %w", err)
55		}
56		// Stable iteration order so logs are deterministic.
57		sort.Slice(entries, func(i, j int) bool { return entries[i].Name() < entries[j].Name() })
58	
59		// Cache "has-articles?" per board so several seed files targeting the
60		// same board only hit the DB once.
61		boardEmpty := map[int64]bool{}
62	
63		for _, e := range entries {
64			if e.IsDir() || !strings.HasSuffix(e.Name(), ".md") {
65				continue
66			}
67			body, err := articlesFS.ReadFile("articles/" + e.Name())
68			if err != nil {
69				log.Printf("seed.Articles: read %s: %v", e.Name(), err)
70				continue
71			}
72			parsed, err := markdown.Parse(string(body))
73			if err != nil {
74				log.Printf("seed.Articles: parse %s: %v", e.Name(), err)
75				continue
76			}
77			if parsed.BoardName == "" {
78				log.Printf("seed.Articles: %s has no `board:` frontmatter, skipping", e.Name())
79				continue
80			}
81			if parsed.Title == "" {
82				log.Printf("seed.Articles: %s has no `title:` frontmatter, skipping", e.Name())
83				continue
84			}
85	
86			board, err := st.Boards().GetByName(ctx, parsed.BoardName)
87			if err != nil {
88				if errors.Is(err, store.ErrBoardNotFound) {
89					log.Printf("seed.Articles: %s targets board %q which doesn't exist; skipping",
90						e.Name(), parsed.BoardName)
91					continue
92				}
93				return fmt.Errorf("seed.Articles: lookup board %q: %w", parsed.BoardName, err)
94			}
95	
96			empty, cached := boardEmpty[board.ID]
97			if !cached {
98				existing, err := st.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, board.ID, 1)
99				if err != nil {
100					return fmt.Errorf("seed.Articles: list %q: %w", parsed.BoardName, err)
101				}
102				empty = len(existing) == 0
103				boardEmpty[board.ID] = empty
104			}
105			if !empty {
106				continue
107			}
108	
109			if _, err := st.Articles().Create(ctx, board.ID, admin.ID, admin.UserID, parsed.Title, parsed.Body); err != nil {
110				log.Printf("seed.Articles: insert %s: %v", e.Name(), err)
111				continue
112			}
113			// Mark this board as no-longer-empty so subsequent seed files for
114			// the same board are skipped.
115			boardEmpty[board.ID] = false
116			log.Printf("seed.Articles: seeded %s into board %q", e.Name(), parsed.BoardName)
117		}
118		return nil
119	}
120

1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"time"
8	)
9	
10	type Board struct {
11		ID          int64
12		Name        string
13		Title       string
14		Description string
15		BM          string
16		Attr        int64
17		CreatedAt   time.Time
18	}
19	
20	type BoardRepo struct{ s *Store }
21	
22	var ErrBoardNotFound = errors.New("board not found")
23	
24	const boardColumns = `id, name, title, description, bm, attr, created_at`
25	
26	func scanBoard(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Board, error) {
27		b := &Board{}
28		err := row.Scan(&b.ID, &b.Name, &b.Title, &b.Description, &b.BM, &b.Attr, &b.CreatedAt)
29		return b, err
30	}
31	
32	func (r *BoardRepo) List(ctx context.Context) ([]*Board, error) {
33		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
34			`SELECT `+boardColumns+` FROM boards ORDER BY name COLLATE NOCASE`)
35		if err != nil {
36			return nil, err
37		}
38		defer rows.Close()
39		var out []*Board
40		for rows.Next() {
41			b, err := scanBoard(rows)
42			if err != nil {
43				return nil, err
44			}
45			out = append(out, b)
46		}
47		return out, rows.Err()
48	}
49	
50	func (r *BoardRepo) GetByID(ctx context.Context, id int64) (*Board, error) {
51		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+boardColumns+` FROM boards WHERE id = ?`, id)
52		b, err := scanBoard(row)
53		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
54			return nil, ErrBoardNotFound
55		}
56		return b, err
57	}
58	
59	func (r *BoardRepo) GetByName(ctx context.Context, name string) (*Board, error) {
60		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+boardColumns+` FROM boards WHERE name = ? COLLATE NOCASE`, name)
61		b, err := scanBoard(row)
62		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
63			return nil, ErrBoardNotFound
64		}
65		return b, err
66	}
67	
68	// SeedDefaults is a belt-and-braces complement to migration 0002. It runs
69	// every startup; INSERT OR IGNORE makes it a no-op once the rows exist.
70	func (r *BoardRepo) SeedDefaults(ctx context.Context) error {
71		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
72		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
73		_, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx, `
74			INSERT OR IGNORE INTO boards (name, title, description) VALUES
75				('Welcome',  '歡迎來到 SSH-BBS', 'System welcome and announcements'),
76				('Test',     '測試板',           'Try things here. Posts may be cleared periodically.'),
77				('ChitChat', '閒聊',             'General chat. Be kind.')
78		`)
79		return err
80	}

1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"strings"
5	
6		"github.com/mattn/go-runewidth"
7	)
8	
9	// PadRight pads s with spaces on the right so its display width equals w.
10	// If s is wider than w, it's truncated. Critical for table-like rendering
11	// with mixed ASCII + CJK strings — lipgloss's automatic padding miscounts
12	// double-width glyphs in some terminals.
13	func PadRight(s string, w int) string {
14		if w <= 0 {
15			return ""
16		}
17		cw := runewidth.StringWidth(s)
18		if cw == w {
19			return s
20		}
21		if cw > w {
22			return runewidth.Truncate(s, w, "")
23		}
24		return s + strings.Repeat(" ", w-cw)
25	}
26	
27	// PadLeft is PadRight's mirror, padding before the string.
28	func PadLeft(s string, w int) string {
29		if w <= 0 {
30			return ""
31		}
32		cw := runewidth.StringWidth(s)
33		if cw == w {
34			return s
35		}
36		if cw > w {
37			return runewidth.Truncate(s, w, "")
38		}
39		return strings.Repeat(" ", w-cw) + s
40	}
41	
42	// Truncate cuts s to display-width w, appending "…" if truncation occurred.
43	func Truncate(s string, w int) string {
44		if runewidth.StringWidth(s) <= w {
45			return s
46		}
47		if w <= 1 {
48			return runewidth.Truncate(s, w, "")
49		}
50		return runewidth.Truncate(s, w, "…")
51	}
52	
53	// Width returns the display width of s.
54	func Width(s string) int { return runewidth.StringWidth(s) }
55
```

1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"

8		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"

11	)
12	
13	type boardViewModel struct {
14		deps     Deps
15		board    *store.Board
16		articles []*store.Article
17		cursor   int
18		width    int
19		height   int
20		loadErr  error
21	}
22	
23	func newBoardViewModel(deps Deps, boardID int64) boardViewModel {
24		ctx := context.Background()
25		board, err := deps.Store.Boards().GetByID(ctx, boardID)
26		if err != nil {
27			return boardViewModel{deps: deps, loadErr: err}
28		}
29		articles, err := deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, boardID, 100)
30		return boardViewModel{deps: deps, board: board, articles: articles, loadErr: err}
31	}
32	
33	func (m boardViewModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
34	
35	func (m boardViewModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
36		switch msg := msg.(type) {
37		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
38			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
39			return m, nil
40		case ArticleAddedMsg:
41			if m.board != nil && msg.BoardID == m.board.ID {
42				ctx := context.Background()
43				if arts, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, m.board.ID, 100); err == nil {
44					m.articles = arts
45				}
46			}
47			return m, nil
48		case tea.KeyMsg:
49			switch msg.String() {
50			case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
51				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardList} }
52			case "Q":
53				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
54			case "up", "k", "[":
55				if m.cursor > 0 {
56					m.cursor--
57				}
58			case "down", "j", "]":
59				if m.cursor < len(m.articles)-1 {
60					m.cursor++
61				}
62			case "home", "g":
63				m.cursor = 0
64			case "end", "G":
65				if len(m.articles) > 0 {
66					m.cursor = len(m.articles) - 1
67				}
68			case "enter", " ", "right", "l":
69				if len(m.articles) == 0 {
70					return m, nil
71				}
72				a := m.articles[m.cursor]
73				return m, func() tea.Msg {
74					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ArticleID: a.ID, BoardID: a.BoardID}
75				}
76			case "p":
77				if m.board == nil {
78					return m, nil
79				}
80				return m, func() tea.Msg {
81					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenPostCompose, BoardID: m.board.ID}
82				}
83			}
84		}
85		return m, nil
86	}
87	
88	func (m boardViewModel) View() string {
89		var b strings.Builder
90		b.WriteString("\n")
91		if m.board != nil {
92			b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" 看板 %s · %s ", m.board.Name, m.board.Title)))
93		} else {
94			b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 看板 "))
95		}
96		b.WriteString("\n\n")
97	
98		if m.loadErr != nil {
99			b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n")
100			return b.String()
101		}
102		if len(m.articles) == 0 {
103			hint := "(no articles yet — press p to write one)"
104			help := "p post · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
105			if m.isGuest() {
106				hint = "(no articles yet)"
107				help = "Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
108			}
109			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(hint) + "\n")
110			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render(help))
111			return b.String()
112		}
113	
114		const (
115			idxW    = 4
116			dateW   = 11
117			scoreW  = 4
118			authorW = 14
119		)
120		titleW := max(20, m.width-2-idxW-1-dateW-1-scoreW-1-authorW-2)
121		header := fmt.Sprintf(" %s  %s  %s  %s  %s",
122			PadRight("#", idxW),
123			PadRight("Date", dateW),
124			PadRight("Sc.", scoreW),
125			PadRight("Author", authorW),
126			PadRight("Title", titleW),
127		)
128		b.WriteString(StyleDim.Render(header) + "\n")
129	
130		for i, a := range m.articles {
131			score := ""
132			switch {
133			case a.RecommendScore >= 100:
134				score = "爆"
135			case a.RecommendScore > 0:
136				score = fmt.Sprintf("%d", a.RecommendScore)
137			case a.RecommendScore < 0:
138				score = "X" + fmt.Sprintf("%d", -a.RecommendScore)
139			}
140			row := fmt.Sprintf(" %s  %s  %s  %s  %s",
141				PadRight(fmt.Sprintf("%d", i+1), idxW),
142				PadRight(a.CreatedAt.Format("01/02 15:04"), dateW),
143				PadRight(score, scoreW),
144				PadRight(a.AuthorUserID, authorW),
145				Truncate(a.Title, titleW),
146			)
147			if i == m.cursor {
148				b.WriteString(StyleHighlight.Render("▸"+row[1:]) + "\n")
149			} else {
150				b.WriteString(" " + row + "\n")
151			}
152		}
153	
154		help := "↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l open · p post · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
155		if m.isGuest() {
156			help = "↑/↓ j/k move · Enter/→/l open · Esc/←/h back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
157		}
158		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render(help))
159		b.WriteString("\n")
160		return b.String()
161	}
162	
163	func (m boardViewModel) isGuest() bool {
164		return m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role == store.RoleGuest
165	}

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="AskUserQuestion"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **AskUserQuestion**</summary>
**Banner 預設要怎麼出現在使用者面前？(三種都會把 banner 存在 boards.banner，差別只在 render 時機)**

Options:
- **永久 inline + 按 b 召喚 splash (Recommended)** - 進板就在文章列表上方看到 banner（截至 8 行），任何時候按 b 進全螢幕 splash 看完整版。不需要 session 級狀態，最簡單也最 PTT-like。
- **首次進板 splash + 之後 inline** - 每個 SSH session 第一次進該板會看到全螢幕 banner（按 Enter 進列表）；同 session 再進就只看到 inline header。需在 chat.Session 加 seenBanners map[boardID]bool。
- **永遠 splash on entry** - 每次進板都先看 splash，按 Enter 才進列表；列表完全沒有 banner。最單純的 render 路徑（不用碰 runewidth.go），但每次進板都被擋一下。

**Banner 內容格式要支援什麼？**

Options:
- **純 ANSI/ASCII passthrough (Recommended)** - 原文照印，支援 figlet / lolcat / 手刻 ANSI 色碼。最符合 PTT 文化，render 路徑單純。代價：要在 runewidth.go 加 stripANSI helper（給 width / pad / truncate 用）。先不過 glamour。
- **Frontmatter 切換：ANSI 或 Markdown** - banner 開頭可放 --- mode: markdown --- 走 glamour；不寫就當 ANSI passthrough。雙 render 路徑，多一個分支但日後彈性大。Markdown banner 不能含 ANSI 色碼（glamour 會吃掉）。
- **Markdown only (glamour)** - 全部過 glamour。優點：可重用文章 render 路徑。缺點：失去 PTT 風 ASCII art (色碼會被 glamour 吃掉或 escape 顯示)，違背 ASCII art 的初衷。


**Answer:** 永久 inline + 按 b 召喚 splash (Recommended)" selected preview:
進板畫面 (inline header)：

 看板 Welcome · 歡迎來到 SSH-BBS

  ░██████╗░██████╗██╗░░██╗
  ╚════██╗██╔════╝██║░░██║
  ░░███╔═╝╚█████╗░███████║
  ░██╔══╝░░╚═══██╗██╔══██║
  ░███████╗██████╔╝██║░░██║
  ╚══════╝╚═════╝░╚═╝░░╚═╝

  #   Date         Sc.   Author       Title
▸ 1   05/06 12:34        admin        Welcome
  2   05/06 11:02        alice        Test post

↑/↓ k/j · Enter open · b banner · p post · Esc back

按 b → 全螢幕 splash 看完整 banner，Enter/Esc 返回, 純 ANSI/ASCII passthrough (Recommended)" selected preview:
範例 banner.txt 檔內容（直接寫入 boards.banner）：

\x1b[31m░██████╗░██████╗██╗░░██╗\x1b[0m
\x1b[33m╚════██╗██╔════╝██║░░██║\x1b[0m
\x1b[32m░░███╔═╝╚█████╗░███████║\x1b[0m
\x1b[36m░██╔══╝░░╚═══██╗██╔══██║\x1b[0m
\x1b[34m░███████╗██████╔╝██║░░██║\x1b[0m
\x1b[35m╚══════╝╚═════╝░╚═╝░░╚═╝\x1b[0m

render 時：原樣寫入終端，色碼直接生效
寬度計算：先 stripANSI 再 runewidth.StringWidth

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
