```md
# Per-Board ANSI/ASCII-Art Banner

## Context

Board screens currently jump straight from a single-line `StyleHeader` ("看板 X · 標題") into the article list — there's no place to express a board's identity, no PTT-style splash, no signature ASCII art. We want each board to carry an editable banner that:

1. Renders **inline** above the article list (PTT-like, always visible while browsing the board).
2. Can be expanded into a **full-screen splash** on demand (when capped inline doesn't show the full piece).
3. Is **seeded once per board** from `internal/seed/banners/<Name>.txt`, with a shared `_default.txt` fallback for boards that don't ship a per-board file.
4. Is **editable by mods+** through a TUI form, mirroring the existing article-edit screen.

UX decisions (locked via AskUserQuestion in this session):

- **Mode**: permanent inline header (capped 8 lines) + summonable full splash via `b`. No auto-splash on entry — that needs per-session "seen" state we don't otherwise need yet.
- **Format**: raw ANSI/ASCII passthrough only. No `glamour` for banners — PTT culture is colored ASCII art (figlet/lolcat output, manual `\x1b[…m` codes), and glamour mangles raw ANSI. Future markdown-banner mode can ship later via a frontmatter switch if desired.

Non-goals this round: per-board mod binding (the `boards.bm` column stays unused; mod+ is the global role check, matching `ArticleRepo.Update`); markdown rendering through glamour; splash-on-entry session tracking; banner versioning / history.

## Architecture summary

| Layer | Change |
|---|---|
| Schema | New column `boards.banner TEXT` via migration `0006_boards_banner.sql` |
| Repo | `BoardRepo.UpdateBanner(ctx, boardID, requesterID, requesterRole, banner)` mirroring `ArticleRepo.Update` shape (`internal/store/articles.go:144-165`) |
| Seed | New `internal/seed/banners.go` + `internal/seed/banners/*.txt` (embed). Idempotency = `banner IS NULL` per board (different from articles' "board has zero rows" rule because banner is one field, not many rows) |
| Width helpers | New `stripANSI` / `bannerVisualWidth` / `bannerTruncate` in `internal/tui/runewidth.go` — leave existing `Width/Truncate/PadRight/PadLeft` untouched (they're load-bearing for the article-list table; banner is the only ANSI-bearing render path) |
| Inline render | `screen_board_view.View()` gains a banner block between current header (line 96) and error/list (line 98); cap at `MaxInlineBannerLines = 8` with "(b 看完整)" tail when over |
| Splash screen | New `screen_board_splash.go` (`ScreenBoardSplash`) — full-render banner, any key returns to `ScreenBoardView` |
| Edit screen | New `screen_board_banner_edit.go` (`ScreenBoardBannerEdit`) — single `textarea.Model`, ctrl+s submits, esc cancels, no h/l binding. Permission gated to `RoleMod`+ at construction (early `loadErr`) |
| Keys on board view | `b` → splash (everyone); `B` → edit (mod+ only, hint hidden otherwise) |
| Wiring | Two new `Screen*` constants in `messages.go`; two new cases in `root.go` navigate switch |

## Files to create

- `internal/store/migrations/0006_boards_banner.sql` — `ALTER TABLE boards ADD COLUMN banner TEXT;`
- `internal/seed/banners.go` — `//go:embed banners/*.txt` + `Banners(ctx, st)` function
- `internal/seed/banners/Welcome.txt`, `Test.txt`, `ChitChat.txt`, `_default.txt` — small ANSI placeholders (figlet-style "Welcome" / "Test" / "ChitChat" + a generic "BBS" fallback). Keep ≤ 6 lines so they fit inline cap with breathing room.
- `internal/tui/screen_board_splash.go`
- `internal/tui/screen_board_banner_edit.go`
- Tests:
  - `internal/store/boards_banner_test.go` — `UpdateBanner` permission/notfound/success
  - `internal/seed/banners_test.go` — seed-on-NULL + skip-on-set + `_default.txt` fallback
  - `internal/tui/runewidth_ansi_test.go` — `stripANSI`, `bannerVisualWidth`, `bannerTruncate` (preserves CSI across cuts, doesn't drop trailing reset)
  - `internal/tui/screen_board_view_test.go` *(if not present)* — banner block appears, `b` emits splash navigate, `B` gated by role
  - `internal/tui/screen_board_banner_edit_test.go` — guest/user denied at construction; mod can save; ctrl+s persists

## Files to modify

- `internal/store/boards.go`:
  - Add `Banner string` field to `Board` struct (line 10-18)
  - Add `, banner` to `boardColumns` (line 24)
  - Add `&b.Banner` to `scanBoard` (line 28)
  - New `UpdateBanner(ctx, boardID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role, banner string) error` — pattern from `ArticleRepo.Update` (`internal/store/articles.go:144-165`): take `writeMu`, return `ErrPermissionDenied` if not `requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod)`, return `ErrBoardNotFound` if missing, single `UPDATE boards SET banner = ? WHERE id = ?`. Reuse existing `ErrPermissionDenied` if exported, else mirror locally.
- `internal/tui/runewidth.go`:
  - Add `stripANSI(s string) string` — regex `\x1b\[[0-9;?]*[a-zA-Z]` (matches CSI escapes; covers SGR colors and the rare cursor codes a banner author might paste)
  - Add `bannerVisualWidth(s string) int` = `runewidth.StringWidth(stripANSI(s))`
  - Add `bannerTruncate(s string, w int) string` — walks `s`, copying CSI runs verbatim (zero width) and stopping body chars when visual width hits `w`; appends a trailing `\x1b[0m` if the input contained any CSI (defensive — author may have forgotten the reset). On overflow, append `…` after the visible width budget.
  - Don't touch the existing four helpers' behavior — they're naive and that's fine for the article table.
- `internal/tui/screen_board_view.go`:
  - Add `func (m boardViewModel) renderBanner(width int) string` returning rendered block (or `""` if no banner). Splits on `\n`, applies `bannerTruncate(line, width-2)` per line, caps at 8 lines, appends `"  " + StyleDim.Render("…(b 看完整)")` line when truncated by line count.
  - Insert call into `View()` after line 96 (`b.WriteString("\n\n")`) and before line 98 (loadErr block). Output a trailing `"\n"` when banner is non-empty so the article header has breathing room.
  - Add to `Update()` KeyMsg switch:

## Idempotent seed flow (mirrors `seed.Articles`)

`internal/seed/banners.go` outline:

\```
//go:embed banners/*.txt
var bannersFS embed.FS

func Banners(ctx, st) error {
    boards, _ := st.Boards().List(ctx)         // existing method
    defaultBytes, hasDefault := readEmbed("banners/_default.txt")
    for _, board := range boards {
        if board.Banner != "" { continue }     // admin-edited or already seeded
        bytes, ok := readEmbed("banners/" + board.Name + ".txt")
        if !ok {
            if !hasDefault { continue }
            bytes = defaultBytes
        }
        st.Boards().UpdateBanner(ctx, board.ID, adminID, RoleAdmin, string(bytes))
    }
}
\```

The contract intentionally differs from `seed.Articles`: that one keys on "board has any article" (because articles are a row collection); this one keys on "the single banner field is empty". Once a mod edits, the field is non-empty and never gets overwritten.

`UpdateBanner` is called from seed with a synthetic "admin" identity — same trick as `seed.Articles` resolving `adminUserID` via `st.Users().GetByUserID(ctx, auth.ReservedUsernameAdmin)` (`internal/seed/articles.go:47`).

## Reused functions / patterns (don't reinvent)

- `seed.Articles` flow (`internal/seed/articles.go`) — copy the embed + admin-resolve + per-entity loop shape.
- `ArticleRepo.Update` permission/error pattern (`internal/store/articles.go:144-165`) — verbatim shape for `UpdateBanner`.
- `articleEditModel` (`internal/tui/screen_article_edit.go`) — body-only variant for banner-edit. Drop the title field and the focus toggle; keep ctrl+s submit, esc cancel, loadErr early-deny.
- `articleViewModel.canEditArticle` (`screen_article_view.go:135-143`) — template for `canEditBanner`.
- `boardViewModel.isGuest` style (`screen_board_view.go:163-165`) — same pattern for permission predicates.
- Form-screen no-h/l convention — just don't bind them in the Update switch (per `screen_post_compose.go`).
- `StyleHeader` / `StyleDim` / `StyleHelp` / `StyleHighlight` / `StyleError` from `internal/tui/styles.go` — reuse for banner block + splash.

## Approved Plan:
# Per-Board ANSI/ASCII-Art Banner

## Context

Board screens currently jump straight from a single-line `StyleHeader` ("看板 X · 標題") into the article list — there's no place to express a board's identity, no PTT-style splash, no signature ASCII art. We want each board to carry an editable banner that:

1. Renders **inline** above the article list (PTT-like, always visible while browsing the board).
2. Can be expanded into a **full-screen splash** on demand (when capped inline doesn't show the full piece).
3. Is **seeded once per board** from `internal/seed/banners/<Name>.txt`, with a shared `_default.txt` fallback for boards that don't ship a per-board file.
4. Is **editable by mods+** through a TUI form, mirroring the existing article-edit screen.

UX decisions (locked via AskUserQuestion in this session):

- **Mode**: permanent inline header (capped 8 lines) + summonable full splash via `b`. No auto-splash on entry — that needs per-session "seen" state we don't otherwise need yet.
- **Format**: raw ANSI/ASCII passthrough only. No `glamour` for banners — PTT culture is colored ASCII art (figlet/lolcat output, manual `\x1b[…m` codes), and glamour mangles raw ANSI. Future markdown-banner mode can ship later via a frontmatter switch if desired.

Non-goals this round: per-board mod binding (the `boards.bm` column stays unused; mod+ is the global role check, matching `ArticleRepo.Update`); markdown rendering through glamour; splash-on-entry session tracking; banner versioning / history.

## Architecture summary

| Layer | Change |
|---|---|
| Schema | New column `boards.banner TEXT` via migration `0006_boards_banner.sql` |
| Repo | `BoardRepo.UpdateBanner(ctx, boardID, requesterID, requesterRole, banner)` mirroring `ArticleRepo.Update` shape (`internal/store/articles.go:144-165`) |
| Seed | New `internal/seed/banners.go` + `internal/seed/banners/*.txt` (embed). Idempotency = `banner IS NULL` per board (different from articles' "board has zero rows" rule because banner is one field, not many rows) |
| Width helpers | New `stripANSI` / `bannerVisualWidth` / `bannerTruncate` in `internal/tui/runewidth.go` — leave existing `Width/Truncate/PadRight/PadLeft` untouched (they're load-bearing for the article-list table; banner is the only ANSI-bearing render path) |
| Inline render | `screen_board_view.View()` gains a banner block between current header (line 96) and error/list (line 98); cap at `MaxInlineBannerLines = 8` with "(b 看完整)" tail when over |
| Splash screen | New `screen_board_splash.go` (`ScreenBoardSplash`) — full-render banner, any key returns to `ScreenBoardView` |
| Edit screen | New `screen_board_banner_edit.go` (`ScreenBoardBannerEdit`) — single `textarea.Model`, ctrl+s submits, esc cancels, no h/l binding. Permission gated to `RoleMod`+ at construction (early `loadErr`) |
| Keys on board view | `b` → splash (everyone); `B` → edit (mod+ only, hint hidden otherwise) |
| Wiring | Two new `Screen*` constants in `messages.go`; two new cases in `root.go` navigate switch |

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"errors"
7		"time"
8	)
9	
10	type Article struct {
11		ID             int64
12		BoardID        int64
13		AuthorID       int64
14		AuthorUserID   string
15		Title          string
16		Body           string
17		RecommendScore int64
18		Filemode       int64
19		CreatedAt      time.Time
20		UpdatedAt      sql.NullTime
21	}
22	
23	type ArticleRepo struct{ s *Store }
24	
25	var (
26		ErrArticleNotFound  = errors.New("article not found")
27		ErrPermissionDenied = errors.New("permission denied")
28	)
29	
30	const articleColumns = `id, board_id, author_id, author_userid, title, body,
31		recommend_score, filemode, created_at, updated_at`
32	
33	func scanArticle(row interface{ Scan(...any) error }) (*Article, error) {
34		a := &Article{}
35		err := row.Scan(
36			&a.ID, &a.BoardID, &a.AuthorID, &a.AuthorUserID, &a.Title, &a.Body,
37			&a.RecommendScore, &a.Filemode, &a.CreatedAt, &a.UpdatedAt,
38		)
39		return a, err
40	}
41	
42	func (r *ArticleRepo) ListByBoard(ctx context.Context, boardID int64, limit int) ([]*Article, error) {
43		if limit <= 0 {
44			limit = 50
45		}
46		rows, err := r.s.db.QueryContext(ctx,
47			`SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE board_id = ? ORDER BY created_at DESC, id DESC LIMIT ?`,
48			boardID, limit,
49		)
50		if err != nil {
51			return nil, err
52		}
53		defer rows.Close()
54		var out []*Article
55		for rows.Next() {
56			a, err := scanArticle(rows)
57			if err != nil {
58				return nil, err
59			}
60			out = append(out, a)
61		}
62		return out, rows.Err()
63	}
64	
65	func (r *ArticleRepo) GetByID(ctx context.Context, id int64) (*Article, error) {
66		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, id)
67		a, err := scanArticle(row)
68		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
69			return nil, ErrArticleNotFound
70		}
71		return a, err
72	}
73	
74	// NeighboursOf returns the article IDs immediately older (prev) and newer
75	// (next) than articleID within the same board, or 0 when there is no
76	// neighbour on that side. "Older" / "newer" are by id (monotonic insert
77	// order); this matches the user-visible "[ / ]" semantics in article view.
78	func (r *ArticleRepo) NeighboursOf(ctx context.Context, boardID, articleID int64) (prev, next int64, err error) {
79		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
80			`SELECT id FROM articles WHERE board_id = ? AND id < ? ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1`,
81			boardID, articleID,
82		)
83		if scanErr := row.Scan(&prev); scanErr != nil && !errors.Is(scanErr, sql.ErrNoRows) {
84			return 0, 0, scanErr
85		}
86		row = r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
87			`SELECT id FROM articles WHERE board_id = ? AND id > ? ORDER BY id ASC LIMIT 1`,
88			boardID, articleID,
89		)
90		if scanErr := row.Scan(&next); scanErr != nil && !errors.Is(scanErr, sql.ErrNoRows) {
91			return 0, 0, scanErr
92		}
93		return prev, next, nil
94	}
95	
96	func (r *ArticleRepo) Create(ctx context.Context, boardID, authorID int64, authorUserID, title, body string) (*Article, error) {
97		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
98		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
99		res, err := r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
100			`INSERT INTO articles (board_id, author_id, author_userid, title, body) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
101			boardID, authorID, authorUserID, title, body,
102		)
103		if err != nil {
104			return nil, err
105		}
106		id, err := res.LastInsertId()
107		if err != nil {
108			return nil, err
109		}
110		row := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT `+articleColumns+` FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, id)
111		return scanArticle(row)
112	}
113	
114	// Delete hard-deletes the article. Permission: requester must be the author
115	// OR have a role at-or-above mod. Returns ErrArticleNotFound when the row
116	// is gone, ErrPermissionDenied when the requester is unauthorized.
117	// Cascades to pushes via the FK on pushes.article_id.
118	func (r *ArticleRepo) Delete(ctx context.Context, articleID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role) error {
119		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
120		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
121		var authorID int64
122		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
123			`SELECT author_id FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
124		).Scan(&authorID)
125		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
126			return ErrArticleNotFound
127		}
128		if err != nil {
129			return err
130		}
131		if authorID != requesterID && !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
132			return ErrPermissionDenied
133		}
134		_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx, `DELETE FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID)
135		return err
136	}
137	
138	// Update mutates the article's title and body and sets updated_at to now.
139	// Permission mirrors Delete: requester must be the author OR have a role
140	// at-or-above mod. Returns ErrArticleNotFound / ErrPermissionDenied. Score,
141	// board, and author_id are intentionally not editable here — those would be
142	// separate concerns (move-board / re-attribute) outside the user-facing
143	// edit flow.
144	func (r *ArticleRepo) Update(ctx context.Context, articleID, requesterID int64, requesterRole Role, newTitle, newBody string) error {
145		r.s.writeMu.Lock()
146		defer r.s.writeMu.Unlock()
147		var authorID int64
148		err := r.s.db.QueryRowContext(ctx,
149			`SELECT author_id FROM articles WHERE id = ?`, articleID,
150		).Scan(&authorID)
151		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
152			return ErrArticleNotFound
153		}
154		if err != nil {
155			return err
156		}
157		if authorID != requesterID && !requesterRole.AtLeast(RoleMod) {
158			return ErrPermissionDenied
159		}
160		_, err = r.s.db.ExecContext(ctx,
161			`UPDATE articles SET title = ?, body = ?, updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE id = ?`,
162			newTitle, newBody, articleID,
163		)
164		return err
165	}
166
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/store/role.go`

```text
1	package store
2	
3	// Role is the user permission tier. Stored as a TEXT column with a CHECK
4	// constraint; ordered by AtLeast for permission gates. Lives in the store
5	// package (rather than auth) because the type is the column type, and
6	// auth already imports store — the reverse would create a cycle.
7	type Role string
8	
9	const (
10		RoleGuest Role = "guest" // read-only spectator; no password required
11		RoleUser  Role = "user"  // registered, default after register
12		RoleMod   Role = "mod"   // can delete anyone's article/push
13		RoleAdmin Role = "admin" // can promote/demote others
14	)
15	
16	var roleRank = map[Role]int{
17		RoleGuest: 0,
18		RoleUser:  1,
19		RoleMod:   2,
20		RoleAdmin: 3,
21	}
22	
23	// AtLeast returns true if r is at or above min in the rank table.
24	// Unknown roles always return false (fail-closed).
25	func (r Role) AtLeast(min Role) bool {
26		rr, rok := roleRank[r]
27		mr, mok := roleRank[min]
28		if !rok || !mok {
29			return false
30		}
31		return rr >= mr
32	}
33	
34	// Valid reports whether r is one of the four known role values.
35	func (r Role) Valid() bool {
36		_, ok := roleRank[r]
37		return ok
38	}
39
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"strings"
7	
8		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textarea"
9		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput"
10		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
11	
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
13	)
14	
15	const (
16		editFocusTitle = iota
17		editFocusBody
18	)
19	
20	// articleEditModel mirrors postComposeModel but pre-fills from an existing
21	// article and routes to ArticleRepo.Update on submit. This is a form
22	// screen — h/l are intentionally NOT bound (they belong to the textarea).
23	type articleEditModel struct {
24		deps      Deps
25		articleID int64
26		boardID   int64
27		title     textinput.Model
28		body      textarea.Model
29		focus     int
30		width     int
31		height    int
32		err       string
33		loadErr   error
34	}
35	
36	func newArticleEditModel(deps Deps, articleID int64) articleEditModel {
37		ti := textinput.New()
38		ti.Placeholder = "標題 title"
39		ti.CharLimit = 64
40		ti.Width = 60
41	
42		ta := textarea.New()
43		ta.Placeholder = "內容 body…"
44		ta.CharLimit = 8000
45		ta.SetWidth(72)
46		ta.SetHeight(12)
47	
48		m := articleEditModel{deps: deps, articleID: articleID, title: ti, body: ta, focus: editFocusTitle}
49	
50		if deps.User == nil {
51			m.loadErr = errors.New("not logged in")
52			return m
53		}
54		a, err := deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(context.Background(), articleID)
55		if err != nil {
56			m.loadErr = err
57			return m
58		}
59		// Permission check at construction time — deny early so the screen
60		// doesn't even mount for a user who can't edit. Mirrors
61		// canDeleteArticle: author OR mod+.
62		if a.AuthorID != deps.User.ID && !deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod) {
63			m.loadErr = store.ErrPermissionDenied
64			return m
65		}
66		m.boardID = a.BoardID
67		m.title.SetValue(a.Title)
68		m.body.SetValue(a.Body)
69		m.title.Focus()
70		return m
71	}
72	
73	func (m articleEditModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return textinput.Blink }
74	
75	func (m articleEditModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
76		switch msg := msg.(type) {
77		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
78			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
79			bodyW := max(40, msg.Width-8)
80			bodyH := max(8, msg.Height-12)
81			m.body.SetWidth(bodyW)
82			m.body.SetHeight(bodyH)
83			m.title.Width = max(20, msg.Width-12)
84			return m, nil
85	
86		case tea.KeyMsg:
87			if m.loadErr != nil {
88				// Any key bounces back to article view (or board view if we
89				// don't even know the article).
90				return m, m.cancelCmd()
91			}
92			switch msg.String() {
93			case "esc":
94				return m, m.cancelCmd()
95			case "tab":
96				m.toggleFocus()
97				return m, nil
98			case "ctrl+s":
99				return m.submit()
100			}
101		}
102	
103		if m.loadErr != nil {
104			return m, nil
105		}
106	
107		var cmd tea.Cmd
108		if m.focus == editFocusTitle {
109			if k, ok := msg.(tea.KeyMsg); ok && k.String() == "enter" {
110				m.toggleFocus()
111				return m, nil
112			}
113			m.title, cmd = m.title.Update(msg)
114		} else {
115			m.body, cmd = m.body.Update(msg)
116		}
117		return m, cmd
118	}
119	
120	func (m *articleEditModel) toggleFocus() {
121		if m.focus == editFocusTitle {
122			m.focus = editFocusBody
123			m.title.Blur()
124			m.body.Focus()
125		} else {
126			m.focus = editFocusTitle
127			m.body.Blur()
128			m.title.Focus()
129		}
130	}
131	
132	func (m articleEditModel) cancelCmd() tea.Cmd {
133		id := m.articleID
134		boardID := m.boardID
135		return func() tea.Msg {
136			// If we never loaded a board id (loadErr early), fall back to the
137			// board list — better than landing on an invalid article view.
138			if boardID == 0 {
139				return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardList}
140			}
141			return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ArticleID: id, BoardID: boardID}
142		}
143	}
144	
145	func (m articleEditModel) submit() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
146		title := strings.TrimSpace(m.title.Value())
147		body := m.body.Value()
148		if title == "" {
149			m.err = "title is required"
150			return m, nil
151		}
152		if strings.TrimSpace(body) == "" {
153			m.err = "body is required"
154			return m, nil
155		}
156		ctx := context.Background()
157		u := m.deps.User
158		if err := m.deps.Store.Articles().Update(ctx, m.articleID, u.ID, u.Role, title, body); err != nil {
159			m.err = err.Error()
160			return m, nil
161		}
162		if m.deps.Broker != nil {
163			// Exclude the editor's own session — they already mutated their
164			// local article state by reloading on the way back to article view.
165			m.deps.Broker.SendToAll(u.ID, ArticleUpdatedMsg{ArticleID: m.articleID})
166		}
167		id := m.articleID
168		boardID := m.boardID
169		return m, func() tea.Msg {
170			return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ArticleID: id, BoardID: boardID}
171		}
172	}
173	
174	func (m articleEditModel) View() string {
175		var b strings.Builder
176		b.WriteString("\n")
177		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 編輯文章 Edit Post "))
178		b.WriteString("\n\n")
179	
180		if m.loadErr != nil {
181			b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n")
182			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("press any key to go back"))
183			b.WriteString("\n")
184			return b.String()
185		}
186	
187		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Title:") + "\n")
188		b.WriteString("  " + m.title.View() + "\n\n")
189	
190		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Body:") + "\n")
191		b.WriteString("  " + indent(m.body.View(), "  ") + "\n")
192	
193		if m.err != "" {
194			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err) + "\n")
195		}
196		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("Tab switch field · Enter (in title) → body · Ctrl+S save · Esc cancel"))
197		b.WriteString("\n")
198		return b.String()
199	}

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/tui/messages.go`

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	// Screen identifies which sub-model the Root should be showing.
4	type Screen int
5	
6	const (
7		ScreenMainMenu Screen = iota
8		ScreenBoardList
9		ScreenBoardView
10		ScreenArticleView
11		ScreenPostCompose
12		ScreenWBInbox
13		ScreenWBCompose
14		ScreenOnline
15		ScreenMailInbox
16		ScreenMailThread
17		ScreenMailCompose
18		ScreenPasswordChange
19		ScreenAdminUsers
20		ScreenArticleEdit
21		ScreenArticleExport
22	)
23	
24	// NavigateMsg is emitted by sub-screens to ask the Root to swap screens.
25	// Optional fields carry context the destination screen needs.
26	type NavigateMsg struct {
27		To           Screen
28		BoardID      int64
29		ArticleID    int64
30		Recipient    string // for ScreenWBCompose / ScreenMailCompose: prefilled recipient userid
31		MailID       int64  // for ScreenMailCompose: parent mail id when replying
32		MailThreadID int64  // for ScreenMailThread: which thread to open
33	}
34	
35	// ErrorMsg surfaces an error as a transient toast.
36	type ErrorMsg struct{ Err error }
37	
38	// WBIncomingMsg is delivered by the chat broker (or replayed from DB on Init)
39	// to notify the recipient that a new water balloon arrived.
40	type WBIncomingMsg struct {
41		ID         int64
42		FromUserID string
43		Body       string
44	}
45	
46	// PushAddedMsg is broadcast by the chat broker when a push is added to an
47	// article. The article-view model checks ArticleID to decide whether to
48	// re-render.
49	type PushAddedMsg struct {
50		ArticleID  int64
51		UserUserID string
52		Kind       string
53		Body       string
54	}
55	
56	// MailIncomingMsg notifies the recipient that a new mail has arrived.
57	// Unlike WBIncomingMsg it does NOT auto-toast — mail is asynchronous; the
58	// open mail inbox can choose to refresh, and the main menu reads the unread
59	// counter from DB.
60	type MailIncomingMsg struct {
61		ID         int64
62		ThreadID   int64
63		FromUserID string
64		Subject    string
65	}
66	
67	// ArticleAddedMsg is broadcast by the chat broker when a new article is
68	// posted. Board-view models filter by BoardID and re-fetch the article
69	// list from DB so timestamps and ordering reflect canonical state.
70	type ArticleAddedMsg struct {
71		BoardID      int64
72		ArticleID    int64
73		AuthorUserID string
74		Title        string
75	}
76	
77	// ArticleUpdatedMsg is broadcast by the chat broker when an article's
78	// title or body is edited. Article-view models filter by ArticleID and
79	// re-fetch from DB so they don't render stale text.
80	type ArticleUpdatedMsg struct {
81		ArticleID int64
82	}
83
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/tui/root.go`

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"time"
7	
8		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
9	
10		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/chat"
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
12	)
13	
14	// Deps bundles everything every screen may need. Pass-by-value into
15	// constructors keeps screens self-contained.
16	type Deps struct {
17		Store              *store.Store
18		Broker             *chat.Broker // nil during register
19		User               *store.User  // nil iff IsRegister
20		IsRegister         bool
21		MustChangePassword bool // true iff user must rotate password before reaching the main menu
22	}
23	
24	// Root is the top-level tea.Model. It owns the active sub-model and
25	// handles cross-cutting concerns: window size, global hotkeys, navigation,
26	// and the toast footer.
27	type Root struct {
28		deps   Deps
29		sub    tea.Model
30		width  int
31		height int
32	
33		toast      string
34		toastUntil time.Time
35	}
36	
37	const toastDuration = 3 * time.Second
38	
39	// NewRoot builds the Root with the right initial sub-screen.
40	//
41	// Priority: IsRegister wins over MustChangePassword wins over the regular
42	// authenticated main menu — these flags are mutually exclusive in practice
43	// (set by the SSH password callback / makeProgramHandler).
44	func NewRoot(deps Deps) Root {
45		r := Root{deps: deps}
46		switch {
47		case deps.IsRegister:
48			r.sub = newRegisterModel(deps)
49		case deps.MustChangePassword:
50			r.sub = newPasswordChangeModel(deps)
51		default:
52			r.sub = newMainMenuModel(deps)
53		}
54		return r
55	}
56	
57	func (m Root) Init() tea.Cmd {
58		return m.sub.Init()
59	}
60	
61	func (m Root) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
62		switch msg := msg.(type) {
63		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
64			m.width = msg.Width
65			m.height = msg.Height
66			var cmd tea.Cmd
67			m.sub, cmd = m.sub.Update(msg)
68			return m, cmd
69	
70		case tea.KeyMsg:
71			// Global ctrl+c always quits.
72			if msg.String() == "ctrl+c" {
73				return m, tea.Quit
74			}
75			// Global Ctrl+U opens water balloon inbox (logged-in only).
76			if msg.String() == "ctrl+u" && !m.deps.IsRegister {
77				return m.navigate(NavigateMsg{To: ScreenWBInbox})
78			}
79	
80		case NavigateMsg:
81			return m.navigate(msg)
82	
83		case ErrorMsg:
84			if msg.Err != nil {
85				m.setToast("⚠ " + msg.Err.Error())
86			}
87			return m, nil
88	
89		case WBIncomingMsg:
90			m.setToast("💧 " + msg.FromUserID + ": " + msg.Body)
91			// Mark this WB as read so it doesn't replay on reconnect.
92			if m.deps.Store != nil && msg.ID != 0 {
93				go func(id int64) {
94					_ = m.deps.Store.WaterBalloons().MarkRead(context.Background(), id)
95				}(msg.ID)
96			}
97			// fall through so the active screen can also react
98		}
99	
100		var cmd tea.Cmd
101		m.sub, cmd = m.sub.Update(msg)
102		return m, cmd
103	}
104	
105	func (m Root) View() string {
106		v := m.sub.View()
107		if m.toast != "" && time.Now().Before(m.toastUntil) {
108			v += "\n" + StyleToast.Render(m.toast)
109		}
110		return v
111	}
112	
113	func (m *Root) setToast(s string) {
114		m.toast = s
115		m.toastUntil = time.Now().Add(toastDuration)
116	}
117	
118	// guestWriteBlocked returns an ErrorMsg cmd if a guest is trying to enter
119	// a write-capable screen, otherwise nil. Layer 1 of the read-only path.
120	func (m Root) guestWriteBlocked(to Screen) tea.Cmd {
121		if m.deps.User == nil || m.deps.User.Role != store.RoleGuest {
122			return nil
123		}
124		switch to {
125		case ScreenPostCompose, ScreenWBCompose, ScreenMailCompose, ScreenAdminUsers, ScreenArticleEdit:
126			return func() tea.Msg { return ErrorMsg{Err: errors.New("guest 為唯讀帳號 (read-only)")} }
127		}
128		return nil
129	}
130	
131	// navigateNonAdminBlocked returns an ErrorMsg cmd if a non-admin tries
132	// to reach the admin user-management screen.
133	func (m Root) navigateNonAdminBlocked(to Screen) tea.Cmd {
134		if to != ScreenAdminUsers {
135			return nil
136		}
137		if m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role == store.RoleAdmin {
138			return nil
139		}
140		return func() tea.Msg { return ErrorMsg{Err: errors.New("管理員專用 (admin only)")} }
141	}
142	
143	func (m Root) navigate(n NavigateMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
144		if cmd := m.guestWriteBlocked(n.To); cmd != nil {
145			return m, cmd
146		}
147		if cmd := m.navigateNonAdminBlocked(n.To); cmd != nil {
148			return m, cmd
149		}
150		var sub tea.Model
151		switch n.To {
152		case ScreenMainMenu:
153			sub = newMainMenuModel(m.deps)
154		case ScreenBoardList:
155			sub = newBoardListModel(m.deps)
156		case ScreenBoardView:
157			sub = newBoardViewModel(m.deps, n.BoardID)
158		case ScreenArticleView:
159			sub = newArticleViewModel(m.deps, n.ArticleID)
160		case ScreenPostCompose:
161			sub = newPostComposeModel(m.deps, n.BoardID)
162		case ScreenWBInbox:
163			sub = newWBInboxModel(m.deps)
164		case ScreenWBCompose:
165			sub = newWBComposeModel(m.deps, n.Recipient)
166		case ScreenOnline:
167			sub = newOnlineModel(m.deps)
168		case ScreenMailInbox:
169			sub = newMailInboxModel(m.deps)
170		case ScreenMailThread:
171			sub = newMailThreadModel(m.deps, n.MailThreadID)
172		case ScreenMailCompose:
173			sub = newMailComposeModel(m.deps, n.Recipient, n.MailID)
174		case ScreenPasswordChange:
175			sub = newPasswordChangeModel(m.deps)
176		case ScreenAdminUsers:
177			sub = newAdminUsersModel(m.deps)
178		case ScreenArticleEdit:
179			sub = newArticleEditModel(m.deps, n.ArticleID)
180		case ScreenArticleExport:
181			sub = newArticleExportModel(m.deps, n.ArticleID)
182		default:
183			// Unknown screen — leave sub untouched. Later steps add cases.
184			return m, nil
185		}
186		m.sub = sub
187		cmd := sub.Init()
188		if m.width > 0 {
189			// Forward current size to the new sub so it can lay out immediately.
190			var cmd2 tea.Cmd
191			m.sub, cmd2 = m.sub.Update(tea.WindowSizeMsg{Width: m.width, Height: m.height})
192			if cmd2 != nil {
193				cmd = tea.Batch(cmd, cmd2)
194			}
195		}
196		return m, cmd
197	}
198
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/tui/screen_article_view.go`

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"strings"
7	
8		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput"
9		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
10		"github.com/charmbracelet/glamour"
11	
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
13	)
14	
15	// glamourFallbackWidth is the wrap width used before we get a
16	// WindowSizeMsg. 80 is the historical terminal default; the renderer
17	// re-runs with the real width as soon as the first resize lands.
18	const glamourFallbackWidth = 80
19	
20	type articleViewModel struct {
21		deps    Deps
22		article *store.Article
23		pushes  []*store.Push
24		width   int
25		height  int
26		scroll  int
27		loadErr error
28	
29		// rendered is the article body after running through glamour. We cache
30		// because (a) glamour's first call loads chroma's syntax highlighter
31		// (~tens of ms) and (b) View() is called on every keystroke. Re-render
32		// only when the article changes or the window width changes.
33		rendered      string
34		renderedWidth int
35	
36		// push input state
37		pushing  bool
38		pushKind store.PushKind
39		pushIn   textinput.Model
40		err      string
41	
42		// delete state. pendingDelete: showing the y/N confirm overlay.
43		// pushCursor: -1 = no push selected (D targets article); 0..len-1 = D targets that push.
44		pendingDelete bool
45		pushCursor    int
46	}
47	
48	// renderBody runs the article body through glamour with word-wrap set
49	// to the current viewport. Falls back to the raw body on any glamour
50	// error so a corrupt-markdown article is still readable.
51	func (m *articleViewModel) renderBody() {
52		if m.article == nil {
53			m.rendered = ""
54			m.renderedWidth = 0
55			return
56		}
57		width := m.width - 4
58		if width < 40 {
59			width = glamourFallbackWidth
60		}
61		r, err := glamour.NewTermRenderer(
62			glamour.WithStandardStyle("dark"),
63			glamour.WithWordWrap(width),
64		)
65		if err != nil {
66			m.rendered = m.article.Body
67			m.renderedWidth = width
68			return
69		}
70		out, err := r.Render(m.article.Body)
71		if err != nil {
72			m.rendered = m.article.Body
73			m.renderedWidth = width
74			return
75		}
76		// Trim leading/trailing blank lines glamour likes to add for
77		// breathing room — they push the content off-screen on small
78		// viewports. Internal blank lines stay.
79		m.rendered = strings.Trim(out, "\n")
80		m.renderedWidth = width
81	}
82	
83	func newArticleViewModel(deps Deps, articleID int64) articleViewModel {
84		ctx := context.Background()
85		a, err := deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, articleID)
86		if err != nil {
87			return articleViewModel{deps: deps, loadErr: err}
88		}
89		pushes, err := deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, articleID)
90	
91		ti := textinput.New()
92		ti.Placeholder = "comment…"
93		ti.CharLimit = 80
94		ti.Width = 60
95	
96		m := articleViewModel{deps: deps, article: a, pushes: pushes, loadErr: err, pushIn: ti, pushCursor: -1}
97		// Render with the fallback width so the body is readable even before
98		// the first WindowSizeMsg arrives — Update re-renders on resize.
99		m.renderBody()
100		return m
101	}
102	
103	// canDeleteArticle returns true if the current user may delete the loaded
104	// article (author, or mod-or-above). Used both as a permission gate at
105	// the key handler and to decide whether to render the help-line hint.
106	func (m articleViewModel) canDeleteArticle() bool {
107		if m.deps.User == nil || m.article == nil {
108			return false
109		}
110		if m.deps.User.ID == m.article.AuthorID {
111			return true
112		}
113		return m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
114	}
115	
116	// canDeletePush mirrors canDeleteArticle for the push at index idx.
117	func (m articleViewModel) canDeletePush(idx int) bool {
118		if m.deps.User == nil || idx < 0 || idx >= len(m.pushes) {
119			return false
120		}
121		if m.deps.User.ID == m.pushes[idx].UserID {
122			return true
123		}
124		return m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
125	}
126	
127	// canPush returns true if the current user is allowed to add new pushes.
128	// Guests can read but not write.
129	func (m articleViewModel) canPush() bool {
130		return m.deps.User != nil && m.deps.User.Role != store.RoleGuest
131	}
132	
133	// canEditArticle mirrors canDeleteArticle: the author may edit their own
134	// post, mods and admins may edit anyone's.
135	func (m articleViewModel) canEditArticle() bool {
136		if m.deps.User == nil || m.article == nil {
137			return false
138		}
139		if m.deps.User.ID == m.article.AuthorID {
140			return true
141		}
142		return m.deps.User.Role.AtLeast(store.RoleMod)
143	}
144	
145	func (m articleViewModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return nil }
146	
147	func (m articleViewModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
148		switch msg := msg.(type) {
149		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
150			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
151			// Only re-render when the width target actually changes — height
152			// changes don't affect glamour wrap.
153			newWidth := max(msg.Width-4, 40)
154			if newWidth != m.renderedWidth {
155				m.renderBody()
156			}
157			return m, nil
158	
159		case PushAddedMsg:
160			if m.article != nil && msg.ArticleID == m.article.ID {
161				ctx := context.Background()
162				// Re-fetch from DB so timestamps and recommend_score reflect
163				// the canonical state, not the just-synthesized broker payload.
164				if pushes, err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
165					m.pushes = pushes
166				}
167				if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
168					m.article = a
169				}
170			}
171	
172		case ArticleUpdatedMsg:
173			if m.article != nil && msg.ArticleID == m.article.ID {
174				// Edit happened in another session — refetch the canonical
175				// title/body so we render the new text.
176				if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(context.Background(), m.article.ID); err == nil {
177					m.article = a
178					m.renderBody()
179					// Reset scroll if the new body is shorter than where we
180					// were parked.
181					if m.scroll > m.bodyLineCount() {
182						m.scroll = 0
183					}
184				}
185			}
186			return m, nil
187	
188		case tea.KeyMsg:
189			if m.pushing {
190				return m.updatePushInput(msg)
191			}
192			if m.pendingDelete {
193				return m.updateDeleteConfirm(msg)
194			}
195			switch msg.String() {
196			case "esc", "backspace", "left", "h":
197				boardID := int64(0)
198				if m.article != nil {
199					boardID = m.article.BoardID
200				}
201				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: boardID} }
202			case "Q":
203				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenMainMenu} }
204			case "up", "k":
205				if m.scroll > 0 {
206					m.scroll--
207				}
208			case "down", "j":
209				m.scroll++
210			case "pgup", "b":
211				m.scroll = max(0, m.scroll-10)
212			case "pgdown", " ":
213				m.scroll += 10
214			case "g":
215				m.scroll = 0
216			case "G":
217				m.scroll = max(0, m.bodyLineCount()-m.viewportLines())
218			case "[":
219				if m.article == nil {
220					return m, nil
221				}
222				prev, _, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().NeighboursOf(context.Background(), m.article.BoardID, m.article.ID)
223				if err != nil || prev == 0 {
224					return m, nil
225				}
226				return m, func() tea.Msg {
227					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ArticleID: prev, BoardID: m.article.BoardID}
228				}
229			case "]":
230				if m.article == nil {
231					return m, nil
232				}
233				_, next, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().NeighboursOf(context.Background(), m.article.BoardID, m.article.ID)
234				if err != nil || next == 0 {
235					return m, nil
236				}
237				return m, func() tea.Msg {
238					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleView, ArticleID: next, BoardID: m.article.BoardID}
239				}
240			case "+":
241				if !m.canPush() {
242					return m, nil
243				}
244				return m.openPush(store.PushKindPush), nil
245			case "-":
246				if !m.canPush() {
247					return m, nil
248				}
249				return m.openPush(store.PushKindBoo), nil
250			case "=":
251				if !m.canPush() {
252					return m, nil
253				}
254				return m.openPush(store.PushKindArrow), nil
255			case "p":
256				// Advance the push cursor. -1 → 0 → ... → len-1 → -1 (cycles
257				// back to "no selection, D targets the article").
258				if len(m.pushes) == 0 {
259					return m, nil
260				}
261				m.pushCursor++
262				if m.pushCursor >= len(m.pushes) {
263					m.pushCursor = -1
264				}
265				m.err = ""
266				return m, nil
267			case "P":
268				if len(m.pushes) == 0 {
269					return m, nil
270				}
271				if m.pushCursor < 0 {
272					m.pushCursor = len(m.pushes) - 1
273				} else {
274					m.pushCursor--
275				}
276				m.err = ""
277				return m, nil
278			case "D":
279				if m.pushCursor >= 0 {
280					if !m.canDeletePush(m.pushCursor) {
281						m.err = "權限不足 (only the push author or a mod can delete it)"
282						return m, nil
283					}
284				} else {
285					if !m.canDeleteArticle() {
286						m.err = "權限不足 (only the author or a mod can delete)"
287						return m, nil
288					}
289				}
290				m.pendingDelete = true
291				m.err = ""
292				return m, nil
293			case "E":
294				if m.article == nil {
295					return m, nil
296				}
297				if !m.canEditArticle() {
298					m.err = "權限不足 (only the author or a mod can edit)"
299					return m, nil
300				}
301				id := m.article.ID
302				boardID := m.article.BoardID
303				return m, func() tea.Msg {
304					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleEdit, ArticleID: id, BoardID: boardID}
305				}
306			case "y":
307				if m.article == nil {
308					return m, nil
309				}
310				id := m.article.ID
311				boardID := m.article.BoardID
312				return m, func() tea.Msg {
313					return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenArticleExport, ArticleID: id, BoardID: boardID}
314				}
315			}
316		}
317		return m, nil
318	}
319	
320	// updateDeleteConfirm handles y/n while the confirm overlay is up.
321	// y: dispatch the delete; if targeting a push, refresh the push list
322	// and the article (for the score). If targeting the article, navigate
323	// back to the board view.
324	// n / esc: cancel.
325	func (m articleViewModel) updateDeleteConfirm(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
326		switch msg.String() {
327		case "y", "Y":
328			if m.article == nil || m.deps.User == nil {
329				m.pendingDelete = false
330				return m, nil
331			}
332			ctx := context.Background()
333			if m.pushCursor >= 0 && m.pushCursor < len(m.pushes) {
334				pushID := m.pushes[m.pushCursor].ID
335				if err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().Delete(ctx, pushID, m.deps.User.ID, m.deps.User.Role); err != nil {
336					m.err = err.Error()
337					m.pendingDelete = false
338					return m, nil
339				}
340				// Refresh the push list and re-fetch the article so its
341				// recommend_score reflects the reverted delta.
342				if pushes, err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().ListByArticle(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
343					m.pushes = pushes
344				}
345				if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
346					m.article = a
347				}
348				// Clamp the cursor so the next D / p continues from a valid index.
349				if m.pushCursor >= len(m.pushes) {
350					m.pushCursor = -1
351				}
352				m.pendingDelete = false
353				return m, nil
354			}
355			if err := m.deps.Store.Articles().Delete(ctx, m.article.ID, m.deps.User.ID, m.deps.User.Role); err != nil {
356				m.err = err.Error()
357				m.pendingDelete = false
358				return m, nil
359			}
360			boardID := m.article.BoardID
361			return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: boardID} }
362		case "n", "N", "esc":
363			m.pendingDelete = false
364			return m, nil
365		}
366		return m, nil
367	}
368	
369	// bodyLineCount returns the number of lines View will render for the
370	// loaded article. Uses the glamour-rendered body so g/G/PgDn agree
371	// with what's actually on screen; falls back to raw if rendering hasn't
372	// run yet (shouldn't happen post-construction but defensive).
373	func (m articleViewModel) bodyLineCount() int {
374		if m.rendered != "" {
375			return len(strings.Split(m.rendered, "\n"))
376		}
377		if m.article == nil {
378			return 0
379		}
380		return len(strings.Split(m.article.Body, "\n"))
381	}
382	
383	// viewportLines mirrors the maxLines computation in View so g/G/PgDn
384	// agree on how many lines fit on screen.
385	func (m articleViewModel) viewportLines() int {
386		return max(m.height-16, 5)
387	}
388	
389	func (m articleViewModel) openPush(k store.PushKind) tea.Model {
390		m.pushing = true
391		m.pushKind = k
392		m.pushIn.SetValue("")
393		m.pushIn.Focus()
394		m.err = ""
395		return m
396	}
397	
398	func (m articleViewModel) updatePushInput(msg tea.KeyMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
399		switch msg.String() {
400		case "esc":
401			m.pushing = false
402			m.pushIn.Blur()
403			return m, nil
404		case "enter":
405			body := strings.TrimSpace(m.pushIn.Value())
406			if body == "" && m.pushKind != store.PushKindArrow {
407				m.err = "comment required for 推/噓"
408				return m, nil
409			}
410			ctx := context.Background()
411			u := m.deps.User
412			p, err := m.deps.Store.Pushes().Create(ctx, m.article.ID, u.ID, u.UserID, m.pushKind, body)
413			if err != nil {
414				m.err = err.Error()
415				return m, nil
416			}
417			// Append locally so the originator sees it immediately.
418			m.pushes = append(m.pushes, p)
419			if a, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().GetByID(ctx, m.article.ID); err == nil {
420				m.article = a
421			}
422			m.pushing = false
423			m.pushIn.Blur()
424			// Broadcast to other live sessions; they filter by ArticleID.
425			if m.deps.Broker != nil {
426				m.deps.Broker.SendToAll(u.ID, PushAddedMsg{
427					ArticleID:  m.article.ID,
428					UserUserID: u.UserID,
429					Kind:       string(m.pushKind),
430					Body:       body,
431				})
432			}
433			return m, nil
434		}
435		var cmd tea.Cmd
436		m.pushIn, cmd = m.pushIn.Update(msg)
437		return m, cmd
438	}
439	
440	func (m articleViewModel) View() string {
441		if m.loadErr != nil {
442			return "\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.loadErr.Error()) + "\n"
443		}
444		if m.article == nil {
445			return "\n  " + StyleDim.Render("(no article)") + "\n"
446		}
447		a := m.article
448	
449		var b strings.Builder
450		b.WriteString("\n")
451		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(fmt.Sprintf(" 文章 #%d · %s ", a.ID, a.AuthorUserID)))
452		b.WriteString("\n\n")
453	
454		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Title: ") + a.Title + "\n")
455		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Date:  ") + a.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02 15:04:05") + "\n")
456		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Score: ") + fmt.Sprintf("%d", a.RecommendScore) + "\n")
457		b.WriteString("\n")
458	
459		body := m.rendered
460		if body == "" {
461			body = a.Body
462		}
463		bodyLines := strings.Split(body, "\n")
464		start := min(m.scroll, len(bodyLines))
465		maxLines := max(m.height-16, 5)
466		end := min(start+maxLines, len(bodyLines))
467		for _, line := range bodyLines[start:end] {
468			b.WriteString(line + "\n")
469		}
470		if start > 0 || end < len(bodyLines) {
471			b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("[lines %d-%d / %d]", start+1, end, len(bodyLines))))
472			b.WriteString("\n")
473		}
474	
475		if len(m.pushes) > 0 {
476			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleDim.Render(fmt.Sprintf("── 推文 (%d) ──", len(m.pushes))) + "\n")
477			for i, p := range m.pushes {
478				ts := p.CreatedAt.Format("01/02 15:04")
479				gutter := "  "
480				if i == m.pushCursor {
481					gutter = "▸ "
482				}
483				line := fmt.Sprintf("%s%s %s %s  %s",
484					gutter,
485					renderPushKind(p.Kind),
486					PadRight(p.UserUserID, 14),
487					p.Body,
488					StyleDim.Render(ts),
489				)
490				if i == m.pushCursor {
491					line = StyleHighlight.Render(line)
492				}
493				b.WriteString(line + "\n")
494			}
495		}
496	
497		if m.pushing {
498			b.WriteString("\n  ")
499			b.WriteString(renderPushKind(m.pushKind) + " ")
500			b.WriteString(m.pushIn.View())
501			b.WriteString("\n")
502			if m.err != "" {
503				b.WriteString("  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err) + "\n")
504			}
505			b.WriteString("  " + StyleHelp.Render("Enter send · Esc cancel"))
506		} else if m.pendingDelete {
507			prompt := "確定刪除這篇文章? (y/N)"
508			if m.pushCursor >= 0 && m.pushCursor < len(m.pushes) {
509				prompt = fmt.Sprintf("確定刪除推文 #%d? (y/N)", m.pushCursor+1)
510			}
511			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+prompt))
512		} else {
513			help := "j/k scroll · y 匯出"
514			if m.canPush() {
515				help += " · + 推 · - 噓 · = →"
516			}
517			if len(m.pushes) > 0 {
518				help += " · p/P 選推文"
519			}
520			if m.canEditArticle() {
521				help += " · E 編輯"
522			}
523			if m.pushCursor >= 0 && m.canDeletePush(m.pushCursor) {
524				help += " · D 刪除推文"
525			} else if m.canDeleteArticle() {
526				help += " · D 刪除文章"
527			}
528			help += " · Esc/← back · Ctrl+C disconnect"
529			if m.err != "" {
530				b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err))
531			}
532			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render(help))
533		}
534		b.WriteString("\n")
535		return b.String()
536	}
537	
538	func renderPushKind(k store.PushKind) string {
539		switch k {
540		case store.PushKindPush:
541			return StylePushKind.Render("推")
542		case store.PushKindBoo:
543			return StyleBooKind.Render("噓")
544		case store.PushKindArrow:
545			return StyleArrowKind.Render("→")
546		}
547		return "?"
548	}
549
```

1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"
5		"github.com/muesli/termenv"
6	)
7	
8	// Force lipgloss's package-level renderer to a profile that supports
9	// background colors. The default renderer auto-detects from os.Stdout,
10	// which is wrong for a server: stdout might be redirected to a log file
11	// (e.g. `./sshbbs >server.log 2>&1 &`), making termenv pick `Ascii` and
12	// silently strip every Background() escape we render. The actual SSH
13	// session renders into the client's terminal (capable of TrueColor in
14	// every modern client), so committing to TrueColor here is safe and
15	// matches what wish's per-session renderer would pick anyway.
16	func init() {
17		lipgloss.SetColorProfile(termenv.TrueColor)
18	}
19	
20	var (
21		StyleHeader = lipgloss.NewStyle().
22				Bold(true).
23				Foreground(lipgloss.Color("231")).
24				Background(lipgloss.Color("63")).
25				Padding(0, 1)
26	
27		StyleFooter = lipgloss.NewStyle().
28				Foreground(lipgloss.Color("250")).
29				Background(lipgloss.Color("236")).
30				Padding(0, 1)
31	
32		StyleHelp = lipgloss.NewStyle().
33				Foreground(lipgloss.Color("241"))
34	
35		StyleError = lipgloss.NewStyle().
36				Foreground(lipgloss.Color("196")).
37				Bold(true)
38	
39		StyleSuccess = lipgloss.NewStyle().
40				Foreground(lipgloss.Color("46")).
41				Bold(true)
42	
43		StyleHighlight = lipgloss.NewStyle().

45				Background(lipgloss.Color("60"))

1	package main
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6		"flag"
7		"log"
8		"os"
9		"os/signal"
10		"strings"
11		"syscall"
12		"time"
13	
14		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
15		"github.com/charmbracelet/ssh"
16	
17		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/auth"
18		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/chat"
19		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/seed"
20		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/server"
21		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
22		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/version"
23	)
24	
25	func main() {
26		// Subcommand dispatch: anything that isn't a flag is treated as a
27		// subcommand. Today only `import` exists, but the shape leaves room
28		// for future ops tools (e.g. `export`, `dump`).
29		if len(os.Args) >= 2 && !strings.HasPrefix(os.Args[1], "-") {
30			switch os.Args[1] {
31			case "import":
32				os.Exit(runImport(os.Args[2:]))
33			default:
34				log.Fatalf("unknown subcommand: %s", os.Args[1])
35			}
36		}
37	
38		addr := flag.String("addr", ":2222", "SSH listen address")
39		dbPath := flag.String("db", "data/bbs.db", "SQLite database path")
40		hostkey := flag.String("hostkey", ".ssh/host_ed25519", "SSH host key path")
41		adminPassword := flag.String("admin-password", "",
42			"override admin password on first seed; empty uses the baked default 'admin' with must_change_password=1")
43		flag.Parse()
44	
45		st, err := store.Open(*dbPath)
46		if err != nil {
47			log.Fatalf("store.Open: %v", err)
48		}
49		defer st.Close()
50		if err := st.Boards().SeedDefaults(context.Background()); err != nil {
51			log.Fatalf("seed boards: %v", err)
52		}
53		if err := auth.SeedSystemAccounts(context.Background(), st, auth.SeedOpts{
54			AdminPassword: *adminPassword,
55		}); err != nil {
56			log.Fatalf("seed system accounts: %v", err)
57		}
58		if err := seed.Articles(context.Background(), st, auth.ReservedUsernameAdmin); err != nil {
59			log.Fatalf("seed articles: %v", err)
60		}
61		log.Printf("storage ready at %s", *dbPath)
62	
63		broker := chat.NewBroker()
64	
65		srv, err := server.New(server.Config{Addr: *addr, HostKey: *hostkey}, st, broker)
66		if err != nil {
67			log.Fatalf("server.New: %v", err)
68		}
69	
70		ctx, stop := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
71		defer stop()
72	
73		errCh := make(chan error, 1)
74		go func() {
75			log.Printf("sshbbs %s listening on %s", version.Version, *addr)
76			errCh <- srv.ListenAndServe()
77		}()
78	
79		select {
80		case err := <-errCh:
81			if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, ssh.ErrServerClosed) {
82				log.Fatalf("listen: %v", err)
83			}
84		case <-ctx.Done():
85			log.Println("shutdown signal received")
86			shutdown(srv, broker)
87		}
88	}
89	
90	// shutdown closes the listener (refuses new connections), then asks every
91	// live bubbletea program to quit, then closes the SSH server hard. The DB
92	// closes on the deferred st.Close() in main.
93	func shutdown(srv *ssh.Server, broker *chat.Broker) {
94		const drainTimeout = 3 * time.Second
95	
96		// 1. Stop accepting new connections.
97		listenerCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), drainTimeout)
98		defer cancel()
99		if err := srv.Shutdown(listenerCtx); err != nil {
100			log.Printf("server.Shutdown: %v", err)
101		}
102	
103		// 2. Tell every active session to quit.
104		sessions := broker.SessionsSnapshot()
105		if len(sessions) == 0 {
106			return
107		}
108		log.Printf("draining %d active session(s)", len(sessions))
109		for _, s := range sessions {
110			s.Program.Send(tea.Quit())
111		}
112	
113		// 3. Give them a moment to flush their final view, then force-close.
114		timer := time.NewTimer(drainTimeout)
115		defer timer.Stop()
116		for {
117			if len(broker.SessionsSnapshot()) == 0 {
118				log.Println("all sessions drained")
119				return
120			}
121			select {
122			case <-timer.C:
123				log.Printf("drain timeout; %d session(s) still attached", len(broker.SessionsSnapshot()))
124				return
125			case <-time.After(100 * time.Millisecond):
126			}
127		}
128	}
129
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/store/migrate.go`

```text
1	package store
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"database/sql"
6		"embed"
7		"fmt"
8		"sort"
9		"strconv"
10		"strings"
11	)
12	
13	//go:embed migrations/*.sql
14	var migrationsFS embed.FS
15	
16	// apply runs every migration whose version number isn't already in
17	// schema_migrations. Filenames must be NNNN_name.sql; the leading integer is
18	// the version. Migrations are applied in ascending version order, each in
19	// its own transaction.
20	func apply(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB) error {
21		if _, err := db.ExecContext(ctx, `
22			CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS schema_migrations (
23				version    INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
24				applied_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
25			)
26		`); err != nil {
27			return fmt.Errorf("create schema_migrations: %w", err)
28		}
29	
30		applied, err := loadApplied(ctx, db)
31		if err != nil {
32			return err
33		}
34	
35		entries, err := migrationsFS.ReadDir("migrations")
36		if err != nil {
37			return fmt.Errorf("read embedded migrations: %w", err)
38		}
39		type migration struct {
40			version  int
41			filename string
42		}
43		var pending []migration
44		for _, e := range entries {
45			if e.IsDir() || !strings.HasSuffix(e.Name(), ".sql") {
46				continue
47			}
48			v, err := parseVersion(e.Name())
49			if err != nil {
50				return err
51			}
52			if !applied[v] {
53				pending = append(pending, migration{version: v, filename: e.Name()})
54			}
55		}
56		sort.Slice(pending, func(i, j int) bool { return pending[i].version < pending[j].version })
57	
58		for _, m := range pending {
59			body, err := migrationsFS.ReadFile("migrations/" + m.filename)
60			if err != nil {
61				return fmt.Errorf("read migration %s: %w", m.filename, err)
62			}
63			if err := runOne(ctx, db, m.version, string(body)); err != nil {
64				return fmt.Errorf("apply %s: %w", m.filename, err)
65			}
66		}
67		return nil
68	}
69	
70	func loadApplied(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB) (map[int]bool, error) {
71		rows, err := db.QueryContext(ctx, `SELECT version FROM schema_migrations`)
72		if err != nil {
73			return nil, fmt.Errorf("load applied migrations: %w", err)
74		}
75		defer rows.Close()
76		out := map[int]bool{}
77		for rows.Next() {
78			var v int
79			if err := rows.Scan(&v); err != nil {
80				return nil, err
81			}
82			out[v] = true
83		}
84		return out, rows.Err()
85	}
86	
87	func runOne(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB, version int, body string) error {
88		tx, err := db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
89		if err != nil {
90			return err
91		}
92		defer tx.Rollback()
93		if _, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx, body); err != nil {
94			return err
95		}
96		if _, err := tx.ExecContext(ctx, `INSERT INTO schema_migrations(version) VALUES (?)`, version); err != nil {
97			return err
98		}
99		return tx.Commit()
100	}
101	
102	func parseVersion(filename string) (int, error) {
103		// "0001_init.sql" -> 1
104		idx := strings.IndexByte(filename, '_')
105		if idx <= 0 {
106			return 0, fmt.Errorf("migration filename missing version prefix: %s", filename)
107		}
108		v, err := strconv.Atoi(filename[:idx])
109		if err != nil {
110			return 0, fmt.Errorf("parse version in %s: %w", filename, err)
111		}
112		return v, nil
113	}
114
```
</details></tool-use>

13	// mustAdmin inserts an admin-role system user and returns its UserID
14	// (string) so seed.Articles can look it up the same way main.go does.
15	func mustAdmin(t *testing.T, st *store.Store) string {
16		t.Helper()
17		if _, err := st.Users().InsertSystemAccount(
18			context.Background(), "admin", "$2a$12$placeholderplaceholderplaceholderplaceholderplaceholder.",
19			store.RoleAdmin, false,
20		); err != nil {
21			t.Fatalf("seed admin: %v", err)
22		}
23		return "admin"
24	}
25	
26	func TestArticles_SeedsWhenBoardEmpty(t *testing.T) {
27		ctx := context.Background()
28		st := storetest.New(t)
29		if err := st.Boards().SeedDefaults(ctx); err != nil {
30			t.Fatalf("SeedDefaults: %v", err)
31		}
32		admin := mustAdmin(t, st)
33	
34		if err := seed.Articles(ctx, st, admin); err != nil {
35			t.Fatalf("seed.Articles: %v", err)
36		}
37	
38		welcome, err := st.Boards().GetByName(ctx, "Welcome")
39		if err != nil {
40			t.Fatalf("GetByName: %v", err)
41		}
42		got, _ := st.Articles().ListByBoard(ctx, welcome.ID, 10)
43		if len(got) != 1 {
44			t.Fatalf("Welcome article count = %d, want 1", len(got))
45		}
46		if got[0].AuthorUserID != "admin" {
47			t.Errorf("author = %q, want admin", got[0].AuthorUserID)
48		}
49		if !strings.Contains(got[0].Title, "歡迎") {
50			t.Errorf("title = %q, want it to contain 歡迎", got[0].Title)
51		}

53			t.Error("body is empty")
54		}
55	}

12	func newBoardViewFixture(t *testing.T) (boardViewModel, *store.Board, []*store.Article) {
13		t.Helper()
14		st := storetest.New(t)
15		user := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "alice", "")
16		board := storetest.MustBoard(t, st, "Test")
17		var arts []*store.Article
18		for _, title := range []string{"first", "second", "third"} {
19			a, err := st.Articles().Create(context.Background(), board.ID, user.ID, user.UserID, title, "")
20			if err != nil {
21				t.Fatalf("create %s: %v", title, err)
22			}
23			arts = append(arts, a)
24		}
25		deps := Deps{Store: st, User: user, Broker: chat.NewBroker()}
26		return newBoardViewModel(deps, board.ID), board, arts
27	}
28	
29	// `[` and `]` move the cursor up/down the article list; matches j/k aliases.
30	func TestBoardView_BracketCursorAliases(t *testing.T) {
31		m, _, _ := newBoardViewFixture(t)
32		if m.cursor != 0 {
33			t.Fatalf("initial cursor = %d, want 0", m.cursor)
34		}
35		model, _ := m.Update(keyOf("]"))
36		m = model.(boardViewModel)
37		if m.cursor != 1 {
38			t.Errorf("after ]: cursor = %d, want 1", m.cursor)
39		}
40		model, _ = m.Update(keyOf("["))
41		m = model.(boardViewModel)
42		if m.cursor != 0 {
43			t.Errorf("after [: cursor = %d, want 0", m.cursor)
44		}
45	}
46	
47	// `Q` from board view jumps straight to main menu (skipping board list).
48	func TestBoardView_QuitToMenu(t *testing.T) {
49		m, _, _ := newBoardViewFixture(t)
50		_, cmd := m.Update(keyOf("Q"))
51		msg := runCmd(cmd)
52		nav, ok := msg.(NavigateMsg)
53		if !ok {
54			t.Fatalf("got %T, want NavigateMsg", msg)
55		}
56		if nav.To != ScreenMainMenu {
57			t.Errorf("To = %v, want ScreenMainMenu", nav.To)
58		}
59	}
60	
61	// ArticleAddedMsg for THIS board re-fetches the article list from DB.
62	func TestBoardView_AcceptsArticleAddedForThisBoard(t *testing.T) {
63		m, board, arts := newBoardViewFixture(t)
64		if len(m.articles) != 3 {
65			t.Fatalf("constructor loaded %d, want 3", len(m.articles))
66		}
67		// Insert a new article externally, then fire the broadcast.
68		user := m.deps.User
69		a4, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().Create(context.Background(), board.ID, user.ID, user.UserID, "fourth", "")
70		if err != nil {
71			t.Fatalf("create fourth: %v", err)
72		}
73		model, _ := m.Update(ArticleAddedMsg{
74			BoardID:      board.ID,
75			ArticleID:    a4.ID,
76			AuthorUserID: "alice",
77			Title:        "fourth",
78		})
79		got := model.(boardViewModel)
80		if len(got.articles) != 4 {
81			t.Errorf("after ArticleAddedMsg: %d articles, want 4", len(got.articles))
82		}
83		// Newest first — fourth should be at index 0.
84		if got.articles[0].Title != "fourth" {
85			t.Errorf("got[0].Title = %q, want fourth (existing arts: %d)", got.articles[0].Title, len(arts))
86		}
87	}

14	// editFixture seeds a Test board with one article authored by alice and

20		if editor == nil {

22		} else {

26				if editor.Role != "" && editor.Role != store.RoleUser {
27					if err := st.Users().SetRole(t.Context(), u.ID, editor.Role); err != nil {
28						t.Fatalf("SetRole: %v", err)

39		}

101			case "h":
102				msg = tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyRunes, Runes: []rune("h")}
103			case "l":
104				msg = tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyRunes, Runes: []rune("l")}
105			}
106			got, _ := m.Update(msg)
107			if _, ok := got.(articleEditModel); !ok {
108				t.Errorf("key %q: model became %T, want articleEditModel (h/l must not navigate)", key, got)
109			}
110		}
111	}
112	
113	func TestArticleEdit_CtrlSSavesAndBroadcasts(t *testing.T) {
114		st := storetest.New(t)
115		alice := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "alice", "")
116		bob := storetest.MustUser(t, st, "bob", "")
117		board := storetest.MustBoard(t, st, "Test")
118		a, _ := st.Articles().Create(context.Background(), board.ID, alice.ID, alice.UserID, "old", "old body")
119	
120		br := chat.NewBroker()
121		bobRec := &recordingSender{}
122		br.Register(&chat.Session{UserID: bob.ID, UserIDStr: bob.UserID, Program: bobRec})
123	
124		deps := Deps{Store: st, User: alice, Broker: br}
125		m := newArticleEditModel(deps, a.ID)
126		m.title.SetValue("new title")
127		m.body.SetValue("new body content")
128	
129		model, cmd := m.submit()
130		got := model.(articleEditModel)
131		if got.err != "" {
132			t.Fatalf("submit err = %q", got.err)
133		}
134		nav := runCmd(cmd).(NavigateMsg)
135		if nav.To != ScreenArticleView || nav.ArticleID != a.ID {
136			t.Errorf("nav = %+v want article view %d", nav, a.ID)
137		}
138	
139		// DB reflects the update.
140		updated, _ := st.Articles().GetByID(context.Background(), a.ID)
141		if updated.Title != "new title" || updated.Body != "new body content" {
142			t.Errorf("DB title/body = %q/%q", updated.Title, updated.Body)
143		}
144		if !updated.UpdatedAt.Valid {
145			t.Error("UpdatedAt not set")
146		}
147	
148		// Bob (other session) must have got an ArticleUpdatedMsg.
149		if len(bobRec.msgs) != 1 {
150			t.Fatalf("bob got %d msgs, want 1", len(bobRec.msgs))
151		}
152		upd, ok := bobRec.msgs[0].(ArticleUpdatedMsg)
153		if !ok || upd.ArticleID != a.ID {
154			t.Errorf("got %+v, want ArticleUpdatedMsg{ArticleID: %d}", bobRec.msgs[0], a.ID)
155		}
156	}
157	
158	func TestArticleEdit_EscapeReturnsToArticleView(t *testing.T) {

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"strings"
6	
7		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textarea"
8		"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/textinput"
9		tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
10	
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/markdown"
12	)
13	
14	const (
15		composeFocusTitle = iota
16		composeFocusBody
17	)
18	
19	type postComposeModel struct {
20		deps    Deps
21		boardID int64
22		title   textinput.Model
23		body    textarea.Model
24		focus   int
25		width   int
26		height  int
27		err     string
28	}
29	
30	func newPostComposeModel(deps Deps, boardID int64) postComposeModel {
31		ti := textinput.New()
32		ti.Placeholder = "標題 title"
33		ti.CharLimit = 64
34		ti.Width = 60
35		ti.Focus()
36	
37		ta := textarea.New()
38		ta.Placeholder = "內容 body…"
39		ta.CharLimit = 8000
40		ta.SetWidth(72)
41		ta.SetHeight(12)
42	
43		return postComposeModel{
44			deps:    deps,
45			boardID: boardID,
46			title:   ti,
47			body:    ta,
48			focus:   composeFocusTitle,
49		}
50	}
51	
52	func (m postComposeModel) Init() tea.Cmd { return textinput.Blink }
53	
54	func (m postComposeModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
55		switch msg := msg.(type) {
56		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
57			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
58			bodyW := max(40, msg.Width-8)
59			bodyH := max(8, msg.Height-12)
60			m.body.SetWidth(bodyW)
61			m.body.SetHeight(bodyH)
62			m.title.Width = max(20, msg.Width-12)
63			return m, nil
64	
65		case tea.KeyMsg:
66			switch msg.String() {
67			case "esc":
68				return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: m.boardID} }
69			case "tab":
70				m.toggleFocus()
71				return m, nil
72			case "ctrl+s":
73				return m.submit()
74			case "ctrl+i":
75				m.importFromBody()
76				return m, nil
77			}
78		}
79	
80		var cmd tea.Cmd
81		if m.focus == composeFocusTitle {
82			// In title field, Enter advances to body.
83			if k, ok := msg.(tea.KeyMsg); ok && k.String() == "enter" {
84				m.toggleFocus()
85				return m, nil
86			}
87			m.title, cmd = m.title.Update(msg)
88		} else {
89			m.body, cmd = m.body.Update(msg)
90		}
91		return m, cmd
92	}
93	
94	func (m *postComposeModel) toggleFocus() {
95		if m.focus == composeFocusTitle {
96			m.focus = composeFocusBody
97			m.title.Blur()
98			m.body.Focus()
99		} else {
100			m.focus = composeFocusTitle
101			m.body.Blur()
102			m.title.Focus()
103		}
104	}
105	
106	// importFromBody parses the current body as markdown and prefills title +
107	// body from any frontmatter found. If the body has no frontmatter it's
108	// left alone (with a helpful error). Pushes in the parsed input are
109	// silently ignored — see plan: import via Ctrl+I always credits the
110	// current user as author and never restores foreign-authored pushes.
111	func (m *postComposeModel) importFromBody() {
112		raw := m.body.Value()
113		if strings.TrimSpace(raw) == "" {
114			m.err = "貼上 markdown（含 --- frontmatter ---）後再按 Ctrl+I"
115			return
116		}
117		parsed, err := markdown.Parse(raw)
118		if err != nil {
119			m.err = "parse: " + err.Error()
120			return
121		}
122		if parsed.Title == "" && parsed.Body == strings.TrimRight(raw, "\n") {
123			// Parser didn't find frontmatter — body is verbatim. Tell the
124			// user so they don't think Ctrl+I silently no-op'd.
125			m.err = "找不到 frontmatter（請貼上開頭含 --- ... --- 的 markdown）"
126			return
127		}
128		if parsed.Title != "" {
129			m.title.SetValue(parsed.Title)
130		}
131		m.body.SetValue(parsed.Body)
132		m.err = ""
133	}
134	
135	func (m postComposeModel) submit() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
136		title := strings.TrimSpace(m.title.Value())
137		body := m.body.Value()
138		if title == "" {
139			m.err = "title is required"
140			return m, nil
141		}
142		if strings.TrimSpace(body) == "" {
143			m.err = "body is required"
144			return m, nil
145		}
146		ctx := context.Background()
147		u := m.deps.User
148		art, err := m.deps.Store.Articles().Create(ctx, m.boardID, u.ID, u.UserID, title, body)
149		if err != nil {
150			m.err = err.Error()
151			return m, nil
152		}
153		if err := m.deps.Store.Users().IncrementPosts(ctx, u.ID); err != nil {
154			m.err = err.Error()
155			return m, nil
156		}
157		// Broadcast to other live sessions; recipients filter by BoardID and
158		// re-fetch the canonical list (mirrors the PushAddedMsg pattern).
159		if m.deps.Broker != nil {
160			m.deps.Broker.SendToAll(u.ID, ArticleAddedMsg{
161				BoardID:      m.boardID,
162				ArticleID:    art.ID,
163				AuthorUserID: u.UserID,
164				Title:        art.Title,
165			})
166		}
167		return m, func() tea.Msg { return NavigateMsg{To: ScreenBoardView, BoardID: m.boardID} }
168	}
169	
170	func (m postComposeModel) View() string {
171		var b strings.Builder
172		b.WriteString("\n")
173		b.WriteString(StyleHeader.Render(" 發表新文章 New Post "))
174		b.WriteString("\n\n")
175	
176		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Title:") + "\n")
177		b.WriteString("  " + m.title.View() + "\n\n")
178	
179		b.WriteString("  " + StyleDim.Render("Body:") + "\n")
180		b.WriteString("  " + indent(m.body.View(), "  ") + "\n")
181	
182		if m.err != "" {
183			b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleError.Render("⚠ "+m.err) + "\n")
184		}
185		b.WriteString("\n  " + StyleHelp.Render("Tab switch field · Enter (in title) → body · Ctrl+S send · Ctrl+I import markdown · Esc cancel"))
186		b.WriteString("\n")
187		return b.String()
188	}
189	
190	func indent(s, prefix string) string {
191		lines := strings.Split(s, "\n")
192		for i, ln := range lines {
193			if i == 0 {
194				continue
195			}
196			lines[i] = prefix + ln
197		}
198		return strings.Join(lines, "\n")
199	}
200

1	-- Track edits to articles. NULL = never edited; the original CreatedAt is the
2	-- only authoritative timestamp. The article-edit screen and ArticleRepo.Update
3	-- set this to CURRENT_TIMESTAMP on every successful update.
4	--
5	-- Ordering: ListByBoard intentionally still sorts by created_at, so an edited
6	-- article does NOT bubble up to the top — bookmark/next-prev semantics stay
7	-- intact.
8	
9	ALTER TABLE articles ADD COLUMN updated_at DATETIME;
10

```text
1	-- Initial schema for the SSH BBS.
2	-- All tables use auto-increment INTEGER PRIMARY KEY plus a unique business
3	-- key where appropriate. We deliberately do NOT mimic pttbbs's recno-based
4	-- addressing (a holdover from mmap'd binary .DIR files) — a relational DB
5	-- removes the recno-tombstone fragility documented in pttbbs.
6	
7	PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;
8	
9	CREATE TABLE users (
10	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
11	    user_id         TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE COLLATE NOCASE, -- userec_t.userid
12	    password_hash   TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- bcrypt; replaces userec_t.passwd (DES)
13	    nickname        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- userec_t.nickname
14	    realname        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- userec_t.realname
15	    email           TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- userec_t.email
16	    num_logins      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- userec_t.numlogindays (loose)
17	    num_posts       INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- userec_t.numposts
18	    last_login_at   DATETIME,                            -- userec_t.lastlogin
19	    last_host       TEXT,                                -- userec_t.lasthost
20	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
21	);
22	
23	CREATE TABLE boards (
24	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
25	    name            TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE COLLATE NOCASE, -- boardheader_t.brdname
26	    title           TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- boardheader_t.title
27	    description     TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
28	    bm              TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',            -- boardheader_t.BM (comma-sep)
29	    attr            INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- boardheader_t.brdattr (P1+)
30	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
31	);
32	
33	CREATE TABLE articles (
34	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
35	    board_id        INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES boards(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
36	    author_id       INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
37	    author_userid   TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- denormalized (fileheader_t.owner)
38	    title           TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- fileheader_t.title
39	    body            TEXT NOT NULL,
40	    recommend_score INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- cached sum of pushes (+1) - boos (-1)
41	    filemode        INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,          -- fileheader_t.filemode (P1+)
42	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
43	);
44	CREATE INDEX idx_articles_board_created ON articles(board_id, created_at DESC);
45	
46	CREATE TABLE pushes (
47	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
48	    article_id      INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES articles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
49	    user_id         INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
50	    user_userid     TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- denormalized
51	    kind            TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (kind IN ('push','boo','arrow')), -- 推/噓/→
52	    body            TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
53	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
54	);
55	CREATE INDEX idx_pushes_article ON pushes(article_id, id);
56	
57	CREATE TABLE water_balloons (
58	    id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
59	    from_user_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
60	    from_userid     TEXT NOT NULL,                       -- denormalized
61	    to_user_id      INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
62	    body            TEXT NOT NULL,
63	    delivered_live  INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
64	    read_at         DATETIME,
65	    created_at      DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
66	);
67	CREATE INDEX idx_wb_to_unread ON water_balloons(to_user_id, read_at);
68

```text
1	// Package storetest provides shared helpers for tests that need a fresh
2	// SQLite store. It lives in its own package so any internal package can
3	// import it without creating cycles.
4	package storetest
5	
6	import (
7		"context"
8		"path/filepath"
9		"testing"
10	
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/sshbbs/internal/store"
12	)
13	
14	// New opens a fresh on-disk SQLite store in t.TempDir(), runs migrations,
15	// and registers a Cleanup to close it. Use it whenever you'd write
16	// `store.Open` in a test — never share stores across tests.
17	func New(tb testing.TB) *store.Store {
18		tb.Helper()
19		dir := tb.TempDir()
20		st, err := store.Open(filepath.Join(dir, "test.db"))
21		if err != nil {
22			tb.Fatalf("store.Open: %v", err)
23		}
24		tb.Cleanup(func() { _ = st.Close() })
25		return st
26	}
27	
28	// MustUser inserts a user with a placeholder password hash and returns it.
29	// Convenient for tests that need a logged-in identity but don't care about auth.
30	func MustUser(tb testing.TB, st *store.Store, userID, nickname string) *store.User {
31		tb.Helper()
32		u, err := st.Users().Create(context.Background(), userID, "$2a$12$placeholderplaceholderplaceholderplaceholderplaceholder.", nickname, "")
33		if err != nil {
34			tb.Fatalf("create user %q: %v", userID, err)
35		}
36		return u
37	}
38	
39	// MustBoard returns a seeded board by name, failing the test if not found.
40	// Useful with the default Welcome / Test / ChitChat boards which
41	// SeedDefaults / migration 0002 create automatically.
42	func MustBoard(tb testing.TB, st *store.Store, name string) *store.Board {
43		tb.Helper()
44		b, err := st.Boards().GetByName(context.Background(), name)
45		if err != nil {
46			tb.Fatalf("get board %q: %v", name, err)
47		}
48		return b
49	}
50
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/seed/articles/welcome.md`

```text
1	---
2	title: 歡迎來到 SSH-BBS
3	board: Welcome
4	author: admin
5	---
6	
7	哈囉，歡迎來到這個簡化版的 PTT 風格 BBS。整個站台就是一個 SSH server：
8	你看到的所有東西都跑在 wish + bubbletea 上面，後端用 SQLite 存資料。
9	
10	# 這裡能做什麼
11	
12	- **看板**：預設有 `Welcome`（這裡）、`Test`、`ChitChat`。
13	- **發文 / 讀文**：UTF-8 全程支援，CJK 寬度自動處理。
14	- **推文**：在文章畫面按 `+` 推、`-` 噓、`=` 留 →。其他線上看同篇的人會即時看到。
15	- **水球**：私訊一行話。`Ctrl+U` 開水球收件匣，離線收到的也會在你重連時補播。
16	- **站內信 (mail)**：水球留不下，重要的話寫信。`Ctrl+U` 進收件匣後切到郵件分頁。
17	
18	# 鍵盤總覽
19	
20	```
21	↑/↓ 或 j/k     上下移動
22	←/→ 或 h/l     上下層導覽（表單例外，h/l 留給文字編輯）
23	Enter / Space  進入 / 翻頁
24	Esc / Backspace 返回
25	g / G          捲到頂 / 底
26	[ / ]          上一篇 / 下一篇
27	p              在看板裡發新文
28	+ / - / =      推 / 噓 / →
29	y              匯出此篇為 markdown
30	E              編輯此篇（作者本人或 mod 以上）
31	D              刪除此篇 / 推文（作者本人或 mod 以上）
32	Ctrl+U         水球 / 站內信
33	Ctrl+C         斷線離開
34	```
35	
36	# 權限
37	
38	- **guest**：唯讀，只看不寫。SSH 用 `guest` 帳號連進來不需密碼。
39	- **user**：一般註冊帳號。`ssh new@host -p 2222` 註冊，密碼欄位會被忽略，用 TUI 內表單填。
40	- **mod**：可以刪 / 編輯任何人的文與推文。
41	- **admin**：站長，可以調整他人權限。
42	
43	# 文章是 markdown
44	
45	每篇文章都能用 markdown 表達。在文章畫面按 `y` 會跳到匯出檢視，
46	你可以選 (1) 純內文 (2) 含留言 (3) 寫到伺服器端檔案 (`data/exports/<你的帳號>/`)，
47	或按 `c` 推到終端機剪貼簿（OSC 52，需終端機支援）。
48	
49	寫文也可以走 markdown 路：在發文畫面按 `Ctrl+I`，貼上含 frontmatter 的 markdown，
50	標題會自動從 `title:` 帶進來。
51	
52	# 這篇文是怎麼長出來的
53	
54	這篇 welcome 不是寫死在程式碼或 DB migration 裡。它是 `internal/seed/articles/welcome.md`，
55	用 `go:embed` 包進 binary，server 第一次發現 `Welcome` 看板是空的就把它寫進 DB。
56	之後 admin 改過一次，下次重啟也不會被覆蓋（種子的冪等規則：看板有任何文就跳過）。
57	
58	要新增種子文章，丟一個新的 `*.md` 進那個資料夾，frontmatter 寫 `board: 看板名` 就好。
59	
60	# 想了解更多
61	
62	- 專案 README：repo 根目錄 `README.md`
63	- pttbbs 對照筆記：`docs/ptt_trace_code/*`
64	- 測試策略：`docs/testing.md`
65	- 待辦清單：`TODO.md`（含 P1～P3 + 工作量估計）
66
```
</details></tool-use>
